ideas.concept: Modular medium-voltage DC (1-35kV) hybrid solid-state circuit breaker productized for datacenter campuses, microgrids, and MVDC collection
named_buyer_examples: hyperscale campus power teams (Microsoft/Google archetype)
GE Vernova electrification projects
ARPA-E DC-GRIDS project teams
Pattern Energy-class transmission developers
ideas.painful_job: DC fault interruption is the documented showstopper for MVDC: no converged breaker topology, no product category, forcing point-to-point architectures and over-engineered protection while datacenter DC distribution scales.
ideas.product: A standardized, modular hybrid SSCB (mechanical ultra-fast disconnect + SiC interruption branch + energy absorber) in 1-35kV/1-4kA frames with coordinated non-unit protection relaying, sold as engineered-but-catalogued units rather than per-project HVDC engineering.
ideas.frontier_vision: Enable meshed MVDC campuses and DC industrial parks; expand to MVDC collection for storage/generation and eventually HVDC tap protection.
ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: comparative topology study and scaled (kV-class) interruption prototypes in a university high-power lab; track ARPA-E DC-GRIDS cohort results (L02-034). 2027: SiC interruption-duty die-characterization study (di/dt, avalanche energy, series sharing vs press-pack IGBT baselines) and fail-short press-pack qualification concept - device-strategy workstream imported from wave-F seed F-08 (2026-07-13); cheap, publishable, and feeds the module design regardless of breaker-market timing (L08-001/003/004/017). 2028: 10kV single-module interruption demo at a national-lab test facility (ORNL SSCB lineage, L08-051); IP on module scaling and relay coordination. 2029: co-develop requirements with one hyperscaler campus power team and one DC-GRIDS performer; secure test-bed MOU.
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: MVDC datacenter/industrial distribution becomes economic ahead of protection availability; 2030 is when first US MVDC campus pilots need catalogued breakers, before ABB/Hitachi commit to a productized mid-voltage line.
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: US MVDC campus distribution pilots and DC-GRIDS-seeded utility demonstrations reaching procurement 2030-2033 (ARPA-E DC-GRIDS 2026 awards, L02-034; datacenter electrification order growth, L08-033; Southern Spirit-class HVDC construction from 2029, L08-041).
ideas.competition_outlook_2030: Hitachi Energy hybrid breaker heritage (L08-052), Siemens/GE per-project offerings, 1-2 ARPA-E-spun startups; academic topologies still non-converged (L08-001/003/004), so productization discipline is the differentiator.
ideas.timing_window_risk: MVDC adoption could stay point-to-point (breaker-avoiding) longer than expected; per-project incumbents may bundle protection into converter stations.
ideas.extreme_edge: Millisecond-to-microsecond staged interruption with no natural current zero at tens of kV - the hardest unsolved switching problem in power delivery.
ideas.first_experiment: 2028: 10kV/2kA hybrid module interrupting a simulated pole-to-pole fault in <2 ms with >=100 operations without refurbishment, at a national-lab or university high-power facility.
ideas.founder_fit_note: Founder circuits, controls, and embedded-systems skills transfer to the breaker's sensing, relaying, and trigger electronics, but medium-voltage kA-class power hardware, the mechanical ultra-fast disconnect, and certification programs lie outside the profile.
ideas.merge_import_notes: 2026-07-13 R2: imported P3R2-F-08's (wave-F REJECT) device-strategy workstream - 2027 SiC interruption-duty die-characterization study (di/dt, avalanche energy, series sharing vs press-pack IGBT baselines) and fail-short press-pack qualification concept; cheap, publishable, useful regardless of breaker-market timing. No new source IDs: L08-001/003/004/017 already cited and eligible; F-08's L08-016 is not accepted in the ledger and was NOT imported.
ideas.adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (PROMOTE): Canonical MVDC hybrid breaker. Documented showstopper niche; MVDC adoption pace and $1.5M pre-company experiment are the honest risks; lean on DC-GRIDS/ORNL facilities.

ideas.concept: Containerized MW-class grid-emulation test system for NERC PRC-029-1 / ride-through verification of inverter-based resources at plant sites
named_buyer_examples: US battery-storage and solar owner-operators subject to PRC-029-1
GE Vernova and other inverter OEMs
compliance test houses
ideas.painful_job: NERC PRC-029-1 (FERC Order 909, July 2025) imposes ride-through requirements on the US IBR fleet; owners and OEMs lack field-deployable MW-scale hardware to verify plant-level ride-through, and grid-forming claims are contested in procurement (NESO awarded zero GFM contracts).
ideas.product: A trailerized 1-5 MW back-to-back converter grid emulator (voltage/frequency/phase-jump event playback, weak-grid emulation) with automated PRC-029/IEEE-2800-style test sequencing and evidence-grade reporting.
ideas.frontier_vision: The standard verification instrument for a re-inverterized grid: ride-through, grid-forming capability attestation, and interconnection model validation as a hardware-anchored service network.
ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: track PRC-029 compliance guidance and audit findings; design event-playback converter control in an academic/PHIL lab; interview 20+ owner-operators and 5 OEM compliance teams. 2028: 250kW prototype emulator validated against lab PHIL benchmarks. 2029: MW-unit design freeze, LOIs from two owner-operators facing re-verification. FIX gate (2027): obtain written regional-entity compliance guidance or two owner-operator LOIs evidencing that PRC-029 verification will require/pay for field MW hardware rather than EMT-model submissions; if model-based compliance suffices, kill the standalone thesis and fold the test-sequencing/reporting stack into P3R2-E-14's qualification platform.
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: Enforcement and event-triggered re-verification peak in the early 2030s as the standard beds in and the IBR fleet grows; incumbent test-equipment firms sell lab simulators, not field MW hardware.
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: NERC PRC-029-1 compliance/enforcement phase-in and post-disturbance re-verification wave across the US IBR fleet, 2030-2034 (L08-043). CONDITION (unproven): the standard may be satisfiable with EMT-model evidence alone; field MW-hardware demand must be affirmatively documented (regional-entity guidance or owner LOIs) by 2027 or the seed fails. GFM-attestation leg dropped: no US operator GFM procurement spec exists in the atlas and the closest procurement evidence is negative (NESO awarded zero GFM contracts); AEMO precedent (L08-048) retained as watch-item only, not as demand.
ideas.competition_outlook_2030: NREL/Sandia lab facilities (non-commercial), PHIL vendors (OPAL-RT/Typhoon class) at lab scale, engineering consultancies with models only; syncon vendors (L08-036/037) as substitute stability providers rather than verifiers. Positioning per adjudication: field-hardware complement to P3R2-E-14's relay/HIL qualification platform, not a competitor to it.
ideas.timing_window_risk: If NERC accepts model-based (EMT simulation) compliance evidence broadly, field hardware demand shrinks to OEM labs; regulatory softening is possible.
ideas.extreme_edge: Playing back real grid disturbance records at MW power into a live plant - hardware-in-the-loop at fleet scale.
ideas.first_experiment: 2028: 250kW grid-emulator prototype reproducing a recorded multi-phase-jump disturbance within 2% envelope error against a PHIL reference, with automated PRC-029-format reporting.
ideas.founder_fit_note: Automated test sequencing, data acquisition, and controls experience transfer to the event-playback and evidence-grade reporting layer, while MW-class back-to-back converter design and trailerized power integration are outside the profile.
ideas.fix_applied_notes: 2026-07-13 FIX applied: demand mechanism hedged (field-MW-hardware requirement unproven vs EMT-model compliance; 2027 documentation gate added); GFM-attestation leg dropped on the NESO negative finding (AEMO kept as watch-item only); repositioned as the field-hardware complement to E-14 with a fold-in path if the gate fails.
ideas.adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (FIX): Real instrument gap; demand mechanism unproven if model-based compliance suffices.

ideas.concept: US merchant NEG-coating and low-outgassing surface engineering line for UHV/XHV chambers (accelerators, quantum, EUV, space simulation)
named_buyer_examples: DOE accelerator labs (SLAC/ANL/BNL archetype)
US-ITER/ORNL
quantum hardware chamber builders
NASA space-simulation facilities
ideas.painful_job: NEG coating - the pumping architecture accelerators specify - is effectively single-sourced globally (SAES), and hydrogen-outgassing control still requires 30-hour 250C bakeouts baked into every chamber schedule; US labs and OEMs carry single-point-of-supply and schedule risk.
ideas.product: A domestic merchant coating line: NEG (TiZrV-class) sputter coating of beam pipes and chambers, vacuum-firing/low-H treatment, and certified outgassing metrology, sold per-part with quality data - a second source with US supply-chain preference.
ideas.frontier_vision: Own engineered vacuum surfaces: from accelerator beam pipes to XHV quantum and EUV chambers, then integrated getter-pump products.
ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: replicate published NEG-coating recipes on a university sputter rig; validate pumping speed/activation temperature against literature; survey US lab procurement specs. 2028: coat and qualify pilot beam-pipe sections for one DOE lab under a cooperative agreement; certify outgassing test bench to lab standards. 2029: production-line design, LOIs from two labs and one chamber OEM.
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: US accelerator upgrade projects and quantum/EUV chamber demand rise into the 2030s while the sole incumbent remains foreign and capacity-limited; domestic-sourcing preferences strengthen for DOE-funded procurement.
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: DOE accelerator-upgrade and QIS-facility construction procurements 2030-2034 (DOE $625M QIS centers, L13-028) plus continuing ITER-class vacuum tenders (L07-041/042/043/044) and NASA facility sustainment (L07-035).
ideas.competition_outlook_2030: SAES Getters (sole merchant incumbent, L07-037), in-house coating at CERN-affiliated labs, general vacuum houses without NEG physics; ULVAC-class majors could enter if the niche grows.
ideas.timing_window_risk: Niche size may stay small if accelerator budgets stall; SAES could add US capacity and close the sovereignty gap.
ideas.extreme_edge: Reducing hydrogen outgassing by >10,000x and replacing lumped pumps with the chamber wall itself as the pump.
ideas.first_experiment: 2027: deposit TiZrV NEG on 1-m test pipes, demonstrate activation at <=200C and sticking coefficients within 20% of published SAES-class performance, verified by an independent lab outgassing bench.
ideas.founder_fit_note: Low direct fit: certified outgassing metrology draws on instrumentation and data-acquisition skills, but the core sputter-coating, vacuum-firing, and surface-chemistry process line is outside the founder's electrical-engineering profile.
ideas.merge_import_notes: 2026-07-13: absorbed B-12 (CN NEG line). No content imported: the license-into-China model was rejected by the adjudicator as IP leakage in a process-know-how business with CEPC-dependent lumpy demand; A-05 remains US-primary with no CN chapter.
ideas.adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (PROMOTE): Single-source (SAES) supply gap + US sovereignty preference; process know-how moat; modest vision ceiling accepted; SAES adding US capacity is the kill risk.

ideas.concept: Vendor-neutral ion-energy-distribution metrology + tailored-waveform bias control retrofit for plasma etch chambers
named_buyer_examples: US leading-edge fabs (Intel/Micron archetype, CHIPS-funded expansions)
etch tool OEMs (Lam/Applied archetype)
Advanced Energy as partner/acquirer
AMEC (CN, via partitioned >=28nm entity, from B-05)
NAURA / Piotech (CN, via partitioned >=28nm entity, from B-05)
ideas.painful_job: Tailored-voltage-waveform biasing for atomic-scale etch is an unsettled 2024-26 research frontier; fabs and OEMs lack independent ion-energy-distribution measurement and closed-loop waveform control - they tune blind against drifting sheaths at sub-2nm nodes.
ideas.product: A chamber-mounted IEDF sensor (RF-immune retarding analyzer / derived-signal estimator) plus a waveform-synthesis bias controller that closes the loop on ion energy rather than on electrical setpoints; retrofits onto existing generators. Merged extensions: (1) company structure = two-entity export partition (from C-06) - the US entity serves leading-edge OCP/fab customers, a separate CN-serving line is limited to mature-node (>=28nm) applications; (2) CN product chapter (from B-05): waveform-bias RF delivery for AMEC/NAURA/Piotech-class OEM localization programs, mature-node scope only; (3) arc/ESC-discharge event-detection and forensics layer (from B-21), contingent on FTO analysis - patent-dense field.
ideas.frontier_vision: Ion-energy-defined processing as the control standard across etch, deposition, and emerging plasma applications - the measurement layer incumbent generator vendors do not own.
ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: build IEDF estimation methods on a university ICP/CCP reactor; benchmark against gridded analyzers; publish and patent sensor+control coupling. 2028: alpha retrofit on an OEM lab chamber under JDA; quantify etch-profile improvement on test wafers. 2029: SEMI-standards engagement and pilot with one US fab process-development group. Merged gates: export-control counsel review of the two-entity partition is a 2027 gate (before any CN-facing work); FTO analysis precedes any investment in the B-21 event-detection layer. KR validation channel (from E-03): Samsung TVW co-authorship pull (L06-009) and the Korean plasma-diagnostics base (L06-024/025) as reference-user and validation channel.
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: Waveform-capable generators (AE Everest class) proliferate 2026-29, creating the installed base that then needs independent metrology and control by 2030; generator vendors sell sources, not vendor-neutral measurement.
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: US: sub-2nm/backside-power etch capacity ramps at CHIPS-funded US fabs 2030-2033 and second-generation waveform-bias platform adoption (Advanced Energy new-platform ramp evidence, L06-039). CN (independent leg, merged from B-05/C-06; mature-node scope): AMEC/NAURA/Piotech supplier-localization programs under the 15th FYP - AMEC etch revenue RMB7.28bn +54.7% (L06-048), the Hengyunchang/Injet domestic-substitution wave (L06-042/043/044), and the <12% RF/precision-component localization gap (L06-054) those OEMs are documented to be closing; served only through the partitioned >=28nm entity, subject to the 2027 export-counsel gate.
ideas.competition_outlook_2030: Advanced Energy/MKS/Comet embedding partial capability in generators (L06-039, L06-049); Impedans-class plasma diagnostics; tool-OEM internal sensors. Neutral closed-loop IEDF control remains open.
ideas.timing_window_risk: OEMs may lock chamber data behind proprietary interfaces; if generator vendors integrate good-enough IEDF inference by 2030 the retrofit market narrows to process-development labs.
ideas.extreme_edge: Controlling ion energy distributions to electron-volt precision through an RF sheath at 100+ MHz superposition - measurement where physics fights the measurement.
ideas.first_experiment: 2027-2028: closed-loop IEDF control demo on a lab reactor holding a programmed bimodal ion-energy peak within +/-2 eV across intentional pressure/chemistry drift; correlate to etch-profile metrics.
ideas.founder_fit_note: Strong transfer: the RF-immune ion-energy sensor, signal-derived estimation, and closed-loop waveform bias controller map directly onto founder sensors/instrumentation, circuits, controls, and electromagnetics skills.
ideas.merge_import_notes: 2026-07-13: absorbed C-06 (US/CN two-entity export partition as company structure), B-05 (CN mature-node RF-delivery chapter with localization evidence), E-03 (Samsung co-authorship pull + KR validation channel), B-21 (event-detection/forensics layer, FTO-gated). primary_market US->US+CN and china_beachhead->true because the CN demand leg is independent and cited to eligible CN evidence (L06-042/043/044/048/054); honesty conditions documented - mature-node (>=28nm) scope only, 2027 export-control counsel gate, leading-edge line stays in the US entity.
ideas.adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (PROMOTE): Canonical TVW/IEDF control. Merge in C-06 dual-entity export partition, E-03 KR validation channel, B-21 event-detection layer. Export-control counsel is a 2027 gate.

ideas.concept: Sub-100ms no-overshoot gas delivery: pressure-insensitive fast MFC with inline transient metrology for ALD/ALE throughput
named_buyer_examples: etch/deposition tool OEMs (Lam/Applied archetype via gas-panel integrators)
Ichor/UCT-class gas-panel builders
US fabs running throughput-limited ALD/ALE
ideas.painful_job: SEMI E17 codifies transient response as the constraint; thermal MFCs still overshoot on fast steps, and ALD/ALE cycle counts keep rising - gas switching time is now direct throughput money on $100M+ tools fleets.
ideas.product: A fast-actuation MFC (pressure-based sensing + model-predictive valve control) hitting sub-100 ms settled steps without overshoot, plus an inline transient-verification module that gives OEMs SEMI E17-style evidence per channel.
ideas.frontier_vision: The gas-delivery timing layer for atomic-layer processing everywhere - semiconductors first, then precision CVD industries.
ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: control-theory and sensor work on a lab flow bench (building on estimated-output-feedback literature); prototype actuator/sensing stack. 2028: third-party SEMI E17-protocol characterization vs incumbent MFCs; JDA discussions with one gas-panel integrator. 2029: alpha units on an OEM development tool; reliability data. FIX additions: 2026-27 includes a quantified $/tool/year throughput-value model vs OEM qualification switching costs, and broadening of the technical base beyond L06-026/L06-034 (additional peer-reviewed transient-flow sources to be added in P4). Hard 2028 gate: a paid evaluation or LOI from one gas-panel integrator/OEM evidencing dual-sourcing appetite on transient specs; absent it, shelve the seed.
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: ALD/ALE step counts and gas-panel channel counts keep climbing through 2030; incumbents optimize steady-state accuracy and are slow to re-architect sensing; a demonstrable throughput delta converts to design-ins on next tool generations 2030-2033.
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: Next-generation US fab tool builds 2030-2033 (CHIPS-funded capacity, Edwards subfab precedent L06-033) and an expected SEMI E17 revision cycle tightening transient specs (standard vintage evidence, L06-035).
ideas.competition_outlook_2030: Horiba/Brooks/MKS/Fujikin incumbent MFC lines (market structure via L07-049 gas-panel concentration); pressure-based fast MFCs may partially close the gap - differentiation must be verified transient evidence.
ideas.timing_window_risk: Incumbent catch-up is the main risk; OEM qualification cycles are long and single-sourced relationships sticky. Adjudication: incumbent (Horiba/Brooks/MKS) catch-up could close the window before 2030; the 2028 dual-sourcing gate is the go/no-go.
ideas.extreme_edge: Metering reactive gases to 1% in tens of milliseconds against varying upstream pressure - flow control at chemical-reaction timescales.
ideas.first_experiment: 2027: bench demonstration of 0-90% flow steps settling <100 ms with zero overshoot across 2x upstream-pressure variation, measured by an independent rate-of-rise standard.
ideas.founder_fit_note: Strong transfer: pressure-based sensing, model-predictive valve control, and inline transient metrology sit squarely in founder instrumentation, controls, and data-acquisition skills, with only the fluidic/valve mechanics outside the profile.
ideas.fix_applied_notes: 2026-07-13 FIX applied: 2028 paid-eval/LOI dual-sourcing gate added; $/tool/year throughput-value-vs-switching-cost quantification added; P4 task to broaden the technical base beyond L06-026/034.
ideas.adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (FIX): Incumbent (Horiba/Brooks/MKS) catch-up could close window pre-2030.

ideas.concept: Modular rad-tolerant GaN power processing units (2-20kW class) with standardized interfaces for electric propulsion and spacecraft power
named_buyer_examples: NASA GRC programs (AEPS/NEXT-C lineage)
Maxar-class primes
SDA constellation primes (Lockheed, Northrop, Rocket Lab)
Busek as thruster partner
ideas.painful_job: PPU topology never converged after 50 years; the flagship US supplier now sits inside a missile-focused conglomerate; every program pays fresh NRE, and small/mid satellites are priced out of EP by PPU cost and mass - while GaN/SiC space qualification remains unfinished.
ideas.product: A qualified modular PPU family: rad-tolerant GaN power stages, standardized anode/keeper/mag supplies, common telemetry and (AIAA S-122 / ECSS-E-ST-20-20C-aligned) interfaces, scaling 2-20kW by module count with a documented radiation test dossier. Merged extensions: SEE-map-first sequencing - publish device SEE/TID maps before module qualification - and a 'qualified-once, reused-everywhere' qualification file reused across thruster classes (from D-15); ECSS-E-ST-20-20C / AIAA S-122 qualification framing hardened as the interface between merchant modules and primes (from E-10).
ideas.frontier_vision: The merchant power-electronics layer for the electric spacecraft: EP drive, high-voltage buses, and eventually nuclear-surface-power conversion electronics.
ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: publish GaN converter SEE/TID characterization (heavy-ion campaigns via university facilities, building on L09-103/104 methods); breadboard 5kW discharge supply. 2028: SBIR-track qualification unit; thermal-vac + radiation test of the standard module; interface spec whitepaper circulated to primes. 2029: EM unit integrated with a partner thruster (Busek-class) in a vacuum-facility hot-fire test.
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: Proliferated constellations and NASA sustainment demand rise into the 2030s while Aerojet/L3Harris prioritizes missiles; a modern GaN modular line at half the mass/cost has no US merchant incumbent.
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: NASA Gateway/Mars-logistics EP sustainment and SDA Tranche 4/5-class proliferated procurement 2030-2034 (AEPS $67M and NEXT-C $18.4M precedents L09-034/L09-012; PPE supply chain L09-043; SDA T3 $3.5B scale signal L09-039 - EP content flagged unconfirmed). Additional trigger (from D-15): Space Force Maneuverable-GEO-class programs (L09-037). SDA EP content remains flagged unconfirmed - P4 must close it.
ideas.competition_outlook_2030: L3Harris/Aerojet (L09-041), Busek/Moog in-house, EPC Space at component level (L09-054/055), JAXA/Furukawa line in Japan (L09-042); Rocket Lab vertical integration (L09-114).
ideas.timing_window_risk: Primes may vertically integrate PPUs; GaN SEE derating could erode the mass advantage; NASA budget volatility.
ideas.extreme_edge: kW-class GaN switching that survives heavy-ion strikes at full bus voltage - power density where derating rules were written for silicon.
ideas.first_experiment: 2027-2028: 5kW GaN discharge-supply breadboard passing heavy-ion testing at full operating voltage (no destructive SEE at mission-representative LET) plus efficiency >96%; results published as the qualification baseline.
secondary_markets: JP: JAXA-Furukawa J-SPARC GaN Hall-thruster power-supply commercialization as validation-pattern partner/licensee (L09-042) [from E-10]
KR: KARI full-electric-propulsion GEO design studies as an emergent 2030s buyer (L09-019) [from E-10]
ideas.founder_fit_note: Power-stage circuit design, standardized telemetry, and control interfaces draw on founder circuits and embedded-systems skills, but radiation-effects qualification and space-flight hardware processes are outside the profile.
ideas.merge_import_notes: 2026-07-13: absorbed E-10 (ECSS/AIAA qualification framing, JAXA-Furukawa J-SPARC partner pattern, KARI emergent-buyer note) and D-15 (SEE-map-first sequencing, qualified-once qualification-file framing, Space Force Maneuverable-GEO trigger L09-037). Prime vertical integration remains the standing risk.
ideas.adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (PROMOTE): Canonical GaN PPU. SDA EP content unconfirmed - P4 must close. Merge in E-10 ECSS/AIAA framing + JAXA-Furukawa pattern; D-15 SEE-map-first sequencing. Prime vertical integration is standing risk.

ideas.concept: 300C-class mixed-signal instrumentation electronics platform (SiC/SOI) for superhot geothermal, downhole, and turbine environments
named_buyer_examples: Baker Hughes
SLB
Ormat/EGS pilots
FORGE awardees (Welltec, PetroQuip)
DARPA/ARPA-E performers
ideas.painful_job: Commercial high-temp ICs top out at 225C and the two incumbent lines are obsolete/exiting (CISSOID CHT last-time-buy; Honeywell legacy), while funded US programs now target 375C+ reservoirs and 800C ICs - existing buyers face a live sourcing gap between what they can buy and where they must operate.
ideas.product: A qualified 300C mixed-signal instrumentation platform: SiC/SOI front-ends (ADC, mux, drivers), high-temp passives/packaging stack, and a reference logging/telemetry module - sold as chipset + qualified module to downhole/geothermal tool OEMs. Merged extensions: packaging-first qualification strategy - advanced die-attach/ceramic packaging is the documented true bottleneck (L15-005, L15-025) and leads the roadmap (from E-09); a 400C SiC-JFET instrumentation tier (NASA-heritage JFET front ends, partitioned hot-front-end + insulated-DSP architecture) as the roadmap step above 300C (from D-14).
ideas.frontier_vision: Native electronics for hot places: superhot geothermal, aero-engine and hypersonics instrumentation, Venus-class space missions, and close-coupled power-device gate drive.
ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: 300C building-block circuits via university SiC/SOI runs (Arkansas/NASA-Glenn lineage methods); packaging/die-attach thermal-cycling study. 2028: 1,000-hour 300C soak of a mixed-signal front-end; present at FORGE/SUPERHOT community events; discovery with 10+ tool OEM engineering leads. 2029: field-representative logging-module prototype survives 500 hours in a heated test well/autoclave; LOI from one service company. Added roadmap steps: the 1,000h powered-soak artifact is the flagship qualification datum (from E-09); a FORGE-well field-trial step for the logging module follows in 2029 (from D-14). B-17's China chapter NOT imported: the atlas contains no named Chinese HT-electronics procurement (self-flagged inferential), so CN stays out of the demand base; revisit only if named evidence appears.
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: SUPERHOT/FORGE-funded drilling reaches wells hotter than any purchasable electronics by ~2030; CISSOID/Honeywell exit leaves buyers without a roadmap exactly as EGS scales on datacenter power demand.
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: Superhot-rock EGS drilling campaigns 2030-2034 seeded by ARPA-E SUPERHOT (awards from Nov 2025, L15-029) and DOE FORGE cohorts (L15-027/028), plus Baker Hughes-scale geothermal orders (L15-040) and SLB-Ormat commercial EGS expansion (L15-041); DARPA THERMAL Phase II completion ~2029 derisks 500C+ follow-on (L15-026).
ideas.competition_outlook_2030: TI HT-line silicon (175-210C), CISSOID remnants (EU), in-house electronics at SLB/Halliburton (200C class), Ozark IC-class niche design houses; nobody selling a qualified 300C platform.
ideas.timing_window_risk: If superhot drilling slips, the 300C market stays program-funded; oilfield in-house teams may capture the niche for their own tools only.
ideas.extreme_edge: Mixed-signal circuits living for months at 300C where every commercial IC datasheet ends - the temperature frontier of electronics as a product.
ideas.first_experiment: 2028: 300C/1,000-hour continuous operation of an 8-channel SiC/SOI acquisition front-end (ADC + mux + reference) with <1% drift, independently witnessed; thermal-cycling of the packaging stack to 500 cycles.
secondary_markets: JP: Fukushima-decommissioning robotics ecosystem as a qualified-electronics side market (L15-011) [from E-09]
ideas.founder_fit_note: Strong transfer on the mixed-signal instrumentation front-ends, data-acquisition chain, and reference logging module, which are core founder skills; the high-temperature packaging and die-attach materials work that gates the product is outside the profile.
ideas.merge_import_notes: 2026-07-13: absorbed E-09 (packaging-first strategy, 1,000h powered-soak artifact, JP decommissioning side market), D-14 (400C SiC-JFET tier, FORGE field-trial step), B-17 (rejected CN chapter - kept out of demand base for lack of named CN procurement).
ideas.adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (PROMOTE): Canonical 300C platform. Best demand-timing story in family (SUPERHOT/FORGE vs CISSOID/Honeywell exit). Merge in E-09 packaging-first strategy + JP side market; D-14 400C tier.

ideas.concept: Chiplet-era thermal interface: manufacturable liquid-metal/sintered TIM system with per-package reliability engineering for 1,000W+ AI packages
named_buyer_examples: US advanced packagers (Intel Foundry/Amkor-US archetype)
AI accelerator vendors (NVIDIA ecosystem)
defense electronics primes (Raytheon-class, COOLERCHIPS lineage)
ideas.painful_job: 2.5D/3D chiplet packages create hundreds-of-watts hotspots that existing TIM1/TIM1.5 materials cannot serve - the tradeoff among bulk conductivity, bond-line resistance, and thermo-mechanical reliability is a documented, unresolved first-order constraint on AI package design.
ideas.product: A TIM system, not a paste and not a service: a productized SKU family (liquid-metal/sintered-metal composites with engineered barriers/wetting layers, in defined viscosity/bond-line grades) plus a licensed dispensing+containment process package (equipment recipe, containment design rules, QA spec) that packagers run on their own lines; accelerated-reliability data (power cycling, pump-out, corrosion) is the sales artifact. Per-package engineering is limited to paid qualification programs that feed the standard SKU grades - not open-ended engineering services.
ideas.frontier_vision: Own the die-to-cold-plate interface across AI, power electronics, and RF - the last 50 microns that set junction temperature.
ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: university materials program on barrier metallurgy and pump-out physics; build power-cycling reliability rig. 2028: demonstrate <5 mm2K/W effective resistance with 1,000-cycle stability on test vehicles; engage one packager's advanced-development group. 2029: pilot line process definition with a dispensing-equipment partner; qualification plan agreed with an anchor customer. FIX gate (2028): anchor-packager LOI on the SKU + process-license model. Kill gate: the head-to-head reliability delta vs two named incumbent metal TIMs on identical test vehicles - no statistically significant advantage means kill.
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: 3D-stacked accelerators push local flux toward 1,000 W/cm2 by the early 2030s; incumbent silicone/graphite TIM suppliers optimize for volume manufacturability, leaving the extreme-flux, engineered-per-package segment unserved.
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: US advanced-packaging capacity ramps and 4-5kW accelerator packages 2030-2033 (device-level flux trajectory L14-010; NVIDIA rack-scale liquid transition L14-044; COOLERCHIPS-funded co-design ecosystem L14-030).
ideas.competition_outlook_2030: Honeywell/Shin-Etsu/Dow incumbent TIMs, Indium Corp metal TIMs, in-house packager solutions; academic liquid-metal work commoditizing basic formulations - differentiation is reliability engineering + application process.
ideas.timing_window_risk: Packagers may design around TIM1 via direct-liquid or embedded cooling; incumbent materials giants can outspend if the segment proves large.
ideas.extreme_edge: Metallic interfaces that survive thousands of power cycles without pump-out or intermetallic failure at fluxes where polymers burn out.
ideas.first_experiment: 2028: test-vehicle campaign showing <=5 mm2K/W with <10% degradation over 1,000 power cycles at 500 W/cm2 local flux, benchmarked against two incumbent TIMs on identical vehicles.
ideas.founder_fit_note: Low direct fit: this is a materials/metallurgy and process-licensing play, with founder skills contributing only peripherally via instrumentation for power-cycling and accelerated-reliability test rigs.
ideas.fix_applied_notes: 2026-07-13 FIX applied: converted consulting-shaped offering into a productized SKU + dispensing-process license model; 2028 anchor-packager LOI added; the two-incumbent head-to-head reliability delta made the explicit kill gate.
ideas.adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (FIX): Consulting-shaped as written; incumbent materials giants can outspend once segment proves.

ideas.product: A 1.5-6MW modular charging power system: SiC conversion stacks, liquid-cooled MCS dispensing per IEC TS 63379, sequencing/queueing control integrated with fleet-management APIs, containerized for port/rail/mine environments with storage-buffered grid connection options. Merged extensions: optional dual-standard capability - IEC MCS and Chinese swap-station DC interface personalities - plus a station-level DC-backbone architecture for multi-charger sites (from C-15); if P3R2-B-14's offshore-export gate fails, its dual-personality converter folds in here as the interface module (pre-agreed path).

ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: duty-cycle and interconnection studies with one port and one railroad (public data + interviews); MCS standard tracking; power-stack architecture design. 2028: 350kW-1MW prototype stack validated at a university/utility test yard; thermal design of dispenser/cable per published megawatt-cable research. 2029: funded pilot commitment with an EPA Clean Ports grantee port.
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: EPA Clean Ports equipment (already obligated, ~$3B) deploys 2026-2030, and FLXdrive/Cat BEV fleets move from pilot to scale in the early 2030s - the charging bottleneck lands exactly at launch while truck-focused EVSE vendors ignore harsh-duty sites.

ideas.concept: Modular plasma destruction units for concentrated PFAS waste streams (spent AFFF, IX regenerant, membrane reject) for DoD and water utilities
named_buyer_examples: US DoD installation restoration programs (PyroGenesis PFAS contract precedent)
remediation primes (AECOM-class archetype)
municipal utilities with PFAS concentrate streams
ideas.painful_job: DoD has already paid for plasma PFAS destruction (>$2.25M, >300 tonnes treated) but the supplier base is one thin foreign small-cap with MW-torch heritage; utilities and bases generate concentrated PFAS side-streams with no scalable, verifiable on-site destruction option.
ideas.product: A containerized non-thermal/thermal-hybrid plasma destruction unit sized for concentrate streams (100-2,000 L/day), with nanosecond-pulsed power supply lineage, integrated fluoride capture, and destruction-efficiency verification instrumentation producing regulator-grade records.
ideas.frontier_vision: Distributed molecular incineration: PFAS first, then other halogenated and pharma waste streams - plasma chemistry productized around verified destruction, not tonnage.
ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: university plasma-chemistry lab campaign on destruction efficiency vs energy per liter for AFFF/IX-brine surrogates; verification chemistry method development. 2028: skid-scale prototype (10-50 L/day) achieving >99.99% destruction on real concentrates under a DoD-lab or SERDP-style collaboration. 2029: demonstration at one base or utility; cost-per-liter dossier vs SCWO competitors.
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: DoD AFFF transition and site investigations convert into remediation records-of-decision and destruction procurements in the early 2030s; the demonstrated-but-thin supplier base leaves room for a US modular entrant.
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: DoD installation-restoration destruction procurements 2030-2034 following completed AFFF transition (paid precedent L01-036; torch-scale defense buying L01-034/035); utility concentrate-destruction demand as PFAS treatment residuals accumulate (inference - EPA drivers must be re-sourced in P4; flagged).
ideas.competition_outlook_2030: PyroGenesis (torch heritage), SCWO vendors (374Water/Aquagga-class), electrochemical oxidation startups, incineration incumbents under regulatory pressure; verified on-site modularity is the differentiator.
ideas.timing_window_risk: Destruction-technology selection could tip to SCWO if plasma energy economics disappoint; regulatory acceptance of destruction verification methods is unsettled.
ideas.extreme_edge: Breaking the strongest single bond in organic chemistry (C-F) at >99.99% with auditable evidence, in a shipping container.
ideas.first_experiment: 2027-2028: skid prototype demonstrating >99.99% PFOA/PFOS destruction on real AFFF concentrate at <=50 kWh/kg-PFAS with closed fluorine mass balance, verified by an independent analytical lab.
ideas.founder_fit_note: The nanosecond-pulsed power supply and destruction-verification instrumentation align with founder circuits, electromagnetics, and instrumentation skills, while plasma-process chemistry, fluoride handling, and regulatory validation are outside the profile.
ideas.adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (PROMOTE): Paid DoD precedent anchors an enthusiasm-zone lane correctly; verification-chemistry wedge; SCWO competition named; EPA drivers to re-source in P4.

ideas.concept: Sealed negative-pressure (subatmospheric) water/low-GWP two-phase cold-plate loop for >2 kW/chip Chinese AI racks - leak-safe by physics (air leaks in, coolant never leaks out), PFAS-free by design
named_buyer_examples: Envicool (Shenzhen CDU/thermal vendor, one of 7 vendors building Google Deschutes-class CDUs, L14-043)
Alibaba-class hyperscale operators already running HVDC datacenters (L02-048, L02-049)
Chinese colocation operators facing GB 40879 PUE caps (L14-035)
ideas.painful_job: Chinese operators must hit PUE <=1.25/1.2 caps (GB 40879 revision, L14-035) and 'high-efficiency cooling' policy direction (Guo-Neng-Fa-Ke-Ji [2026] No.34, L14-036 - NB: primary text has NO 100%-liquid-cooling mandate) while chip TDP heads to 3-5 kW and 800-1,000 W/cm2 (L14-010), beyond single-phase cold-plate margins; two-phase alternatives historically depended on discontinued PFAS dielectric fluids (two-phase literature L14-001/002/003/029).
ideas.product: A rack-level two-phase loop that runs water or low-GWP fluid below atmospheric pressure through microchannel evaporator cold plates (mechanism: saturation temperature tuned via vacuum setpoint; latent heat absorbs >2 kW/chip at near-isothermal junction; any seal failure leaks air inward, detected by vacuum decay, instead of coolant onto boards). Ships as evaporator plates + manifold + compact vacuum-condenser CDU compatible with T/CIEP 0263-2025 interfaces (L14-039).
ideas.frontier_vision: Become the default leak-safe two-phase thermal architecture for East-Asian AI infrastructure, then extend the same negative-pressure loop to power electronics, EV fast-charge cabinets and aerospace power-dense converters.
ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-2027: university/lab-hosted loop testbed; replicate ITRI-class low-pressure results (L14-053 reports 2.4 kW/chip) on a 1 kW simulated die; file 2-3 core patents (vacuum-setpoint control, inward-leak detection, manifold blind-mate). 2027-2028: joint evaluation agreements (pre-company MOUs) with one Chinese thermal vendor and one TW/JP ODM lab; publish peer-reviewed validation. 2028-2029: 20-50 kW rack demonstrator with a partner lab in HK/Shenzhen; customer discovery with 10+ Chinese operators/ODM thermal teams on GB 40879 compliance economics; qualify supply chain for pumps/vacuum hardware.
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: Single-phase cold plates commoditize by 2030 in China, but >2 kW/chip parts push past single-phase margins exactly as hub-node PUE caps tighten; two-phase is still non-converged in 2026 (four competing architectures, L12-021-class CPO thermal debate; L14-001/002/003) so a 2030 entrant with a leak-safe niche is early, not late.
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: Named: (a) GB 40879 revised PUE caps (<=1.25 new large DCs, <=1.2 national hub nodes) enforced through 2030-2034 buildout (L14-035); (b) East-Data-West-Computing (Dong-Shu-Xi-Suan) hub-node datacenter parks explicitly referenced as active buildout in accepted CN sources (L02-049); (c) national AI-energy action plan cooling-efficiency line (L14-036).
ideas.competition_outlook_2030: Envicool and other Chinese CDU incumbents (7+ vendors already building to Deschutes spec, L14-043) will dominate single-phase; ITRI-fed Taiwan ODM two-phase lines (L14-053) are the closest technical rival; expect at least one Chinese two-phase startup. Differentiator must be the negative-pressure leak-safety mechanism plus fluid-supply independence.
ideas.timing_window_risk: Foreign-founder access: direct sales to Chinese hyperscalers unrealistic; route is IP licensing/joint development with a Chinese thermal vendor (Envicool-class) or ODM design-in via HK entity. Risk that Chinese incumbents productize two-phase in-house before 2030; mitigated by patent position and the documented non-convergence of two-phase architectures.
ideas.commercial_readiness_kill_date: 2034-06-30: kill if no Chinese or Taiwanese ODM/thermal-vendor paid pilot (>=100 kW deployment) by mid-2034.
ideas.extreme_edge: Same loop physics scales to 1,000 W/cm2-class WBG power modules and directed-energy thermal buses.
ideas.first_experiment: 2027-Q2: side-by-side test of negative-pressure water two-phase plate vs best commercial single-phase plate on a 1-2 kW thermal test die; success = >=30% lower junction-to-coolant resistance at equal pump power plus demonstrated inward-leak fail-safe.
ideas.founder_fit_note: Vacuum-setpoint control, vacuum-decay leak-detection instrumentation, and the loop control unit fit founder controls and sensing skills, but the governing two-phase thermal-fluid and evaporator/manifold design work is outside the profile.
ideas.merge_import_notes: 2026-07-13: no content imported. C-04 (FIX) adopts B-01's negative-pressure architecture as its primary variant for the US+CN dual play; B-01 remains the CN-primary canonical with the licensing/ODM access route. Cluster duplicate C-04 is retained as a separate FIX seed per adjudication.
ideas.adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (PROMOTE): Best China-primary seed and only mechanism-level original in thermal family (negative-pressure leak-safe two-phase, PFAS-free by design); correctly rejects the debunked 100%-liquid-mandate claim; access via licensing/ODM honestly planned.

ideas.concept: Electrostatic-chuck power/control subsystem: HV clamp supply with Johnsen-Rahbek leakage compensation, fast declamp, and multi-zone temperature-uniformity electronics for Chinese tool OEMs

Chinese ESC/ceramics houses needing the electronics half (component-localization gap context, L06-054)
ideas.painful_job: ESC behavior is an unsolved coupled problem - dielectric breakdown limits (L06-005), Johnsen-Rahbek charging/discharge dynamics under plasma (L06-006), AlN resistivity tuning (L06-014), and thermal-uniformity control (L06-007, L06-015) - and a Chinese design-space framework paper (L06-004) shows domestic developers are attacking it now; but Chinese OEMs still import the qualified clamp-power/control stack, part of the <12% RF/precision-component localization gap (L06-054).
ideas.product: A qualified ESC electronics module: +/-kV programmable clamp supply with real-time leakage-current-based clamp-force estimation (mechanism: JR contact-current model inversion), arc-safe fast declamp with residual-charge neutralization sequencing, and 10+ zone heater drive with mK-class uniformity control - sold to OEMs and Chinese ceramics makers as the electronic half of a domestic ESC stack. Initial scope (FIX): mature-node (>=28nm) and panel/display tools only, for export-control safety; leading-edge sockets deferred until counsel clears.
ideas.frontier_vision: Standard electronics layer for every electrostatic clamping application - semicon, panel, e-beam tools, space robotics grippers.
ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-2027: lab testbed replicating JR clamp physics on commercial ceramic pucks; publish clamp-force estimation validation; patents on leakage-based force observer + declamp sequencing. 2027-2028: co-development with a university plasma lab to characterize declamp-induced wafer displacement; build reliability data. 2028-2029: joint-evaluation with one Chinese ceramics maker + one OEM dev chamber; pre-qualify supply chain.
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: Chinese ESC localization is behind whole-tool localization; ceramics capability is emerging domestically (L06-004 research base) but the control-electronics half has no focused domestic supplier - 2030 lands after ceramics mature and before electronics commoditize.
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: Named: same 15th-FYP OEM supplier-localization wave (Hengyunchang/Injet precedent L06-042/044; component gap L06-054) extended to ESC subsystems as AMEC/NAURA move to higher-aspect-ratio etch where clamp/thermal control binds (etch growth documented L06-048). ideas.competition_outlook_2030: Incumbent ESC vendors (US/JP) ship integrated solutions; Chinese ceramics entrants will seek electronics partners rather than build - the seed's channel; risk of AE/MKS bundling clamp supplies (L06-039). P4 verification task: confirm AE/MKS clamp-supply bundling (L06-039) does not foreclose the merchant socket.
ideas.timing_window_risk: Same export-control caution as B-05 (advanced-node exposure) though ESC electronics are less node-specific; foreign founder should target mature-node and panel/display tools first. Flag: ESC-specific China demand evidence is inferential (L06-004 + localization gap), not a named tender - verify in P4.
ideas.commercial_readiness_kill_date: 2033-12-31: kill if no ceramics-maker or OEM co-development agreement by end-2033.
ideas.extreme_edge: Clamp-force observer physics reused for electrostatic handling in vacuum robotics and lithography reticle chucks.
ideas.first_experiment: 2027-Q3: demonstrate real-time clamp-force estimation within +/-10% of load-cell ground truth across temperature and plasma-exposure aging on a JR ceramic puck; success includes <100 ms residual-charge-safe declamp.
ideas.founder_fit_note: Strong transfer: the HV programmable clamp supply, leakage-current-based force estimation, declamp sequencing, and multi-zone mK-class thermal control are direct applications of founder circuits, controls, instrumentation, and embedded-systems skills.
ideas.fix_applied_notes: 2026-07-13 FIX applied: product scoped to mature-node/panel tools; demand explicitly labeled inferential pending a named ESC-localization program/tender or ceramics-maker LOI (P4 gate); AE/MKS bundling-foreclosure check added.
ideas.adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (FIX): Genuinely under-picked subsystem; demand currently inferential (self-flagged).

ideas.product: A qualified bidirectional 1-3.75 MW DC converter family with swappable interface personalities: GB/T swap-station DC bus profile on one side, IEC TS 63379 MCS (up to 1,250 V/3,000 A) on the other (mechanism: modular SiC DC-DC bricks + certified protocol/interlock stacks for each standard), enabling one truck/vessel platform to serve both ecosystems.

ideas.concept: DC-link capacitor and converter health prognostics module: embedded ripple/ESR/thermal sensing with physics-based remaining-useful-life models for Chinese HVDC datacenter and industrial converter fleets
named_buyer_examples: Chinese rack/UPS/HVDC power vendors (Delta China L02-048, Autrans L02-049)
State-Grid-supply-chain converter makers as expansion (XJ Electric tender scale, L08-034)
ideas.painful_job: DC-link capacitors are a leading converter failure mechanism and higher power density compresses their thermal margin (canonical reliability physics, L02-010); China's 800V datacenter wave multiplies capacitor-dense converters in mission-critical service (L02-054, L02-048) with no standard embedded prognostics layer - fleet operators discover failures reactively.
ideas.product: A retrofit/embedded sensing module: in-situ ESR/capacitance tracking via existing ripple excitation (mechanism: synchronous demodulation of switching ripple - no injected signals), hotspot thermal estimation, and physics-of-failure RUL models (electrolyte loss / film self-healing statistics), reporting into vendor fleet software. Hardware+model product, not analytics consulting. FIX: defined as a retrofit SKU (clip-on sensing module + gateway) with the cross-vendor failure database as the compounding moat - not vendor-bespoke analytics.
ideas.frontier_vision: The reliability nervous system for the world's converter fleets - datacenters, HVDC valves, EV chargers, drives.
ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-2027: accelerated capacitor-aging lab; validate ripple-demodulation ESR tracking against LCR ground truth across aging; publish. 2027-2028: module prototype in a live 30 kW converter; RUL model calibration; patents on excitation-free tracking. 2028-2029: fleet pilot with one Chinese PSU vendor (design-in via HK); failure-prediction scorecard. FIX gate (2028): LOI from one named design-win path - a PSU/UPS vendor or a third-party maintenance operator; absent it, treat as a feature (sell/license the IP) rather than a company.
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: Prognostics value scales with installed fleet age; China's HVDC/800V fleet installed 2026-2029 hits mid-life exactly at 2030-2034, when unplanned failures become boardroom issues; embedded prognostics resists commoditization via failure-database network effects.
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: Named: maintenance cycles of the Dong-Shu-Xi-Suan-era HVDC datacenter fleet (L02-048/049) and 800V rollouts (L02-054, L02-043) reaching capacitor mid-life 2030-2034; NVIDIA-spec maintenance-cost framing (up to 70% reduction claims, L02-043) shows operators already buy on maintenance economics.
ideas.competition_outlook_2030: Converter vendors' in-house telemetry (default), test-equipment majors, academic spinoffs. Win via excitation-free retrofit capability and cross-vendor failure statistics.
ideas.timing_window_risk: Risk of being a feature, not a product - mitigate by also serving third-party maintenance operators; access via vendor design-in; moderate. Keep the US secondary market active from day one so the business is not hostage to CN vendor access.
ideas.commercial_readiness_kill_date: 2033-12-31: kill if no vendor design-win by end-2033.
ideas.extreme_edge: Same sensing generalizes to IGBT/SiC die-attach degradation tracking.
ideas.first_experiment: 2027-Q1: excitation-free ESR tracking stays within 5% of LCR-meter ground truth across a 2,000 h accelerated-aging run on 20 capacitors inside an operating 10 kW converter, correctly rank-ordering end-of-life.
ideas.founder_fit_note: Strong transfer: synchronous-demodulation ripple sensing, hotspot thermal estimation, and physics-of-failure remaining-useful-life modeling map directly onto founder instrumentation, circuits/signal-processing, embedded, and numerical-analysis skills.
ideas.fix_applied_notes: 2026-07-13 FIX applied: retrofit SKU + cross-vendor failure-database moat made explicit; 2028 named design-win LOI gate added; US secondary market kept active to avoid CN-access dependence.
ideas.adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (FIX): Elegant excitation-free ESR/RUL sensing; feature-not-product is the honest risk.

ideas.product: Primary variant (merged from P3R2-B-01): a sealed negative-pressure (subatmospheric) two-phase loop - saturation temperature set by vacuum setpoint; seal failures leak air inward (vacuum-decay detected) rather than coolant onto boards; PFAS-free by design on water or low-GWP fluids (ITRI-class low-pressure results, L14-053). Second moat: the fluid-menu qualification dataset - cold plate + micro-pump + condenser + charge management co-qualified across a menu of non-PFAS, low-GWP fluids. Sold to ODM/CDU vendors as a drop-in >2kW/chip module with fluid-lifetime warranty data.

ideas.concept: Thermal-shock-tolerant printed-circuit heat exchangers (PCHE): merchant diffusion-bonded recuperators for sCO2 power, heat pumps, and hydrogen precooling
ideas.primary_customer_archetype: power-cycle and heat-pump OEMs, EPC integrators
named_buyer_examples: GTI/SwRI/GE Vernova STEP-lineage sCO2 programs (US)
TerraPower-class advanced-reactor BOP chains (US)
A-CAES/heat-pump developers (DOE LPO-backed) (US)
CNNC/CAS-IET sCO2 projects and steel-sintering retrofit fleet (CN, via licensed entity)
TC Energy-Hanwha pipeline waste-heat lineage (US/KR, L04-047, from E-08)
ideas.painful_job: US: PCHE recuperators are the most-cited sCO2 bottleneck; pilot integrators build bespoke units in-house (L04-039, L04-029, L04-004/005/006), and advanced-reactor BOP components come from a tiny qualified-vendor pool (L04-045). CN: China commercialized waste-heat sCO2 first (Chaotan One 30 MW, L04-048/051) with a state-media-estimated ~300-unit steel-sintering retrofit fleet (single-source figure, flagged) and CAS-IET actively researching thermal-cycling-tolerant PCHEs (L04-029) - i.e., no merchant supplier meets the rapid-cycling spec in either market.
ideas.product: Diffusion-bonded microchannel recuperators engineered for >20 C/min thermal transients (the L04-029 target), with creep/fatigue qualification per ASTM E139 (L04-112) and Sandia-documented materials selections (L04-113); sold as certified cores to cycle OEMs. Merged extensions: sold as catalog frames with published delivery times, not EPC subprojects (from A-12); graded-channel cores with compliant headers distributing transient thermal strain away from bond planes (from B-07); the acceptance-test protocol positioned to substitute for the missing sCO2 performance-test standard (from E-08).
ideas.frontier_vision: The Intel of high-temperature compact heat exchange: standard cores across sCO2 power, industrial heat pumps, thermal storage, hydrogen liquefaction precooling, and fusion-blanket-adjacent heat trains.
ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: materials/bonding research via university + national-lab user facility; subscale core fabrication; publish cycling-durability data addressing the L04-029 gap. 2028: decisive cycling experiment (below); begin ASME code-case conversations. 2029: supply LOIs with one US cycle developer and one CN licensee for the retrofit market; refresh China retrofit-fleet economics from eligible sources (current figure is single-source state media). Added task (from E-08): pre-commitment triangulation of the CN retrofit TAM from eligible sources - the RMB100bn-class figure stays single-source-flagged until then. Structural pairing (adjudication): develop jointly with P3R2-C-19 as one high-temperature-components company.
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: US pilots (STEP scale-up, NET-Power lineage, advanced reactors post-NRC Part 53) and China's first-set-designated sCO2 rollouts both hit equipment-ordering phases 2030-2034; the recuperator is the choke-point component with documented unresolved specs, ideal for a focused merchant entrant.
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: US: advanced-reactor BOP procurement following NRC Part 53 (effective 2026-04, L04-031) and post-STEP commercial sCO2 units (L04-039); DOE LPO-backed long-duration storage builds (L04-114). CN: CNNC Xinjiang molten-salt+sCO2 completion (~2028, L04-051) triggering 'first-set' follow-on procurement and steel-sintering retrofit wave.
ideas.competition_outlook_2030: Heatric/VPE-class incumbent PCHE makers (industry names, not in atlas - flag for P4), OEM in-house shops (L04-039), Chinese institute-affiliated fabricators (L04-029). Differentiation: qualified rapid-transient tolerance + code documentation, not capacity.
ideas.timing_window_risk: Export separability: heat exchangers for CN steel/waste-heat retrofits are low-sensitivity; nuclear-qualified US product line kept in US entity (no CN transfer). Risks: sCO2 commercialization slips (kill if no >=10MW-class merchant order by end-2033); Chinese institutes verticalize; manufacturing capex heavier than electronics seeds. All 2026 project schedules must be re-verified 2029.
ideas.extreme_edge: Surviving 500-700 C, 20+ MPa, and >20 C/min transients for 20-year life in a bonded microchannel core - simultaneous creep, fatigue, and corrosion frontier (L04-113).
ideas.first_experiment: 2028: subscale diffusion-bonded core; 1,000-cycle rapid-transient rig at >=20 C/min with 550 C peaks; post-test sectioning + creep data vs ASTM E139; publish.
secondary_markets: KR: KAERI/KAIST/Jinsol sCO2 consortium and Hanwha Power Systems as first-adopter partners and channel (L04-028, L04-047) [from E-08]
JP: MHI-owned Turboden ORC channel as adjacent heat-to-power distribution relationship (L04-044) [from E-08]
ideas.founder_fit_note: Low direct fit: diffusion-bonded heat-exchanger cores are a materials and thermo-mechanical product, with founder skills applying only peripherally to test-loop instrumentation and creep/fatigue data acquisition.
ideas.merge_import_notes: 2026-07-13: absorbed A-12 (catalog-frames/lead-time framing, STEP/TerraPower buyer detail, creep-test source L04-101), B-07 (graded-channel/compliant-header mechanism, CNNC first-set logic; RMB100bn TAM stays single-source-flagged), E-08 (KR KAERI/Jinsol/Hanwha consortium channel, MHI-Turboden adjacency, CN-TAM triangulation task). Flags unchanged (already US+CN).
ideas.adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (PROMOTE): Canonical PCHE. Independent demand legs (STEP/Part 53 vs Chaotan One/Xinjiang); cycling qualification + code documentation wedge; CN TAM single-source-flagged pending P4; absorb A-12, B-07, E-08.

ideas.concept: Standardized solid-state pulsed-power platform: stackable Marx/modulator modules with digital droop control for industrial e-beam, X-ray, and accelerator RF drive
named_buyer_examples: Varex-class cargo/NDT linac OEMs (US)
DOE ARDAP ecosystem (29 companies funded FY2023) (US)
sterilization-capacity builders replacing EtO (US)
CGN Dasheng Accelerator Technology (CN)
China Isotope & Radiation cyclotron programs (CN)
SLAC GREEN-RF commercialization lineage / DOE facility RF upgrades (US, from C-20)
KR channel: Vitzro Nextec / Pohang Accelerator Laboratory klystron-localization ecosystem as manufacturing partner and validation site (L05-037, from E-05)
ideas.painful_job: US: cargo-screening linac demand is growing (+25% YoY segment, >$55M new orders, L05-033) and EtO-replacement e-beam capacity is duopoly-constrained with backlogs (L05-028), while every project re-engineers bespoke, non-interoperable modulators (five incompatible designs documented, L05-003/005/006/007/008; no dedicated acceptance standard beyond IEC 60060, L05-043). CN: CGN Dasheng signed RMB340M in H1-2025 accelerator orders (+21%, L05-035) and national isotope-center cyclotron tenders are live (L05-036) - the same fragmented drive-chain problem at larger unit volume.
ideas.product: A qualified family of stackable solid-state pulse modules (10-100 kV, kA-class, us-ms pulses) with digital droop correction and standard mechanical/control interfaces, sold as the drive chain to beam-system OEMs - 'the Vicor of pulsed power'. Merged product-family extensions: a published open module/interconnect interface spec offered as a de facto standard with N+2 hot-swap redundancy - the 'OCP of pulsed power' move (from D-05); hot-swap serviceability spec of module swap <30 min (from E-05); one qualified 3 kV brick, series-stacked with per-stage droop correction and fiber-synchronized gate timing (from B-09); and a high-efficiency GaN SSPA RF-drive line (20-100kW envelope-tracking blocks, ~70%+ chain-efficiency target) for accelerator and industrial cavity drive (from C-20).
ideas.frontier_vision: Make pulsed power a catalog engineering discipline: e-beam sterilization, cargo scanning, medical linacs, food processing, fusion-adjacent test stands, and eventually klystron-replacement drive for compact accelerators, all on one module family.
ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: reference-design work + published interoperability spec proposal (leveraging the L05-009 standardization thread); patents on module droop-sharing control. 2027: decisive module demo (below). 2028: OEM evaluation units via lab partnership; acceptance-test protocol aligned to IEC 60060-1:2025 (L05-043). 2029: two OEM design-in LOIs (one US NDT/sterilization, one CN industrial e-beam); refresh backlog/tender data.
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: EtO regulatory pressure and customs screening keep beam demand compounding in both countries while the supply chain remains artisanal; a standardized module vendor entering 2030 rides OEM capacity expansion instead of competing with it.
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: US: EtO-replacement sterilization capacity buildout (duopoly backlog through 2026-27 today, L05-028 - re-verify 2029) and customs/ports scanning recapitalization (L05-033). CN: CGN Dasheng multi-site expansion cadence (L05-035) and isotope-center accelerator procurement (L05-036). Acceptance-protocol emphasis (from A-07): the published acceptance-test protocol aligned to IEC 60060-1:2025 is a procurement differentiator, and ISO 11137-1:2025 revalidation cycles add sterilization-QA pull (L05-042). CN facility-RF upside (from C-20): CEPC-lineage efficiency economics (L05-012/013) and HEPS-class tender culture (L07-045) - upside channel only, subject to the existing CN-entity export partition.
ideas.competition_outlook_2030: US/EU 2030: ScandiNova (L05-047), Jema (L05-030), CPI (L05-050), plus OEM in-house. CN 2030: institute-affiliated suppliers (CAEP/CAS lineage, L05-014 context) and CGN in-house. No player currently sells an interoperable module family - that is the gap.
ideas.timing_window_risk: Export separability moderate: pulsed power is dual-use adjacent (radiography/EMP); keep high-energy variants US-only, CN entity ships industrial-class modules to civilian sterilization/NDT buyers - legal review required by 2028. Market risk: OEMs may insist on in-house drive chains; mitigate via performance/cost proof. Kill if no OEM design-in by end-2033.
ideas.extreme_edge: kA-class, <1% flat-top droop pulse synthesis from air-cooled solid-state stacks at half the cooling burden of legacy PFN systems (vendor-claim benchmark, L05-047).
ideas.first_experiment: 2027: two-module stack, 50kV/500A, 100us pulses at 1% droop with active correction; interoperability demo swapping modules mid-campaign; publish acceptance protocol per IEC 60060-1:2025. RF-drive line decisive experiment (from C-20): 20kW 500MHz GaN SSPA block with envelope tracking demonstrating >=68% chain efficiency into a cavity-equivalent load across 6dB backoff.
secondary_markets: KR: Vitzro Nextec/PAL klystron-localization ecosystem as manufacturing partner and validation site (L05-037) [from E-05]
ideas.founder_fit_note: Pulse-module control, digital droop correction, and standardized digital interfaces fit founder circuits, controls, and embedded skills, while 10-100 kV kA-class high-voltage packaging and insulation engineering sit at the edge of the profile.
ideas.merge_import_notes: 2026-07-13: absorbed D-05 (open-interface standard - the differentiating move), E-05 (hot-swap <30min serviceability + KR Vitzro/PAL channel), A-07 (IEC 60060-1:2025 acceptance-protocol emphasis, Varex-class buyer detail), C-20 (SSPA RF-drive product family + its 20kW/>=68% decisive experiment), B-09 (3kV brick with per-stage droop correction; CGN/CIRC tender evidence already cited). China leg unchanged (already evidenced via L05-035/036); high-energy variants remain US-only per the export partition.
ideas.adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (PROMOTE): Canonical modulator platform. Absorb D-05 open-interface standard (the differentiating move), E-05 hot-swap serviceability, A-07 acceptance protocol, C-20 SSPA line, B-09 brick detail.

ideas.concept: kW-class 20-80 K turbo-Brayton cryocoolers: gas-bearing modular cryogenic engines filling the gap between lab cryocoolers and bespoke helium plants
ideas.primary_customer_archetype: fusion magnet plants, HTS grid operators, quantum/science facilities
named_buyer_examples: US fusion magnet factories (CFS-class) (US)
Bluefors-era quantum facilities scaling cryo plant (US)
State Grid HTS cable operators (Shanghai line) (CN)
ASIPP/CFETR-lineage magnet programs (CN)
mid-scale national-lab/university magnet and accelerator programs (US, from D-03)
KEPCO/LS superconducting datacenter-grid lineage (KR side market, L03-043, from D-03)
IHEP-class Chinese facility programs (procurement style per HEPS tenders, L07-045; license-only channel, from B-11)
ideas.painful_job: US: fusion/HTS systems need kW-class 4-20K-adjacent cooling while commercial catalogs top out at mW-W scale (L03-050 vs the ~9.5kW-equivalent JT-60SA-class plants, L03-025-context); dilution-refrigerator cooling power is separately a documented quantum-scaling constraint (L07-014/015/016/017). CN: the operating Shanghai HTS cable and ASIPP's high-field program carry the same mid-scale cryogenic burden with bespoke plants (L03-041/052, L03-029).
ideas.product: A sealed turbo-Brayton cryocooler family: 200W-2kW at 20-80K, gas-bearing turboalternators, >5-year maintenance-free targets, rack-integrable - the missing product class between GM cryocoolers and custom helium refrigerators (the same machine class Nexans' SupraMarine demonstrator uses, L03-034). Product definition imported from merged duplicates D-03/B-11: catalog-priced skids with ~6-month lead times, N+1 redundancy, automated cooldown and remote monitoring - productized like industrial chillers, not engineered plants.
ideas.frontier_vision: Cryogenics as infrastructure: plug-in cold for HTS grids, fusion plants, hydrogen liquefaction nodes, quantum datacenters, and space simulators - the Carrier of the cold economy.
ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: turboalternator + gas-bearing research in a university turbomachinery lab; loss budget model; patents on bearing/alternator integration. 2028: decisive cold-stage demo (below). 2029: pilot LOI with one US fusion/HTS program; CN market entry study (licensing vs entity) with export-counsel review; refresh quantum-export-control scope. FIX: capex staged - component/cold-head work only until a named buyer LOI is in hand; no full-skid integration spend before that.
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: Fusion magnet factories, HTS grid pilots, and quantum buildouts all cross from lab scale to plant scale 2030-2034; every one hits the same cryogenic plant gap; incumbents (GM-cycle vendors, big-plant EPCs) leave the middle open.
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: US: DOE fusion milestone follow-ons and magnet-factory expansions (L03-032/035); quantum facility scaling (Bluefors' US manufacturing base, L13-041, evidences localization pull). CN: HTS cable follow-on city deployments (L03-041/052) and ASIPP/CFETR magnet infrastructure (L03-029, L03-024).
ideas.competition_outlook_2030: Air Liquide/Linde turbo-Brayton at large scale and Cryomech/Sumitomo GM-class at small scale (L03-050) - the mid-band is thin; Stirling vendors adjacent. CN: domestic cryocooler makers scaling up. Expect 1-2 Western entrants by 2030; reliability track record is the moat. FIX (pre-promotion P4 requirement): full competitor map of the 100W-2kW @ 20K band - Air Liquide/Linde turbo-Brayton lines, Stirling vendors, and Chinese cryocooler makers - because the mid-band-gap claim currently rests on one vendor-spec-sheet class of evidence (L03-050).
ideas.timing_window_risk: Export-control separability is the weakest in this batch: cryogenics is entangled with quantum controls (US 2024 rule; 2025 Entity-List additions reportedly targeted Chinese cryo suppliers - unconfirmed, flagged). FIX: CN participation is license-only (no CN beachhead entity) and the flags are set accordingly - US/allied beachhead 2030, CN grid/science demand served via a licensed local partner only if counsel clears, otherwise the CN chapter lapses. Staged capex: no full-skid build before a named buyer LOI. Kill if cold-stage efficiency targets unmet by 2033.
ideas.extreme_edge: Maintenance-free rotating machinery at 20K: gas-bearing turboalternators spinning >100 krpm with cryogenic-cycle efficiencies approaching large-plant figures at 1/100 scale.
ideas.first_experiment: 2028: single-stage turbo-Brayton cold head demo delivering >=200W at 50K equivalent with gas-bearing turboalternator; 1,000h endurance; publish loss budget vs GM baseline.
ideas.founder_fit_note: Low direct fit: gas-bearing turbomachinery and cryogenic engineering dominate the product, with founder skills transferring only to the turboalternator drive electronics, sensing, and health telemetry.
ideas.fix_applied_notes: 2026-07-13 FIX applied: P4 competitor-map requirement (Air Liquide/Linde turbo-Brayton, Stirling vendors, Chinese makers) added before promotion; CN leg set to license-only and flags lowered honestly (primary_market US+CN->US, china_beachhead->false; CN chapter documented in text); staged-capex rule (no full skid before named buyer LOI). Merged in B-11 (CN mid-scale buyers, HEPS tender context) and D-03 (catalog-price/6-month-lead-time product definition, KR side market).
ideas.adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (FIX): Mid-band gap plausible but competitive claim rests on one vendor spec sheet.

ideas.concept: Precision GaN pump-driver and laser-power modules: nanosecond-class programmable current drive as a merchant subsystem for industrial and defense-adjacent laser OEMs
named_buyer_examples: nLIGHT (US)
Coherent (US)
US directed-energy primes (US-entity-only line)
Wuhan Raycus (CN)
Han's Laser (CN)
ideas.painful_job: US: laser-diode drive remains an active research area (GaN pulsed drivers, LLC modulation, ps-resolution control - L12-025/026/027), so every OEM carries driver NRE while A&D laser demand accelerates (nLIGHT A&D $175.3M, L12-033). CN: the fiber-laser price war (high-power revenue -6.6% on +30% units at Han's, L12-037) forces cost-down and outsourcing of non-core electronics while export volumes boom (Raycus handheld +66%, L12-038).
ideas.product: A qualified driver family: multi-channel GaN pump drivers (kA-class aggregate, ns edge control, integrated safety interlocks) plus TMI-aware pump-modulation firmware hooks (L12-016/017/018), sold as modules/reference designs to laser OEMs; separate US-only line for directed-energy primes. Imported differentiator (from merged A-18): integrated diode-health telemetry (droop, threshold-drift tracking) in every driver; TMI-aware pump-modulation firmware remains the durable moat.
ideas.frontier_vision: The power-electronics backbone of photonics: pump drive, pulse shaping, and energy recovery for industrial lasers, DEW, medical lasers, and CPO laser sources - electronics margin extracted from a photonics industry that undervalues it.
ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: driver research with a university fiber-laser lab; demonstrate TMI-mitigation via pump modulation; publish + patents. 2027: decisive multi-channel demo (below). 2028: evaluation units with one US OEM; CN OEM cost/performance benchmark via academic partner. 2029: design-in LOIs both markets; export-control partition review (commercial vs DEW line). FIX gate (2028): OEM buy-vs-build discovery must convert to two PAID evaluations (one US, one CN commercial); if OEM appetite fails, fold the driver line into P3R2-D-10 as its power stage and stop standalone.
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: By 2030 Chinese OEMs are margin-starved commodity manufacturers hungry for cost-down subsystems, and US OEMs are scaling DEW/industrial output - both need drive electronics they under-invest in; a specialist enters with cross-market volume economics no in-house team matches.
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: US: JLWS-generation directed-energy fielding programs scaling 2030-2034 ($847M ceiling structure today, L12-031/032 - US-entity line only) plus industrial/CPO laser source growth (L12-036). CN: continued export-driven handheld/cutting volume growth and consolidation-driven outsourcing (L12-037/038). CN demand chapter (merged from B-19): the fiber-laser price war is pushing Han's/Raycus up-market into ultrafast/UV drilling for AI-server advanced PCB/substrate capacity (L12-037/038) - exactly the platforms that need qualified precision drivers.
ideas.competition_outlook_2030: In-house driver teams at IPG/Raycus/Han's (L12-034/051/037), analog-semi reference designs (TI/ADI-class), 1-2 module startups. Moat: qualified reliability data + TMI-aware firmware integration.
ideas.timing_window_risk: Export-control separability is manageable but sharp-edged: commercial drivers are low-sensitivity; any DEW-affiliated line must be strictly US-entity with no CN ownership links, or US primes will not buy - structure two entities from day one, accept that a forced choice may drop one market (then reassign batch). Chinese OEMs may refuse external drivers (NIH culture) - test willingness in 2028 discovery. FIX: the US(DEW)/commercial two-entity partition is a day-one structural requirement, not a contingency.
ideas.extreme_edge: Kiloamp-aggregate current steering with nanosecond programmability and functional-safety interlocks in a palm-size module.
ideas.first_experiment: 2027: 8-channel GaN pump driver, 1kA aggregate, ns-class edge control; closed-loop pump-modulation TMI trial on a university 1kW fiber amplifier; publish stability gain.
ideas.founder_fit_note: Strong transfer: multi-channel nanosecond-edge current-drive circuits, safety interlocks, and diode-health telemetry are direct founder-profile circuits, embedded-systems, and instrumentation work.
ideas.fix_applied_notes: 2026-07-13 FIX applied: 2028 two-paid-evals buy-vs-build gate added; two-entity US(DEW)/commercial partition made a day-one requirement; fold-into-D-10 fallback codified. Merged in A-18 (diode-health telemetry differentiator) and B-19 (CN price-war up-market OEM pivot as the CN demand chapter).
ideas.adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (FIX): Component-squeeze risk; TMI-aware firmware is the only durable differentiator.

ideas.concept: Gas-foil bearing and shaft-seal cartridges for sCO2/ORC turbomachinery: standardized rotor-support modules solving the documented bearings/seals bottleneck
named_buyer_examples: US sCO2 pilot integrators (GTI/SwRI lineage) (US)
ORC/heat-pump turbomachinery OEMs (US)
CAS-IET / SMDERI compressor programs (CN, via licensed entity)
CNNC sCO2 equipment chain (CN)
ideas.painful_job: US: Sandia's technical assessment names gas-foil bearings, seals, and turbine degradation among five core sCO2 materials/machine challenges (L04-113), and pilot programs still engineer rotor support bespoke per machine (L04-039; sealing research active, L04-101). CN: three separate national compressor programs (SMDERI, CAS-IET - L04-102/106/109) are each re-solving the same bearing/seal problems for the commercial waste-heat fleet (L04-048/051).
ideas.product: Qualified cartridge modules: engineered gas-foil bearing sets and face-seal assemblies for 10-150mm shafts at sCO2/ORC conditions, with life-test documentation - sold to OEMs like turbocharger CHRA cartridges. Structural pairing (FIX): develop and pitch jointly with P3R2-C-08 as one high-temperature-components company (PCHE cores + rotor-support cartridges share buyers, code paths, and test infrastructure).
ideas.frontier_vision: The rotor-support standard for the high-density power-cycle era: sCO2, ORC, heat pumps, fuel-cell air machines, and hydrogen turboexpanders all spin on our cartridges.
ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: bearing/seal test-cell research at a university turbomachinery lab; publish leakage/life maps. 2028: decisive cartridge experiment (below). 2029: OEM supply LOI; re-verify sCO2 project pipeline (2026 schedules will have moved). FIX gate (2028): proceed past test-cell work only on machine-build signals - STEP Phase 2 hardware orders (US) or CNNC first-set follow-on orders (CN); otherwise hold as an option.
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: If sCO2/heat-pump cycles commercialize 2030-2034 (US pilots + China first-set rollouts), every OEM needs rotor support they currently hand-build; a cartridge vendor collapses their schedule risk - a classic component play on someone else's capex wave.
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: US: post-STEP commercial sCO2 machine builds and DOE-backed storage/heat-pump turbomachinery (L04-039, L04-114). CN: Xinjiang first-set completion (~2028) unlocking serial waste-heat machine production (L04-051, L04-102/106).
ideas.competition_outlook_2030: OEM in-house rotor groups; seal majors (John Crane/EagleBurgmann-class - industry names, flag for P4); no merchant sCO2-qualified cartridge vendor visible in atlas. CN: institute-affiliated shops. P4 requirement: map the seal-major incumbent response (John Crane/EagleBurgmann-class) before promotion.
ideas.timing_window_risk: Entirely levered to sCO2/advanced-cycle commercialization pace - if the category slips (US pilots stall, China stays institute-internal), demand never materializes: kill 2033 if no OEM development contract. Export separability good for non-nuclear machines; nuclear-cycle variants US-only.
ideas.extreme_edge: Oil-free rotor support at 500 C-class, 20MPa, 100+ krpm in a supercritical fluid that dissolves lubricants and attacks materials (L04-113).
ideas.first_experiment: 2028: seal+foil-bearing cartridge on a heated high-pressure N2/CO2 spin rig; leakage and lift-off/life map vs labyrinth/oil baselines; publish.
ideas.founder_fit_note: Low direct fit: gas-foil bearings and face seals are mechanical/tribological components, with founder skills applying only peripherally to life-test instrumentation and data acquisition.
ideas.fix_applied_notes: 2026-07-13 FIX applied: 2028 machine-build-signal gate (STEP Phase 2 hardware orders / CNNC follow-ons); paired with C-08 as one high-temperature-components company; P4 seal-major incumbent mapping required. Absorbed B-08 (CN dry-gas-seal/foil-bearing variant; its CN institute buyers and mechanism detail were already present here).
ideas.adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (FIX): 100% levered to others' commercialization pace; sound component play if the wave arrives.

ideas.concept: Direct-DC renewable-to-datacenter coupling converters: multiport MVDC blocks that connect PV/BESS plants straight into 800VDC campuses without AC double conversion
named_buyer_examples: US hyperscalers contracting 24/7 clean power (Google-Fervo precedent) (US)
US datacenter-campus developers (multi-GW electrification orders context) (US)
Chinese campus operators under the green-power direct-connection framework (CN)
Chinese hyperscalers with HVDC lineage (Alibaba ecosystem) (CN)
