ideas.product: Multiport DC-DC power blocks (1-10MW): PV-string/BESS MVDC input to 800VDC campus bus, built on multiport-SST topology research (L02-107); sold to EPCs as the 'DC substation in a container'. Wedge (FIX): protection coordination and inter-port fault isolation (A-02/E-14 lineage) - the capability inverter majors do not productize - NOT efficiency-point marketing.
ideas.frontier_vision: Delete AC from the energy-to-compute chain: generation-to-GPU as one DC system across campuses, then industrial parks and charging hubs - the architecture the 2030s grid converges toward.
ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: architecture + protection-coordination study (university, DC-GRIDS-adjacent); simulate campus-scale direct-DC economics vs AC baseline. 2028: decisive 250kW multiport pilot (below). 2029: developer/EPC LOIs both markets; re-verify CN policy implementation detail and US interconnection rules (both will have evolved from 2026). FIX gate (2028): a named US campus/IPP DC-coupling pilot commitment; absent it, re-batch the seed.
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: By 2030 the two biggest DC ecosystems (US hyperscale, CN policy-backed campuses) both have DC on each side of the meter and only legacy AC in between; the conversion block that removes it rides named policy (CN) and cost/efficiency (US) simultaneously.
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: US: 24/7 clean-power procurement scaling with gigawatt campuses (L04-050 precedent; L08-033 capital flow). CN: green-power direct-connection implementation projects under the 2026 action plan (L14-036).

CN: Huawei/Sungrow-class giants (Sungrow power-electronics base evidenced at L11-051) - the hardest competitor set in this batch; a focused multiport-protection-first design and OCP alignment is the wedge.

FIX: CN participation is license-out only (Sungrow/Huawei-class incumbents make direct entry non-credible) and flags are lowered accordingly - the CN policy leg (green-power direct-connection, L14-036) is documented as a licensing chapter, not a beachhead. Export separability good (grid power electronics). Kill if no MW-class pilot commitment by end-2033.
ideas.extreme_edge: MW-scale multiport DC power flow with sub-millisecond fault management across three DC domains (PV, storage, campus) - the grid problem AC never had to solve.
ideas.first_experiment: 2028: 250kW three-port DC-DC prototype (PV-emulator + battery + 800V load bus) on a microgrid testbed; demonstrate fault isolation between ports in <1ms and round-trip efficiency vs AC-coupled baseline.
ideas.founder_fit_note: Protection-coordination logic and inter-port fault-isolation control align with founder controls and embedded skills, but MW-class multiport converter hardware and EPC-facing deployment are outside the profile.

ideas.concept: Electrolyzer stack degradation-emulation and bankability test systems: programmable ripple/intermittency benches that turn stack warranties into measurable engineering
named_buyer_examples: US H2Hubs project developers and their lenders (US)
Plug Power-class OEM warranty engineering (US)
LONGi/Peric/Sungrow hydrogen divisions (CN)
Chinese EPC/owner acceptance-test labs for mega-tenders (CN)
ideas.painful_job: US: stack warranties are written before the industry knows which stress (OCV rest, ripple amplitude, load-following rate) drives which degradation mode (L11-001/003/004/021/031) - a direct bankability problem for H2Hubs-financed projects (L11-032) and OEMs bleeding order books (L11-047/048). CN: 100+-unit multi-vendor tenders (L11-050/051) and flagship underperformance (Kuqa, L11-049) create buyer-side acceptance-testing demand with no standard test hardware.
ideas.product: Turnkey 50-500kW stack test benches with programmable rectifier-ripple synthesis, renewable-profile playback, OCV-cycling protocols, and impedance diagnostics; plus a shared accelerated-vs-field degradation dataset licensed to financiers - the Chroma of electrolysis (battery-formation analogy: L11-043/044).
ideas.frontier_vision: The qualification authority for electrochemical assets: electrolyzers first, then flow batteries, CO2 electrolyzers, and formation lines - whoever owns degradation truth owns the industry's cost of capital.
ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: replicate published degradation stressors on a 10kW bench (university electrochemistry lab); publish accelerated-test correlation study - the field's missing dataset. 2027: decisive 50kW bench (below). 2028: paid pilot campaigns via lab (OEM + one lender consortium). 2029: purchase LOIs; refresh the demand picture - Western OEM health and Chinese tender cadence will have shifted from 2026.
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: Whether hydrogen booms or consolidates by 2030, both outcomes need qualification: boom -> acceptance testing at tender scale (CN); consolidation -> survivors differentiate on warranty credibility (US/EU). Test infrastructure is the hedge position on a volatile industry - deliberately anti-fragile to the L11 demand contradiction.
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: US: H2Hubs Phase-2 construction/commissioning waves with lender-mandated performance validation (L11-032). CN: second-wave green-H2 base tenders and retrofit/acceptance programs following first-wave underperformance (L11-049/050/051).
ideas.competition_outlook_2030: Greenlight Innovation-class test-stand vendors (industry name, flag for P4 - not in atlas), battery-test majors extending sideways (Chroma/Hioki, L11-043/044), OEM in-house labs. Wedge: degradation-science depth + financier-facing datasets, not just power hardware.
ideas.timing_window_risk: Hydrogen demand volatility caps growth (order books shrinking now, L11-047/048) - but the seed monetizes uncertainty itself; worst case is a slow niche, not a zero. Export separability excellent (test equipment; CN entity straightforward). Kill if neither market yields bench sales nor paid campaigns by end-2033.
ideas.extreme_edge: Synthesizing 20-year renewable-coupled electrochemical stress histories in 90-day campaigns with per-mechanism attribution (catalyst dissolution vs membrane vs contact resistance).
ideas.first_experiment: 2027: 50kW programmable-ripple/OCV-cycling bench; 90-day accelerated campaign on two commercial short stacks; publish correlation of induced vs literature degradation signatures.
ideas.founder_fit_note: Strong transfer: programmable ripple synthesis, renewable-profile playback, impedance diagnostics, and degradation-data pipelines are test-and-measurement engineering within founder instrumentation, controls, and data-acquisition skills, with stack electrochemistry interpretation as the main outside dependency.
ideas.adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (PROMOTE): Anti-fragile response to the L11 contradiction: demand survives boom and consolidation branches; Chroma-sideways threat named; P4 to re-source the standards-gap citation (self-flagged inference).

ideas.concept: Merchant quench-detection-and-protection subsystem for REBCO/HTS magnets: co-wound distributed fiber-optic sensing plus sub-millisecond active energy extraction, sold with a qualification dossier to fusion companies and national labs.
named_buyer_examples: Commonwealth Fusion Systems (sells magnets to Realta, WHAM)
Tokamak Energy / TE Magnetics
DOE milestone-program fusion awardees
university/national-lab high-field magnet programs
US-ITER/ORNL magnet operations (from A-04/E-07, L03-030)
ideas.painful_job: HTS coils have very high minimum quench energy and slow normal-zone propagation, so voltage-tap detection fails; builders must choose between no-insulation windings (poor ramp/control) or unproven fast detection, over-engineering every multi-megajoule magnet (L03-004, L03-018, L03-020, L03-021).
ideas.product: Retrofittable quench detection + protection package: Rayleigh-backscatter fiber co-wound in the coil plus RF/acoustic hotspot channels, fused in a deterministic FPGA trigger driving a qualified multi-kV energy-extraction/CLIQ-class dump circuit; delivered with test data at 10-20 T relevant conditions. Merged extensions: delivered with a published acceptance-test protocol and warranty-support framing for merchant magnet sales (from A-04); the accumulated quench-precursor signature dataset across driven-quench campaigns is the compounding moat (from E-07).
ideas.frontier_vision: Become the certification-grade 'flight recorder and ejection seat' for every high-field HTS magnet -- fusion, NMR, accelerator, industrial -- the way rad-hard PLCs became mandatory in nuclear I&C.
ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: university magnet-lab access (e.g., high-field magnet facility) and instrumentation of small REBCO pancakes; publish detection-latency benchmarks. 2027-28: DOE SBIR/INFUSE-class grants with a fusion partner; controlled-quench campaigns on 1-2 T coils. 2029: demonstrate 100 ms-class pre-quench warning plus automated dump on a multi-kA coil at a partner lab; freeze v1 architecture and qualification plan.
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: SPARC-class results in 2026-27 convert into pilot-plant magnet builds around 2030; every new builder needs protection it cannot buy today, and qualification data accumulated 2027-2029 is the moat at exactly that moment.
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: Post-SPARC fusion pilot-plant magnet procurement (CFS ARC-class, Tokamak Energy ST80-HTS follow-on) and the DOE Milestone-Based Fusion Development Program renewal decision after its FY2027 authorization window (L03-032, L03-033, L03-035); CFETR magnet procurement if it opens (L03-024) is upside, not baseline. Side market (from E-07): KEPCO + LS Cable/LS Electric superconducting datacenter-grid MOU as a cable-monitoring channel (L03-042/043); US-ITER/ORNL magnet operations as lab-side buyers (L03-030). CN chapter (from C-17): ASIPP/CFETR demand logic exists (L03-024/029/037) but is served only via license-out and ONLY if export counsel clears - quench protection is dual-use-adjacent; no CN beachhead, flags unchanged.
ideas.competition_outlook_2030: In-house teams at CFS/TE Magnetics (L03-035, L03-044), national-lab solutions (CERN CLIQ lineage, tuned for LTS), instrumentation vendors without protection power electronics. No merchant vendor of an integrated HTS detection+dump product identified in the atlas.
ideas.timing_window_risk: If no-insulation windings prove fully sufficient at pilot-plant scale, the detection problem shrinks; if a magnet builder open-sources a good solution, differentiation collapses to service/qualification.
ideas.extreme_edge: Watching a superconductor start to die in real time -- centimeter-resolved strain/temperature along kilometers of tape at kilohertz rates -- and killing a 100 MJ discharge before it burns the coil.
ideas.first_experiment: Co-wind fiber + RF pickup in a 1-2 T REBCO double-pancake, drive controlled quenches, and demonstrate >=100 ms warning before thermal runaway with <1% false-trigger rate (2027, university magnet lab).
secondary_markets: JP: Furukawa/Fujikura REBCO ecosystem as conductor-QA partners (L03-044) [from E-07]
KR: KEPCO + LS Cable datacenter superconducting-grid MOU as cable-monitoring side market (L03-042, L03-043) [from E-07]
ideas.founder_fit_note: Strong transfer: distributed fiber-optic sensing readout, multi-channel fusion in a deterministic FPGA trigger, and qualified energy-extraction electronics map directly onto founder instrumentation, embedded-systems, circuits, and data-acquisition skills.
ideas.merge_import_notes: 2026-07-13: absorbed A-04 (acceptance-test protocol / warranty framing for merchant magnet sales; multi-modal sensing sources L03-005/006/007/008), C-17 (CN ASIPP/CFETR chapter - documented as license-out-only, counsel-gated; china_beachhead stays false), and E-07's protection half (precursor-dataset moat + KEPCO/LS cable-monitoring side market; its QA-bench half went to D-02).
ideas.adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (PROMOTE): Canonical quench detection/protection. Falsifiable 2027 experiment; honest NI-winding substitution risk. Absorb A-04 protocol framing, E-07 precursor-dataset moat + KEPCO/LS side market; CN only if counsel clears.

ideas.concept: Reel-to-reel contactless REBCO tape quality metrology (Hall-array critical-current mapping + delamination screening) sold to tape vendors and magnet builders as certified acceptance instrumentation.
named_buyer_examples: Commonwealth Fusion Systems (Devens tape/magnet line)
Furukawa/SuperPower (several-hundred-km supply deal to Tokamak Energy)
Shanghai Superconductor / Shanghai Shangchuang (CN domestic capacity buildout)
Western Superconducting Technologies (CN, ITER strand lineage, L03-031, from B-10)
ASIPP Hefei high-field magnet program (CN, L03-029/037, from B-10)
ideas.painful_job: Fewer than 10 tape vendors supply the world; a single fusion order absorbs a meaningful share of annual output, yet Ic uniformity, strain/delamination and screening-current degradation force magnet builders to over-specify and re-test tape with bespoke, non-comparable acceptance methods (L03-003, L03-019, L03-018, L03-020, L03-021).
ideas.product: 77 K reel-to-reel scanner: dense Hall-sensor array plus lock-in thermal imaging maps position-resolved Ic and detects buried delamination/dropouts at m/s line speed, with transport-Ic-calibrated reporting and a shared acceptance data format for vendor and buyer. Merged extensions: 10kA-class cable/CICC acceptance stations and financier/insurer-grade data packages (from C-11); per-meter 'conductor passport' reporting framing (from B-10); conductor/joint QA benches with a Furukawa/Fujikura partnership channel (from E-07).
ideas.frontier_vision: The KLA of superconductors: every kilometer of HTS tape and every finished cable/coil ships with a standardized, instrument-traceable quality map, closing the loop back into deposition process control.
ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: benchtop magnetscan rig on purchased tape lots; publish correlation of contactless Ic estimate to transport Ic within a few percent. 2027-28: pilot placements with one US magnet builder and one tape vendor under NRE contracts; extend to coil/cable acceptance heads. 2029: inline-speed prototype at a vendor line; draft an open acceptance-report schema with two counterparties.
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: Tape volume triples-to-10x between 2026 and the early-2030s pilot-plant builds; QC pain scales with volume and with new entrants' unproven lines, peaking exactly at launch.
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: Fusion pilot-plant tape procurement waves (CFS ARC-class; Tokamak Energy ST80-HTS wire deal as precedent, L03-044), Chinese domestic REBCO capacity expansion for import independence (L03-052), and CFETR magnet procurement if construction starts (L03-024). Additional evidenced demand (merged): DOE milestone-program QA needs and CFS magnet-as-product certification (L03-032/035, from C-11); the State Grid Shanghai 35 kV commercial HTS cable and follow-ons (L03-041, from B-10); ASIPP's high-field program (L03-029/037) and WST's ITER-strand QA lineage (L03-031).
ideas.competition_outlook_2030: Vendor in-house scanners (THEVA Tapestar-class -- judgment), Lake Shore-style lab instruments, national-lab one-offs. No merchant, buyer-trusted acceptance-metrology vendor in the atlas record set.
ideas.timing_window_risk: Tape vendors may bundle 'good-enough' in-house QC data; if one instrument maker partners exclusively with the largest vendor, the neutral-referee position is lost.
ideas.extreme_edge: Imaging supercurrents through millimeters of steel and silver at highway speed -- a contactless ammeter for a quantum fluid.
ideas.first_experiment: Benchtop 77 K reel-to-reel Hall-array scan of 100 m commercial tape; blind-correlate predicted vs transport Ic on 50 cut samples to <5% error (2027).
ideas.founder_fit_note: Strong transfer: Hall-array magnetic sensing, lock-in thermal imaging, and line-speed data acquisition with calibrated reporting are core founder sensors/instrumentation, electromagnetics, and numerical-analysis work.
ideas.merge_import_notes: 2026-07-13: absorbed C-11 (cable/CICC acceptance stations, financier/insurer data packages), B-10 (conductor-passport framing + CN buyer set: WST, ASIPP), and E-07's QA-bench half (conductor/joint benches, Furukawa/Fujikura JP channel). China leg already primary (US+CN, china_beachhead true) and strengthened with eligible CN evidence (L03-041/029/037/031) - flags unchanged.
ideas.adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (PROMOTE): Best instrument seed in pool; demand scales with conductor volume not fusion success (low-beta). Absorb C-11 cable acceptance stations + insurer packages, B-10 passport framing + CN buyers, E-07 QA-bench half.

ideas.concept: RF energy recovery for the world's klystron and gyrotron fleets: multi-stage depressed collector (MSDC) modules plus adaptive drive electronics that close the 12-point gap between simulated and hardware RF efficiency.
named_buyer_examples: DOE accelerator labs (SLAC GREEN-RF commercialization precedent)
fusion ECH system integrators buying ITER-class gyrotrons
tube OEMs/rebuilders (CPI-class) as channel
ideas.painful_job: Most accelerator RF sources run below 50% wall-plug efficiency (L05-039); the 2015 bunching technique simulated >90% klystron efficiency but funded hardware reached only 78.5% (L05-011, L05-012, L05-013); spent-beam energy is dumped as heat, costing large facilities millions annually in electricity and cooling.
ideas.product: Retrofit-at-rebuild MSDC collector assemblies plus gun/drive waveform optimization electronics, supplied to tube OEMs and lab rebuild shops, with guaranteed wall-plug efficiency uplift measured on a standard test protocol.
ideas.frontier_vision: Every megawatt tube ships energy-recovering by default; the same collector/drive stack later powers fusion-plant heating systems where recirculating power directly sets plant economics.
ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: beam-optics and collector design study anchored on a decommissioned S-band klystron; engage SLAC-lineage GREEN-RF collaborators (L05-032). 2027-28: collector prototype on a lab test stand; measure recovered power vs simulation. 2029: retrofit demonstration on an operating test-stand tube with published before/after wall-plug data. FIX additions: 2026-27 includes fleet-size and $-per-efficiency-point arithmetic from named facilities (SLAC-class linacs, ITER-class gyrotron stations) to bound the retrofit TAM; 2028: OEM/rebuilder channel agreement in principle (CPI-class); 2029 DECISION POINT: license the MSDC IP to tube OEMs or fold the drive-electronics line into P3R2-C-09 - a standalone company only if both the channel agreement and the arithmetic clear.
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: Facility electricity costs and decarbonization targets make efficiency retrofits fundable; rebuild cycles of aging tube fleets 2030-2034 are the natural insertion point without new-tube qualification risk.
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: DOE GREEN-RF-style commercialization push (L05-032), fusion ECH procurement following the ITER/Thales gyrotron template as pilot plants order heating systems (L05-029), and collider-class efficiency programs (CEPC's documented ~RMB130M/yr savings case, L05-013 -- China demand logic, access-limited).
ideas.competition_outlook_2030: CPI (L05-050), Thales (L05-029), Canon (judgment), Vitzro Nextec (KR, L05-037) building their own high-efficiency tubes; labs doing one-off in-house collectors. Merchant retrofit modules with guaranteed uplift are unoccupied.
ideas.timing_window_risk: Invasive retrofits only work at rebuild; if OEMs internalize MSDC quickly the wedge narrows to drive electronics and test protocols.
ideas.extreme_edge: Catching a spent electron beam on a staircase of precisely biased electrodes and pushing its energy back into the wall plug.
ideas.first_experiment: MSDC prototype on a decommissioned S-band klystron at a lab test stand: demonstrate >=15-point wall-plug efficiency uplift vs baseline at 50%+ of rated output (2028).
ideas.founder_fit_note: Electron-optics simulation and drive-waveform electronics draw on founder electromagnetics, numerical-analysis, and circuits skills, but vacuum-tube fabrication, collector thermal design, and rebuild-shop integration are outside the profile.
ideas.fix_applied_notes: 2026-07-13 FIX applied: named-facility fleet/$-per-efficiency-point arithmetic task added; 2028 OEM/rebuilder channel-agreement-in-principle milestone; explicit 2029 license-or-fold-into-C-09 decision. Licensing-shaped structure acknowledged (retrofit-at-rebuild + OEM internalization risk).
ideas.adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (FIX): Non-obvious and documented gap, but retrofit-at-rebuild + OEM internalization makes it licensing-shaped.

ideas.concept: Compact solid-state-RF-driven e-beam/X-ray sterilization machine that breaks the IBA/L3 duopoly with a self-shielded, factory-installable unit for the ethylene-oxide phase-out.
named_buyer_examples: contract sterilizers (SteriLab-type operators; Sotera/Steris-class -- judgment)
medical-device manufacturers in-housing sterilization
food/packaging processors
ideas.painful_job: EtO replacement demand has the two pure-play e-beam accelerator makers (IBA Industrial, L3 Applied Technologies) backlogged for years, leaving medical-device and food producers unable to add sterilization capacity on schedule (L05-028); revised ISO 11137-1:2025 tightens radiation-sterilization validation (L05-042).
ideas.product: Self-shielded 10 MeV/30 kW-class e-beam (with X-ray conversion option) built on mass-produced GaN/LDMOS solid-state RF pallets instead of klystrons, shippable in standard modules, with dose-mapping automation aligned to ISO 11137-1:2025 out of the box. Deployment options (FIX, absorbing A-09): the same accelerator/RF core ships either as a central contract-sterilization machine or as a compact self-shielded factory-floor cell for device-OEM in-house sterilization (conveyor + integrated shielding + ISO 11137 dosimetry records) - acknowledging that per-site radiation licensing and contract-sterilization industry structure favor the central variant first.
ideas.frontier_vision: Sterilization as an appliance: distributed, per-factory beam processing replacing centralized gamma/EtO plants, then food-safety and materials-modification markets on the same platform.
ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: SSPA-driven accelerating-structure design with a university accelerator lab; regulatory mapping (ISO 11137/FDA pathways). 2027-28: 1 MeV/5 kW prototype beamline validating SSPA drive, gun, and dose uniformity. 2029: 10 MeV injector section + shielding/throughput engineering package; LOIs from two contract sterilizers. FIX gates: 2027 backlog-persistence check (kill if IBA/L3 clear backlogs and cut prices); 2028 SSPA-vs-klystron cost-crossover validation with vendor quotes; P4 re-source of the EtO regulatory driver from EPA NESHAP/FDA primaries (the current ledger lacks it).
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: Working hypothesis with three load-bearing assumptions, each gated (FIX): (1) the EtO forcing function is durable regulatory pressure, not a one-cycle backlog - must be re-sourced from eligible primary documents (EPA NESHAP amendments / FDA sterilization guidance) in P4; (2) solid-state RF cost curves cross klystron economics before 2030 - validate with vendor quotes by 2028; (3) incumbent backlog persists - 2027 check: if IBA/L3 backlogs clear and prices fall, kill.
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: Continued EtO-replacement capacity build with duopoly backlog precedent (L05-028), ISO 11137-1:2025 revalidation cycles (L05-042), and cargo/industrial linac demand growth signaling accelerator supply tightness (L05-033); China's CGN Dasheng RMB340M order flow proves category economics (L05-035, CN market evidence -- CN treated as competitor territory, not beachhead).
ideas.competition_outlook_2030: IBA, L3 Applied Technologies (backlogged incumbents), Mevex (judgment), CGN Dasheng expanding from China (L05-035); Co-60 gamma remains substitute with its own supply constraints.
ideas.timing_window_risk: If incumbent backlogs clear by 2029 and they cut prices, a new entrant faces entrenched service networks; regulatory qualification timelines could push first revenue past 2031.
ideas.extreme_edge: A shipping-container linac: thousands of transistor watts phase-summed into a 10 MeV electron broom that sterilizes a pallet in seconds.
ideas.first_experiment: SSPA-driven 1 MeV/5 kW CW prototype beamline: demonstrate stable dose rate and RF-chain wall-plug efficiency >55% (2028, university accelerator facility).
ideas.founder_fit_note: Solid-state RF drive, dose-mapping automation, and machine controls fit founder circuits, controls, and instrumentation skills, while accelerator physics, radiation shielding, and sterilization regulatory validation are outside the profile.
ideas.fix_applied_notes: 2026-07-13 FIX applied: EtO forcing function flagged for P4 re-sourcing from EPA NESHAP/FDA primaries; SSPA-vs-klystron cost crossover to be validated with quotes by 2028; 2027 backlog-persistence kill check added; A-09's self-shielded in-house cell absorbed as a deployment option (with its licensing-friction caveat).
ideas.adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (FIX): Canonical sterilization machine; duopoly opening real but three load-bearing assumptions unproven.

ideas.concept: Charge-buildup-immune beam current and dose metrology for ultra-high-dose-rate (FLASH) and industrial electron beams -- the traceable ammeter the field admits it lacks.
named_buyer_examples: FLASH radiotherapy device developers (Varian/IBA-class -- judgment)
industrial e-beam sterilization operators needing ISO 11137 dose traceability
accelerator labs upgrading diagnostics
ideas.painful_job: Two independent 2024-2025 groups showed beam current transformers are corrupted by electric-field-driven charge buildup at FLASH dose rates -- an unresolved calibration/traceability problem for both clinical and industrial e-beam (L05-023, L05-024); sterilization validation standards meanwhile tighten (L05-042).
ideas.product: Hybrid non-intercepting monitor: cavity-resonator charge measurement cross-calibrated against an integrated Faraday/calorimetric reference, with metrology-grade uncertainty budget and drop-in form factors replacing legacy BCTs (Bergoz-class footprint). Merged extension (from A-08): a sterilization-QA product tier - per-unit calibration certificates for e-beam/X-ray plants validating under ISO 11137-1:2025 - alongside the clinical FLASH tier.
ideas.frontier_vision: Own the traceability layer for every high-dose-rate beam on earth -- clinical FLASH, sterilization, cargo scanning, semiconductor e-beam -- then expand into full beam-diagnostics suites.
ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: uncertainty-budget design and cavity prototype; beam time via university medical-physics collaborations. 2027-28: side-by-side campaign (cavity vs BCT vs Faraday cup) on a FLASH-capable linac, published. 2029: pre-production units at two beta sites (one clinical program, one industrial e-beam plant); NIST-traceable calibration chain drafted.
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: FLASH clinical translation and e-beam sterilization expansion both hit regulatory dose-traceability walls around 2030; a validated instrument with published uncertainty is ready exactly when device makers must specify one.
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: FLASH radiotherapy device qualification campaigns (pain documented in L05-023/024), EtO-replacement e-beam capacity requiring ISO 11137-1:2025-compliant dosimetry (L05-028, L05-042).
ideas.competition_outlook_2030: Bergoz Instrumentation (entrenched, published price list, L05-049), in-house lab diagnostics; incumbents optimized for storage rings, not UHDR transients.
ideas.timing_window_risk: Niche instrument market; if FLASH stalls clinically, industrial e-beam alone caps the market at tens of millions.
ideas.extreme_edge: Weighing 10^11 electrons in a microsecond without touching them, to a certified percent.
ideas.first_experiment: Side-by-side cavity monitor vs BCT vs Faraday cup at a FLASH-capable electron linac; quantify BCT error vs dose rate and demonstrate <1% cavity-monitor agreement with calorimetry (2027).
ideas.founder_fit_note: Strong transfer: cavity-resonator charge measurement, Faraday/calorimetric cross-calibration, and metrology-grade uncertainty budgets are precision instrumentation and electromagnetics work at the center of the founder profile.
ideas.merge_import_notes: 2026-07-13: absorbed A-08 (ISO 11137-1:2025 sterilization-QA channel and calibration-certificate product tier). C-10's CN leg NOT imported (mirrored assertion without CN procurement/regulatory evidence - 'fake dual' per adjudication); D-09 remains US-primary.
ideas.adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (PROMOTE): Canonical UHDR metrology. Documented unresolved failure, cheap decisive experiment, honest small-TAM admission; pairs with C-09/D-08.

ideas.concept: Coherent-beam-combining phase-control engine: a channel-scalable electro-optic 'conductor' (controller ASIC/FPGA + modulator front-ends) sold to directed-energy primes and ultrafast-laser OEMs.
named_buyer_examples: nLIGHT Defense and Lockheed Martin Aculight (JLWS awardees)
DARPA AMPED/MELT performers
industrial ultrafast CBC system builders
ideas.painful_job: Transverse mode instability caps single-fiber power, making coherent combining of many channels the only path to 300-500 kW weapons and multi-kW ultrafast tools -- but multi-channel phase-locking, path-length and dispersion management remain bespoke, unsolved engineering rebuilt per program (L12-016, L12-017, L12-018, L12-001, L12-002).
ideas.product: Scalable phase-control stack: per-channel EO modulator front-ends, deterministic sub-microsecond controller (SPGD + interferometric hybrid) proven from 16 to 1,000+ channels, vibration-hardened, with open telemetry for system integration -- the subsystem every combiner needs and no one sells. Merged extension (from A-17): an environmental-grade deformable-mirror control and turbulence/jitter-compensation tier for 100kW-class systems, extending the phase-control engine from fiber arrays to full beam-control stacks.
ideas.frontier_vision: Be the Synopsys of coherent light: the standard control layer for laser weapons, ultrafast machining arrays, beamed power, and eventually phased optical apertures for space links.

ideas.concept: 2-micron drive-laser modules for next-generation EUV sources: kilowatt-class short-pulse thulium/holmium chains targeting the modeled ~5% conversion-efficiency architecture that could obsolete the CO2 drive chain.
named_buyer_examples: ASML/Cymer EUV source operations (San Diego)
Gigaphoton (JP side market)
national labs running EUV source testbeds
ideas.painful_job: Each EUV power step (250 W to 1,000 W, 2018-2025) required re-engineering the CO2-driven tin-plasma architecture, and peer-reviewed work is already modeling a fundamentally different 2-micron drive achieving ~5% conversion efficiency vs ~2-3% -- source suppliers face architecture-change risk with no merchant 2-micron kilowatt-class drive supplier existing (L12-034, L12-003, L12-006).
ideas.product: Modular 2-micron picosecond/nanosecond pre-pulse and drive modules (Tm:fiber coherently-combined front ends feeding Ho:YLF/Ho:YAG power amplifiers), plus a droplet-interaction test service quantifying CE and debris for customer-specific pulse formats.
ideas.frontier_vision: Supply the drive photons for the post-CO2 EUV era and hyper-NA/beyond-EUV scaling -- the highest-leverage kilowatt of laser light in the world economy.
ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: 100 W-class 2-micron ps source in a university ultrafast lab; partner with an EUV plasma group for droplet targets. 2028: CE measurement campaign at relevant intensity on tin droplets; publish CE vs pulse-format map. 2029: 300 W engineering module; engage source-OEM technical staff via SPIE-track visibility. FIX capital rule: no capital beyond the consortium-scale droplet experiment until the CE gate passes. Likely exit stated openly: acqui-license to a source incumbent (ASML/Cymer or Gigaphoton) - this is an option purchase, not a company plan.
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: EUV throughput economics demand >1 kW source power in the early 2030s; if the CO2 chain saturates, the incumbent must buy or build 2-micron capability on exactly our timeline -- a classic option-value wildcard with one deep-pocketed buyer class.
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: EUV source power roadmap pressure beyond the 1,000 W milestone (L12-034) and Gigaphoton's continued next-generation source R&D creating a second buyer (L12-053). Second-buyer hedge (merged from E-13): Gigaphoton (Komatsu group) as the named second source-developer customer (L12-053), hedging single-customer risk.
ideas.competition_outlook_2030: TRUMPF (incumbent CO2 drive supplier, L12-039), IPG/Coherent 2-micron fiber programs (judgment), national-lab prototypes; merchant kW-class 2-micron short-pulse modules remain unserved.
ideas.timing_window_risk: Highest physics risk in this batch: the 5% CE is modeling-only; 2-micron TMI/photodarkening at kW average power is unproven; ASML may double down on CO2 scaling and never switch.
ideas.commercial_readiness_kill_date: 2033-12 (stricter imported gate: kill unless tin-droplet CE >=4% at relevant rep-rate is demonstrated by end-2033)
ideas.extreme_edge: Hitting a 25-micron molten-tin droplet, fifty thousand times a second, with a wavelength tuned so the plasma itself becomes a better EUV lightbulb.
ideas.first_experiment: Measure EUV conversion efficiency on tin droplets driven by a 2-micron ps source at relevant intensities; kill/continue gate at CE >=3% experimental (2028, ~$600k including beam time and target chamber access). Continue/kill ladder (FIX): 2028 experimental CE >=3% to continue; imported E-13 gate: CE >=4% at relevant rep-rate by end-2033 or kill.
ideas.founder_fit_note: Low direct fit: kilowatt-class thulium/holmium laser-chain development is laser physics and photonic-materials engineering, with founder skills applying mainly to drive electronics, timing, and diagnostics instrumentation.
ideas.fix_applied_notes: 2026-07-13 FIX applied: adopted E-13's stricter kill gate (CE >=4% at relevant rep-rate by end-2033; kill date moved 2032-12 -> 2033-12 with the harder bar); Gigaphoton second-buyer hedge imported; acqui-license stated as the likely exit; capital capped at the consortium experiment until the gate passes. Merged in E-13's amplifier-module framing sources (coherent combining L12-001/002; pre-pulse shaping L06-016/017).
ideas.adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (FIX): Best-disciplined moonshot; option purchase, not a company plan.

ideas.concept: Electrohydrodynamically-pumped two-phase cold plates: field-driven, no-moving-parts per-chip flow control for 3-5 kW AI packages, with a non-PFAS working-fluid plan.
named_buyer_examples: hyperscalers writing OCP cooling specs (Google Deschutes CDU authors)
cold-plate/CDU vendors as OEM channel (Boyd/Vertiv-class)
Chinese datacenter cooling vendors under tightening PUE rules (Envicool-class -- judgment)
ideas.painful_job: AI accelerators head toward 3,000-5,000 W and ~1,000 W/cm2 hotspots (L14-010); single-phase cold plates hit flow/approach-temperature walls (Deschutes already demands 3C approach at 2 MW, L14-043), while two-phase adoption is destabilized by the PFAS fluid exit and HFC phasedowns (L14-033, L14-034). Per-channel flow balancing inside plates is an unsolved control problem.
ideas.product: Two-phase microchannel cold plate with embedded EHD conduction micropumps that actively modulate per-channel flow (+/-20%+) against hotspot maps -- silent, no local moving parts, milliwatt drive power -- qualified on low-GWP HCFO/HFO or non-halogenated dielectric fluids, sold to cold-plate OEMs.
ideas.frontier_vision: Electric fields as the last-centimeter coolant pump: self-balancing boiling networks inside every high-power package, from GPUs to power electronics to laser diodes.
ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: EHD conduction-pump cells characterized in candidate non-PFAS fluids; university thermal-lab testbed with 500 W chip emulator. 2027-28: integrated plate demonstrating CHF uplift and hotspot-following flow modulation; ARPA-E COOLERCHIPS-adjacent funding. 2029: OEM evaluation units with reliability (electrode fouling/EMI) data; OCP-aligned spec sheet. FIX gate (2028): a named cold-plate OEM or COOLERCHIPS-lineage co-development agreement - the demand bridge this wildcard currently lacks.
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: Rubin-class rack roadmaps push per-chip powers past what passive manifolding balances by ~2030; EHD's documented lab maturity plus the field's admitted deployment gap (L16-003, L16-004) means the first credible productization wins the transition window.
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: Hyperscaler CDU/cold-plate specification cycles beyond Project Deschutes (L14-043, L14-044), COOLERCHIPS-commercialization wave (L14-030), and China's PUE tightening (GB 40879 revision toward <=1.25/1.2, L14-035) plus the 2026 national AI-energy plan's high-efficiency-cooling push (L14-036) driving CN liquid-cooling refresh cycles.
ideas.competition_outlook_2030: Flex/JetCool microjets (L14-045), ZutaCore waterless two-phase (L14-046), Vertiv-scale integrators (L14-048); all use mechanical pumping -- none offer in-plate field-driven flow control.
ideas.timing_window_risk: EHD is a documented enthusiasm zone: two 2025 reviews note no industrial-scale deployment (L16-003, L16-004); fluid qualification (dielectric + low-GWP + non-PFAS) may not intersect; hyperscalers may brute-force with bigger CDUs. Hard fluid-intersection gate (FIX): the working fluid must simultaneously be dielectric, non-PFAS, and low-GWP; if no candidate passes qualification, kill regardless of EHD results.
ideas.extreme_edge: A cold plate with no pump: ions dragged by kilovolt fields silently steer boiling refrigerant toward the exact square millimeter that is overheating.
ideas.first_experiment: EHD-assisted two-phase microchannel plate on a 500 W emulator: demonstrate >=20% per-channel flow modulation, 1,000 h electrode stability in a non-PFAS dielectric, AND beat the best mechanical-pump cold plate on CHF and hotspot-following at equal pumping power (2027) - parity with mechanical pumping is a kill result.
ideas.founder_fit_note: The high-voltage EHD pump drive, per-channel flow modulation, and hotspot-map feedback control fit founder electromagnetics, circuits, and controls skills, but microchannel two-phase thermal design and fluid qualification are outside the profile.
ideas.fix_applied_notes: 2026-07-13 FIX applied: 2028 named-OEM/COOLERCHIPS co-development demand-bridge gate added; 2027 experiment upgraded to require beating the best mechanical-pump plate on CHF/hotspot-following (parity = kill); hard dielectric+non-PFAS+low-GWP fluid-intersection gate codified.
ideas.adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (FIX): Most elegant wildcard; honestly cites the enthusiasm-zone reviews against itself.

ideas.concept: Burst-mode 'thermal magazine' for directed-energy weapons: a two-phase + phase-change thermal capacitor that absorbs megawatt engagement heat and rejects it slowly, shrinking chillers 10x.
named_buyer_examples: JLWS integrators (nLIGHT Defense, Lockheed Martin Aculight)
counter-UAS HEL platform builders
radar/EW primes with burst thermal duty cycles
ideas.painful_job: A 300-500 kW-class laser at ~1/3 wall-plug efficiency dumps ~1 MW of heat during engagements, but containerized platforms cannot carry megawatt chillers; GAO documents the prototype-to-fielding gap in which SWaP-C of support subsystems is a named obstacle (L12-031, L12-045); defense thermal management has been an open DARPA research thread for three decades (L14-025).
ideas.product: Containerized thermal capacitor: two-phase spreaders move 100+ W/cm2 from diode/gain modules into tonne-scale engineered PCM banks (300+ MJ absorbed over minutes), with a right-sized trickle chiller recharging between engagements; delivered as a qualified LRU with thermal state-of-charge telemetry.
ideas.frontier_vision: Thermal energy storage as a standard military LRU -- lasers, railguns, radars and hypersonic test assets all fire from thermal magazines instead of dragging peak-rated chillers.
ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: PCM selection/encapsulation trades and two-phase spreader design; SBIR entry under DEW support-equipment topics. 2027-28: 100 kW-for-60 s brassboard with full charge/discharge cycling data. 2029: 300 kW-class pallet demonstrator evaluated by a prime; MIL-E qualification plan.
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: JLWS moves from 150 kW initial prototypes toward 300-500 kW fielded systems in the early 2030s; every power doubling squeezes platform SWaP and makes the thermal magazine the cheapest enabling subsystem to buy rather than build.
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: JLWS production/fielding decisions under the $847M ceiling (L12-031), MELT-class modular HEL platforms (per L12-041 program goals), and multi-decade DoD thermal-management program continuity (L14-025).
ideas.competition_outlook_2030: Prime in-house thermal teams, Advanced Cooling Technologies-class thermal houses (judgment), COTS chiller vendors; no productized burst-mode thermal storage LRU identified in the atlas.
ideas.timing_window_risk: If fielded lasers plateau at 150 kW, conventional chillers suffice; PCM cycle-life and mass may erode the advantage on mobile platforms.
ideas.extreme_edge: Firing a megawatt for five minutes out of a box of engineered wax -- heat stored like ammunition, spent like a battery.
ideas.first_experiment: 100 kW-for-60 s thermal-capacitor brassboard: verify source-to-PCM flux >=100 W/cm2, <10 K diode-plate excursion, and 500 charge/discharge cycles without capacity fade (2027).
ideas.founder_fit_note: Low direct fit: the product is phase-change materials and two-phase thermo-mechanical engineering, with founder skills contributing only the thermal state-of-charge telemetry and test instrumentation.
ideas.adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (PROMOTE): Unique in pool; latent-heat magazine vs peak-rated chiller aimed at a GAO-named SWaP gap with dated program ladder; risks named and testable by 2027 brassboard.

ideas.concept: Merchant 100 kWe-class space closed-Brayton power conversion unit and high-voltage PMAD for fission surface power -- the converter layer for the reactor decade.

commercial lunar infrastructure developers (follow-on)
ideas.painful_job: FSP's requirement jumped 40 kWe to >=100 kWe closed-Brayton within one program cycle (L09-031 vs L09-033), stranding Phase 1 baselines; no US merchant supplier of flight-weight 100 kWe-class Brayton converters or kV-class surface PMAD exists -- each team must reinvent it (L09-031, L09-032, L09-033).
ideas.product: Flight-weight He-Xe closed-Brayton turboalternator module (25-50 kWe unit, paralleled to 100+ kWe) with high-speed alternator, foil bearings, and radiator-matched thermal interfaces, plus kV-class DC PMAD/cabling for kilometer-scale lunar distribution -- sold to all reactor primes rather than one. Fold-down path (FIX): define a terrestrial micro-Brayton variant (remote/microgrid gensets, industrial heat recovery) so the technology base survives an FSP slip.
ideas.frontier_vision: Own the power-conversion layer of off-world energy: reactors change, the converter standard persists -- lunar bases, Mars ISRU, and high-power nuclear-electric tugs.
ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: HELD AS OPTION (FIX): studies/SBIR only - no hardware capex until FSP downselect and flight dates are contracted. 2026-27: cycle/mass optimization studies against the 100 kWe RFI spec; NASA SBIR/STTR entries; recruit Brayton heritage (BRU/Kilopower lineage, L09-105); validate the parallel-unit architecture against the RFI spec with >=2 concept teams expressing written subcontract interest. 2028 CHECKPOINT: proceed to the 10 kWe-class turboalternator rig only if downselect/flight dates are contracted; otherwise stay in study mode or kill. 2029 (conditional): vacuum-chamber endurance run (1,000 h) and PMAD brassboard at representative voltage; position as subcontractor to 2+ FSP teams.
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: NSTM-3 directs a >=100 kWe-class reactor for the 2030s with Q1 FY2030 launch-readiness targeting (L09-033); conversion hardware subcontracts flow 2030-2034 exactly when a validated merchant PCU exists.
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: NASA/DOE Fission Surface Power downselect and flight procurement per the August 2025 RFI and April 2026 NSTM-3 memorandum (L09-033); Westinghouse eVinci continuation contracts as parallel demand (L09-032, L09-047).
ideas.competition_outlook_2030: Creare (inside the Lockheed team, L09-031), Brayton Energy (judgment), primes' in-house conversion; merchant multi-team supplier position is open because each team currently builds captive.
ideas.timing_window_risk: Program risk is the dominant risk: FSP has already re-baselined once; if flight procurement slips past 2034 the market is study contracts only.
ideas.extreme_edge: A turbine the size of a fire extinguisher spinning He-Xe at 90,000 rpm above a nuclear core on the Moon, radiating its waste heat to vacuum.
ideas.first_experiment: 10 kWe-class He-Xe turboalternator on foil bearings with electric heater loop: demonstrate >=20% cycle efficiency at flight-relevant turbine inlet temperature and specific mass model validation (2028). (Conditional on the 2028 FSP-contract checkpoint; until then, studies only.)
ideas.founder_fit_note: kV-class PMAD, high-speed alternator drive, and telemetry draw on founder circuits, controls, and electromagnetics skills, but He-Xe turbomachinery, foil bearings, and space-flight qualification dominate the product and are outside the profile.
ideas.fix_applied_notes: 2026-07-13 FIX applied: held as an option - studies/SBIR only with no hardware capex until FSP downselect/flight dates are contracted (2028 checkpoint); parallel-unit architecture to be validated with >=2 concept teams expressing subcontract interest; terrestrial micro-Brayton fold-down path defined.
ideas.adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (FIX): Clever merchant multi-team position on a program that has already re-baselined once.

ideas.concept: Affordable Ka-band metamaterial electronically-scanned radar tiles: passive/hybrid beam-steering apertures hitting the Army's published $300k-per-unit cost target for test ranges and counter-UAS sensing.
named_buyer_examples: U.S. Army test/training commands (SBIR topic A254-049 sponsor)
counter-UAS system integrators
range-instrumentation providers
ideas.painful_job: The Army's own solicitation says it: Ka-band (30-40 GHz) electronically-scanned radar is unaffordable for test/training use, with a stated $300,000/unit production target that conventional GaAs/GaN AESAs cannot meet (L16-020); meanwhile China's Guangqi demonstrates metamaterial defense hardware is a multi-billion-RMB recurring category (L16-017).
ideas.product: Metasurface aperture tiles (varactor/liquid-crystal-tuned unit cells) that steer a Ka-band beam from a handful of RF chains instead of thousands of T/R modules, packaged as calibrated tiles + beam-control software with open interfaces to third-party radar back-ends.
ideas.frontier_vision: Make phased arrays 10x cheaper per steered watt -- radars on every range pole, drone, and eventually 6G/RIS infrastructure sharing the same tile technology.
ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026: Phase I SBIR under topic A254-049 (opens April 15, 2026, L16-020) via a university-affiliated team. 2027-28: 256-element Ka-band tile with measured steering range, sidelobe and loss budget; Phase II prototype at a range event. 2029: cost-down DFM with a US PCB/LTCC supply chain; draft production unit cost audit against the $300k target. FIX gate (2028): a second named demand anchor - another program office, a prime teaming agreement, or a Phase II award - because one SBIR topic cannot carry the thesis; absent it, kill or fold into a prime-supplier position.
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: Counter-UAS and range-modernization demand keeps compounding into the 2030s while conventional AESA cost floors persist; SBIR Phase III sole-source rights create a protected on-ramp timed to 2030.
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: Army test/training radar procurement following the A254-049 SBIR pipeline (L16-020) and the broader funded counter-UAS/DEW sensing complex (JLWS-era, L12-031) needing cheap Ka-band trackers; KRISS-to-KER transfer shows allied-side metamaterial defense demand (L16-018, KR side).
ideas.competition_outlook_2030: Echodyne (metamaterial ESA incumbent at K/Ku-band -- judgment), conventional AESA primes, Guangqi in the Chinese sphere (L16-017); the Ka-band affordable-tile niche is contested but not closed. P4 requirement: incumbent mapping - Echodyne-class metamaterial ESA vendors and prime GaN AESA cost curves - to confirm the affordability gap still exists at award time.
ideas.timing_window_risk: Metasurface losses and tuning speed at 35 GHz may cap performance below requirement; GaN AESA costs are also falling -- the cost gap could narrow by 2030. KR allied-variant (merged from E-12: the KRISS-to-KER transfer model, L16-018) is upside only - excluded from the base case.
ideas.extreme_edge: A printed sheet of sub-wavelength resonators that bends a millimeter-wave beam like software-defined refraction -- an aperture that is mostly geometry, not silicon.
ideas.first_experiment: 64-element Ka-band metasurface tile: measure beam-steering to +/-45 degrees, insertion loss, and switching latency against the A254-049 draft spec (2027).
ideas.founder_fit_note: Strong transfer: varactor-tuned metasurface unit cells, RF-chain design, aperture calibration, and beam-control software are direct applications of founder electromagnetics, RF circuits, numerical-analysis, and embedded skills.
ideas.fix_applied_notes: 2026-07-13 FIX applied: 2028 second-demand-anchor gate (second program office, prime teaming, or Phase II award); P4 incumbent mapping (Echodyne-class + prime AESA cost curves); E-12's KR allied-variant kept as upside only.
ideas.adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (FIX): Dated Army cost target is real; demand base thin.

ideas.concept: Distributed composite-flywheel kinetic buffers for off-grid AI power plants: containerized magnetic-bearing flywheel strings that absorb GPU-cluster power swings so gas turbines and grids see smooth load.
named_buyer_examples: off-grid AI power-plant developers (the 400 MW East Coast plant that selected Piller SHIELDX)
datacenter EPCs handling GW campuses
gas-turbine power-island integrators
ideas.painful_job: A 400 MW off-grid AI datacenter already selected flywheel-based dynamic power stabilization (Piller SHIELDX with Bergen engines) because reciprocating/turbine plants cannot follow AI load steps (L16-052); GE Vernova books $2.4B/quarter of datacenter electrification orders into this same stress (L08-033); 800VDC racks make sub-second DC-side buffering natural (L02-043).
ideas.product: 250-500 kW / 10-25 MJ composite-rotor flywheel modules on active magnetic bearings, inverter-coupled to the 800VDC/MV bus, deployed as parallel strings at PDU/feeder level with millisecond response and >million-cycle life -- a power-quality machine, not an energy-storage play.
ideas.frontier_vision: Kinetic shock absorbers for the AI grid: every gigawatt campus carries spinning inertia sized to its training-job power spectrum, later extended to grid-forming inertia services.
ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27 (FIX-hardened): instrumented load-spectrum study with a datacenter partner to MEASURE the buffering niche (microcycle depth/frequency spectra) - the niche must be measured, not asserted - plus quantified cycle-life economics vs Piller-class synchronous machines and vs BESS at the measured microcycle rates; rotor/bearing design and loss modeling in parallel. 2027-28: 50 kW / 2 MJ subscale rotor spin-tested in vacuum with magnetic bearings; cycle-life data. 2029: 250 kW prototype string demonstrated against the measured GPU load profile at a power-island testbed.
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: Off-grid and weak-grid AI plants proliferate 2030-2034 as interconnect queues bind; engine/turbine vendors need a buffer partner, and the 2025 SHIELDX selection proves the architecture is procurable now with room for a higher-power-density, DC-native entrant.
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: Off-grid AI power-plant build-outs following the documented 400 MW East Coast SHIELDX/Bergen architecture (L16-052) and the datacenter electrification order supercycle (L08-033); 800VDC rack standardization creating DC-coupled buffer sockets (L02-043, L02-044). CN market (merged from B-18): Dong-Shu-Xi-Suan hub parks and 800VDC rollouts (L02-048, L02-049) are a license-note only - no CN beachhead; the Piller reference architecture is Western.
ideas.competition_outlook_2030: Piller (incumbent, synchronous-machine-based, L16-052), BESS + grid-forming inverters, synchronous condensers at grid scale (L08-036, L08-037), supercapacitors; wedge is cycle-life economics at millions of daily microcycles where batteries age out.
ideas.timing_window_risk: If lithium BESS cycle costs fall far enough or NVIDIA smooths load in software/rack capacitance, the buffering niche shrinks to harmonics-scale.
ideas.extreme_edge: A carbon-fiber cylinder at 40,000 rpm on magnetic bearings in vacuum, catching a 100-megawatt training-run heartbeat so a gas turbine never feels it.
ideas.first_experiment: 50 kW / 2 MJ composite rotor on active magnetic bearings in vacuum: demonstrate 100,000 charge/discharge microcycles with <1% rotor loss growth and millisecond-class response (2028).
ideas.founder_fit_note: Active-magnetic-bearing control, inverter coupling, and millisecond-response electronics fit founder controls, electromagnetics, and circuits skills, while composite-rotor mechanics and containerized multi-megawatt string integration are outside the profile.
ideas.fix_applied_notes: 2026-07-13 FIX applied: 2026-27 instrumented load-spectrum study with a datacenter partner made the sizing basis (niche measured, not asserted); quantified cycle-life economics vs Piller synchronous machines and vs BESS at measured microcycle rates; B-18's CN market absorbed as a license-note only.
ideas.adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (FIX): Fast-follower vs Piller with a real microcycle-aging argument; niche must be measured, not asserted.

ideas.concept: Ultra-high-temperature liquid-metal transport subsystem -- pumps, valves, seals and instrumentation for 1,400-2,400 C molten silicon/tin -- the balance-of-plant layer every TPV thermal battery needs and none can buy.

firebrick/molten-media storage developers (Electrified Thermal-class)
next-gen CSP and process-heat pilots
solar-thermochemical pilot developers (buyer-hypothesis broadening, FIX)
metallurgy/process-heat pilot lines handling molten metals (buyer-hypothesis broadening, FIX)
