ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: Thermal-battery companies move from ARPA-E demos to commercial multi-unit deployments in the early 2030s (Rondo's cement/fuel installs are the leading indicator); at that scale they outsource non-core hot hydraulics exactly as EV firms outsourced pumps -- to whoever spent 2026-2029 accumulating lifetime data.
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: Commercial thermal-battery scale-ups following ARPA-E SCALEUP/FIRES cohorts (L04-033, L04-034), heat-battery purchase precedents at industrial sites (L04-042, L04-043), and 24/7 clean-power procurement pulling storage-plus-heat architectures (Google-Fervo template, L04-050).
ideas.competition_outlook_2030: Vertical in-house builds at Antora/Fourth Power (L04-033, L04-052), graphite component job shops, no merchant qualified supplier; substitutes are solid-media storage architectures (firebrick, L04-034) that avoid liquid metal entirely -- an honest architectural risk.
ideas.timing_window_risk: If solid-media (firebrick/carbon-block conduction) architectures win the market, liquid-metal transport demand never materializes at product scale.
ideas.extreme_edge: Pumping white-hot liquid silicon -- hotter than lava, glowing like a filament -- through ceramic plumbing that must not dissolve for a decade.
ideas.first_experiment: 1,000 h molten-tin pumping loop at 1,400 C with ceramic gear/sump pump: measure seal wear, flow stability and materials attack; publish lifetime model (2027-28).
ideas.founder_fit_note: Low direct fit: 1,400-2,400 C refractory pumps, valves, and seals are extreme-materials mechanical engineering, with founder skills applying only peripherally to level/flow instrumentation in a harsh environment.
ideas.fix_applied_notes: 2026-07-13 FIX applied: 2028 gate requiring >=1 liquid-metal thermal-battery developer with a commercial order book (else kill); buyer hypothesis broadened beyond one architecture to solar-thermochemical and metallurgy/process-heat pilots; first capital capped at molten-tin-loop scale until the gate passes.
ideas.adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (FIX): Highest novelty in pool; demand hostage to liquid-metal architecture winning vs solid media.

ideas.concept: Refrigerant-agnostic two-phase cooling control subsystem: vapor-quality sensor head plus model-predictive pump/expansion controller that makes any CDU or cold-plate loop safe on post-PFAS low-GWP fluids.
named_buyer_examples: Vertiv (liquid-cooling orders +50%, $15B backlog)
Flex/JetCool CDU lines
hyperscalers writing OCP cooling specs (Google Project Deschutes)
ideas.painful_job: Two-phase cooling is the acknowledged path beyond single-phase for >2kW chips, but 3M's PFAS exit removed the qualified fluid family while the AIM Act and EU F-gas rules squeeze HFC alternatives; every OEM faces per-fluid requalification of dryout margins and controls. Evidence: L14-001, L14-002, L14-003, L14-022, L14-029, L14-033, L14-034.
ideas.product: A drop-in control module: capacitive+ultrasonic vapor-quality sensing at cold-plate exit, dryout-margin observer, fluid-property auto-identification, and MPC of pump speed/expansion holding exit quality through 10x heat-flux transients - licensed or sold to CDU/cold-plate OEMs so one hardware SKU covers multiple low-GWP fluids.
ideas.frontier_vision: Become the engine-control-unit of two-phase thermal management across datacenters, power electronics, and megawatt EV charging - the control layer that survives every fluid transition.
ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: academic two-phase rig; publish vapor-quality sensor-fusion accuracy vs gamma-densitometry reference. 2027-28: qualify control across three candidate low-GWP fluids; patents on sensing+observer; present at OCP cooling workstream. 2028-29: joint validation slots at NTT Facilities' DC Cooling Hub (JP) and with a Taiwan cold-plate maker in ITRI's two-phase supply chain. HARD GATE (2028, FIX): two OEM development agreements proving buy-vs-build appetite, secured as a consultant/licensor, not an operating company; if OEMs balk, merge this control layer into the B-01/C-04 negative-pressure loop stack instead of continuing standalone.
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: 2030 is when AIM Act step-downs (30% of baseline from 2029) and >2kW/chip accelerators intersect: two-phase becomes mandatory in new bids exactly as fluid fragmentation peaks, making fluid-agnostic control a purchasable de-risking layer.
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: Named: EPA AIM Act 2029-2033 step-down to 30% of HFC baseline (L14-033) colliding with post-Novec fluid re-qualification (L14-022) during hyperscaler two-phase procurement cycles; ITRI-class 2.4kW/chip cold plates entering ODM volume (L14-053).
ideas.competition_outlook_2030: ZutaCore and Accelsius-class vertically integrated two-phase vendors (single proprietary fluid each), Vertiv/Schneider internal controls, Taiwanese ODM in-house solutions; no merchant fluid-agnostic control/metrology vendor identified in 2026 (L14-046, L14-051, L14-045).
ideas.timing_window_risk: If single-phase water + microchannels stretches to 5kW/chip, or one Novec successor fluid wins outright, fluid-agnostic control loses its premium; incumbents could acquire equivalent sensing startups. The two-phase shipment-share kill trigger (<5% of liquid-cooling shipments by 2033) is binding (FIX).
ideas.commercial_readiness_kill_date: 2033-12 if two-phase remains <5% of liquid-cooling shipments or no OEM license by 2033
ideas.extreme_edge: Real-time vapor-quality servo control validated across multiple fluids - metrology IP that turns a chemistry problem into a controls product.
ideas.first_experiment: 2027: two-channel pumped two-phase rig; demonstrate dryout-margin control across three low-GWP fluids under 10x heat-flux steps with vapor-quality estimation error <5% vs reference instrumentation.
secondary_markets: TW: ITRI low-pressure-refrigerant cold-plate supply chain and ODM cold-plate/CDU makers as co-development and manufacturing wedge (L14-053)
KR: GS Caltex Kixx DLC/immersion fluid lines with named customers Samsung SDS and LG U+ as fluid-qualification partners and pilot sites (L14-049)
JP: NTT Facilities DC Cooling Hub 780kW verification facility as neutral validation venue (L14-050)
ideas.founder_fit_note: Strong transfer: capacitive/ultrasonic vapor-quality sensing, dryout-margin observers, and model-predictive pump/expansion control are founder sensors, controls, embedded, and numerical-analysis work, with two-phase fluid modeling as the main outside dependency.
ideas.fix_applied_notes: 2026-07-13 FIX applied: two OEM development agreements made a hard 2028 gate proving buy-vs-build appetite, with a pre-agreed fallback merge into the B-01/C-04 loop stack as its control layer; the two-phase shipment-share kill trigger made binding.
ideas.adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (FIX): Original control-layer abstraction ('ECU of two-phase') with strong JP/TW/KR side-market plan; company-vs-feature is the open question.

ideas.concept: High-density cryogenic interconnect loader with co-packaged 4K readout front-end (superconducting flex harness + cryo-CMOS multiplexing + cryo-LNA), breaking the 256-channel-per-loader wall for quantum computers.
named_buyer_examples: DOE QIS centers ($625M program: Brookhaven C2QA, Fermilab SQMS, Argonne Q-NEXT, LBNL QSA, ORNL QSC)
Fermilab-Qblox QICK supply chain
US superconducting-qubit companies; Bluefors Syracuse as OEM channel
ideas.painful_job: Cryogenic wiring density is an acknowledged scaling bottleneck (256 channels/loader today vs thousands needed) and cryo-electronics power budgets collide with milliwatt-class dilution-refrigerator cooling capacity; the harness+cold-electronics integration point is owned by no vendor. Evidence: L13-042, L13-004, L13-005, L13-022, L13-023, L07-014, L07-015, L07-016, L07-017.
ideas.product: A fridge-insert loader delivering 2,000+ channels via superconducting/low-thermal-conductance flex laminates with integrated 4K 64:1 cryo-CMOS multiplexers and GaAs/SiGe LNAs, engineered against the fridge's stage-by-stage heat budget and sold as a qualified subsystem. Merged framing (from A-06): sold on channels-per-watt economics (qualified heat load per channel), not per-cable pricing.
ideas.frontier_vision: Become the backplane of the quantum datacenter, then extend to big-science detector readout and cryogenic sensing fleets.
ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: university cryolab work on flex-laminate heat-load/crosstalk trade-offs; tape out first 4K mux via MPW shuttle. 2027-28: 256-channel segment demo; publish; patents on harness+mux co-packaging. 2028-29: pilot integration with a DOE QIS center testbed and a fridge OEM; use SEEQC-ITRI-style Taiwan manufacturing lines to price a scalable supply chain; secure LOIs before incorporating in 2030.
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: Vendor roadmaps put multi-thousand-qubit machines in 2029-2032; the only public density roadmap (a single vendor's unvalidated 4x/2yr claim) leaves an open lane for a validated alternative arriving exactly at the scaling wall.
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: Named: DOE QIS-center funding cycle renewals beyond the $625M tranche (L13-028) and first >5,000-physical-qubit superconducting systems requiring order-of-magnitude interconnect density gains (L13-042), with the Fermilab-Qblox QICK industrialization (L13-032) creating a US supply chain hungry for qualified cold hardware.
ideas.competition_outlook_2030: Delft Circuits (cabling), Bluefors integrated wiring options, in-house solutions at IBM/Google-class labs, Keysight/Quantum Machines/Zurich at room temperature only (L13-037 shows the 1,000-qubit control race is already commercial).
ideas.timing_window_risk: On-chip multiplexing (1000:1) or optical cryo-links could leapfrog external harness density; quantum capex is cyclical and government-dependent. Substitution risks kept on record (from D-17): on-chip 1000:1 multiplexing, SFQ readout, cryo-CMOS moving control fully cold, and optical cryo-links could each leapfrog external harness density; roadmap-validation angle - the only public density roadmap is a single vendor's unvalidated claim, so independent validated density data is itself a wedge.
ideas.commercial_readiness_kill_date: 2034-06 if no fridge-OEM or QIS-center integration contract by 2033
ideas.extreme_edge: Co-engineered harness + cold electronics against a hard physics budget (fridge cooling power) - a systems integration moat, not a cable catalog.
ideas.first_experiment: 2027: 256-channel flex segment with 4K 64:1 mux; demonstrate <1% crosstalk, per-channel heat load <20% of coax equivalent, and readout-fidelity parity on test resonators.
secondary_markets: TW: SEEQC-ITRI superconducting-control-chip manufacturing line as foundry/packaging wedge for the cold mux/SFQ parts (L13-038)
JP: AIST G-QuAT 1,000+-qubit control deployment as reference market and early-adopter testbed (L13-037)
KR: KRISS 1,000-qubit-by-2032 roadmap as an emergent buyer (L13-040)
ideas.founder_fit_note: Strong transfer on the cryo-CMOS multiplexing, LNA readout chain, and channels-per-watt instrumentation engineering, which sit in founder circuits/RF and instrumentation skills; cryogenic materials and flex-laminate fabrication are outside the profile.
ideas.merge_import_notes: 2026-07-13: absorbed A-06 (channels-per-watt sales framing) and D-17 (SFQ/cryo-CMOS/optical substitution-risk framing, roadmap-validation angle).
ideas.adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (PROMOTE): Canonical cryo I/O: flex + 4K mux + LNA against fridge heat budget, TW manufacturing wedge (SEEQC-ITRI), JP/KR reference markets. Keep D-17's substitution risks (SFQ, cryo-CMOS, optical) on record.

ideas.concept: CPO thermal-stabilization module: engineered package lid with embedded two-phase micro-loop plus distributed micro-TEC trim and co-designed TIM stack, holding laser/ring wavelength stability on co-packaged-optics switch ASICs.
named_buyer_examples: Broadcom-class switch-ASIC vendors
Coherent (targeting a $4B datacenter-interconnect transceiver opportunity: L12-035, L12-036)
hyperscalers via OCP cooling specs (L14-043)
ideas.painful_job: CPO concentrates optical power on the switch-ASIC package and no thermal architecture has converged (four independent 2024-26 approaches); lasers/rings need tight temperature control that rack-level CDUs cannot deliver, and TIM stacks for chiplet-scale hotspots remain a live materials bottleneck. Evidence: L12-021, L12-022, L12-023, L12-024, L14-014, L14-015, L14-016, L14-018.
ideas.product: A package-level thermal subsystem: lid-integrated passive two-phase spreader tuned for low-pressure fluids, micro-TEC islands for +/-0.5 degC laser-site regulation, and a validated TIM/bond-line stack - sold to switch vendors and OSATs as a qualified BOM item.
ideas.frontier_vision: Own the thermal interface between photonics and silicon across CPO switches, optical I/O chiplets, and eventually optically-connected accelerators.
ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: university packaging-lab testbed emulating switch-ASIC + 8 laser sites; publish comparative results against the four literature architectures. 2027-28: build lid prototype; patents on micro-loop/TEC-island layout. 2028-29: co-development agreement with one OSAT/ODM in Taiwan's cold-plate/packaging chain and evaluation with one US switch vendor; LOIs before 2030. FIX gate (2028): documented CPO volume-ramp evidence (switch-vendor roadmap commitments/orders) PLUS one switch-vendor or OSAT co-development agreement; absent either, hold or kill.
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: CPO moves from first deployments to volume in the 3.2T/6.4T optics generations around 2030-2033; package-thermal qualification becomes the gating item as ASIC power and laser counts double per generation.
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: Named: Coherent-class datacenter-interconnect roadmaps ramping CPO/next-gen transceivers into the $4B opportunity they have publicly targeted (L12-036), and OCP-driven hyperscaler cooling-spec cycles extending to package level (L14-043).
ideas.competition_outlook_2030: OSAT in-house passive lids, cold-plate vendors extending down (Flex/JetCool: L14-045), ZutaCore-class two-phase players moving to package scale (L14-046), and switch vendors' internal packaging teams - plus Coherent as both buyer and potential competitor.
ideas.timing_window_risk: Linear-drive pluggables (LPO) or ring-less designs could defer CPO volume past 2034; package thermal could be absorbed entirely by OSAT roadmaps. Explicit LPO-substitution monitor (FIX): track linear-drive pluggable share each optics generation; sustained LPO wins defer the thesis. Defensive IP plan required for the Coherent-as-buyer-and-competitor problem (lid/micro-loop/TEC-island layout patents held offensively-defensively).
ideas.commercial_readiness_kill_date: 2033-12 if CPO volume ramp has not started or no vendor co-development by 2033
ideas.extreme_edge: Wavelength-stability-grade thermal control (+/-0.5 degC at laser sites under 30% ASIC power swing) - a photonics spec, not a generic cooling spec.
ideas.first_experiment: 2027: thermal testbed emulating a CPO switch package (ASIC + 8 laser sites); demonstrate +/-0.5 degC laser-site regulation under a 30% ASIC power transient at <3% cooling-power overhead.
secondary_markets: TW: OSAT/ODM packaging supply chain plus ITRI's two-phase cold-plate ecosystem as co-development and manufacturing wedge - CPO assembly physically happens in Taiwan (L14-053)
ideas.founder_fit_note: Micro-TEC regulation electronics and sub-degree control loops fit founder controls and circuits skills, but the lid-integrated two-phase spreader, TIM stack, and package-materials qualification are outside the profile.
ideas.fix_applied_notes: 2026-07-13 FIX applied: 2028 gate on CPO volume-ramp evidence plus one switch-vendor/OSAT co-development agreement; explicit LPO-substitution monitor; defensive IP plan for Coherent as simultaneous buyer and competitor.
ideas.adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (FIX): Real photonics-grade thermal spec nobody sells; narrow window on both sides (CPO slip or OSAT absorption).

ideas.concept: Multi-terminal DC fault-discrimination protection relay plus vendor-neutral hardware-in-the-loop qualification platform for MVDC datacenter distribution and HVDC converter stations - the 'SEL of DC grids.'
named_buyer_examples: Pattern Energy-class US HVDC developers (Southern Spirit, $2.6B private + $360M DOE: L08-041)
GE Vernova-class converter vendors (~$10B HVDC backlog + $2.4B/qtr datacenter orders: L08-033)
US TSOs under NERC PRC-029-1 compliance (L08-043)
ideas.painful_job: DC faults must clear in single-digit milliseconds without zero-crossings; discrimination algorithms (traveling-wave, non-unit) are still being re-derived paper-by-paper, DC breakers remain non-converged, and there is no merchant DC protection relay or neutral qualification suite - so meshed DC grids cannot be built with confidence. Evidence: L08-004, L08-005, L08-006, L08-007, L08-017, L08-019, L08-020, L08-021.
ideas.product: A DC protection relay implementing benchmarked traveling-wave/non-unit discrimination with <1ms decision latency, plus an open RTDS/HIL test suite that qualifies any vendor's breaker/relay/converter combination - sold to developers, converter vendors, and (as compliance tooling) to TSOs.
ideas.frontier_vision: Become the independent protection-and-qualification authority for the DC century: datacenter MVDC, offshore multi-terminal HVDC, and DC industrial parks.
ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: graduate work benchmarking published discrimination algorithms on a 4-terminal RTDS model; release open benchmark. 2027-28: relay prototype hitting <1ms decisions; publish; patents on discrimination/selectivity. 2028-29: HIL studies sold to one converter vendor and one Korean HVDC-localization supplier as engagements; LOIs; incorporate 2030.
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: US interregional HVDC construction (Southern Spirit starts 2029) and first meshed offshore/datacenter MVDC designs hit protection-qualification needs exactly in 2030-2033, when no incumbent offers vendor-neutral DC relaying.
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: Named: Southern Spirit Transmission construction (2029) toward 2032 in-service (L08-041), the continuing GE Vernova-class HVDC/datacenter electrification order wave (L08-033), and KEPCO's West Coast HVDC procurement/localization cycle as the side-market mirror (L08-038, L08-039). CN chapter (from C-16, license-only): Chinese converter-valve/protection suppliers (XJ Electric-class) need factory-acceptance-test infrastructure and State Grid test institutes buy HIL benches - State Grid UHV/DC tender cadence documented at RMB1.275bn first-batch scale (L08-034); served via licensed test-suite packages only, no CN entity or beachhead (flags unchanged).
ideas.competition_outlook_2030: Hitachi Energy/Siemens/GE in-house protection integrated with their converters (L08-052), SEL-class AC-relay incumbents extending to DC, academic tools; the neutral-qualification niche is empty in 2026. Scope coordination (adjudication): E-14 owns the relay + qualification layer; P3R2-A-02 owns breaker hardware; P3R2-A-03 (if its 2027 gate passes) is the field MW-emulation complement.
ideas.timing_window_risk: If US/offshore DC stays point-to-point through 2034, fast discrimination demand defers; converter vendors may refuse third-party protection in their stations.
ideas.commercial_readiness_kill_date: 2034-12 if no multi-terminal project or vendor engagement by 2033
ideas.extreme_edge: An open, published DC-protection benchmark that becomes the de facto acceptance standard - the startup owns the test everyone must pass.
ideas.first_experiment: 2027: RTDS/HIL benchmark of three published DC fault-discrimination algorithms on a 4-terminal MVDC model plus a relay prototype demonstrating <1ms trip decisions; publish as an open benchmark.
secondary_markets: KR: KEPCO 2GW West Coast HVDC 'Energy Expressway' with 2026+ tenders and a 2027 national HVDC-equipment localization target - Korean suppliers (LS Electric/Hyosung-class) need a neutral protection/qualification partner (L08-038, L08-039)
ideas.founder_fit_note: Strong transfer: traveling-wave fault-discrimination algorithms, sub-millisecond relay firmware, and hardware-in-the-loop qualification suites are founder signal-processing, embedded-systems, controls, and numerical-analysis territory.
ideas.merge_import_notes: 2026-07-13: absorbed C-16 (CN factory-acceptance-test license market + State Grid test-institute channel, L08-034) as a license-only chapter - E-14 is not on the adjudicator's endorsed licensed-CN-leg list for flag purposes, so primary_market stays US and china_beachhead stays false. Scope coordinated with A-02 and A-03.
ideas.adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (PROMOTE): Canonical DC protection relay + vendor-neutral qualification ('SEL of DC grids'); open-benchmark strategy converts research chaos into an ownable acceptance standard; dated US anchors + KR localization side market. Absorb C-16 CN license angle; coordinate scope with A-02 and A-03.

ideas.concept: Solid-state microsecond impedance-matching engines (switched SiC/PIN capacitor arrays replacing motorized vacuum-capacitor matchboxes) for pulsed and tailored-waveform RF plasma tools
named_buyer_examples: Lam/AMAT-ecosystem RF integrators via Advanced Energy/MKS-class generator vendors (US)
AMEC
NAURA
Piotech (via Hengyunchang/Injet supply chain, mature-node scope)
ideas.painful_job: Pulsed and tailored-voltage-waveform plasma processes change impedance faster than motorized vacuum-capacitor matching networks can follow; matching remains an active research problem (L06-001/002/003, L01-012/013) while CN RF-power component localization sits under ~12% (L06-054).
ideas.product: Drop-in solid-state matching network family (400 kHz-60 MHz, 1-10 kW initially): switched capacitor/inductor arrays with sub-10-microsecond re-match, pulse-synchronous tuning tables, and health telemetry; sold to RF-generator vendors and tool OEMs; CN line scoped to >=28 nm/panel/display tools. CN chapter delivered as licensing-with-golden-reference (absorbed from F-14): the CN licensee manufactures under milestone-based contracts while the founder retains golden-reference subassemblies, calibration methodology, and factory test benches - certification power stays with the licensor (HK-structured IP entity).
ideas.frontier_vision: The impedance-delivery layer for all fast plasma processing: every pulsed plasma tool, leading-edge or mature-node, tunes in microseconds instead of hundreds of milliseconds, unlocking waveform-level process control as a standard capability.
ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27 university-lab prototype (13.56 MHz, 5 kW) and switching-loss/linearity characterization; 2027 export-control counsel gate on CN scope; 2028 two paid evaluations with a generator vendor or tool OEM; 2029 reliability data (1e9 switch events) and design-freeze of a productizable module. CN-chapter licensing mechanics (from F-14): HK-structured IP entity and milestone-based license contracts scoped 2028, know-how held in calibration/test assets.
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: Pulsed/TVW process adoption broadens through 2030 while incumbent matchboxes remain electromechanical; solid-state matching is demonstrated in literature but not productized as a merchant module, leaving a 2030-2034 design-in window before AE/MKS/Comet fully absorb it.
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: US: next tool-generation qualification cycles at leading-edge fabs (AE Everest/EVOS-class platform ramps per L06-039/040) requiring pulse-synchronous match; CN: 15th/16th Five-Year-Plan semiconductor-equipment localization programs and SMIC/YMTC mature-node expansions pulling domestic RF component content above today's <12% (L06-051/052/054).
ideas.competition_outlook_2030: Comet (Synertia platform), Advanced Energy and MKS in-house matches, DAIHEN; CN: Hengyunchang/Injet moving up-stack. Domestic-substitution speed in CN is fast at generator level but slow at high-end match-component level.
ideas.timing_window_risk: If AE/MKS ship integrated solid-state matching as a standard feature before a merchant module gains a design win, the socket closes; mitigated by selling to the same incumbents and to CN OEMs the US incumbents cannot serve.
ideas.commercial_readiness_kill_date: 2034 (kill if no OEM design-in or paid evaluation converts to a production order by end-2033)
ideas.extreme_edge: Pulse-by-pulse impedance trajectory control co-optimized with bias waveform, enabling ion-energy control on tools that never get an IEDF sensor.
ideas.first_experiment: Build 13.56 MHz/5 kW switched-capacitor matcher; demonstrate <10 us re-match across a pulsed plasma load recipe versus >100 ms motorized baseline, with reflected-power and lifetime data (2027).
ideas.founder_fit_note: Strong transfer: switched-capacitor RF matching arrays, sub-10-microsecond re-tune control, pulse-synchronous tuning tables, and health telemetry map directly onto founder RF circuits, controls, and embedded skills.
ideas.merge_import_notes: 2026-07-13 R2: absorbed P3R2-F-14 (REJECT) as the CN chapter's IP mechanics - licensing-with-golden-reference structure (founder retains golden-reference subassemblies, calibration methodology, and factory test benches; milestone-based contracts via an HK-structured IP entity) and the Hengyunchang/Injet buyer evidence (L06-042/043/044, already cited). F-14's standalone license-into-China model was rejected on the B-12 precedent; imported here only as mechanics subordinate to F-01's hardware business.
ideas.adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 R2 adjudication (PROMOTE, N6/E7/C7/V7/T7): strongest new dual seed; genuinely non-obvious by the convergence test (matching documented unresolved in atlas, absent from all 100 prior seeds); clean complement to A-10 (impedance delivery vs process control); CN leg eligible and country-specific (<12% localization L06-054, named buyers, mature-node scope, 2027 export gate). Absorb F-14's licensing-with-golden-reference mechanics as CN-chapter IP structure. Comet Synertia absorption risk covered by kill trigger; 60 MHz voltage/Q parity is the honest key uncertainty.

ideas.concept: Superconducting-magnet electrical balance-of-plant platform: standardized precision drive-and-dump skids (10 ppm-class high-current DC converters + energy-extraction/dump units + current leads + busbars)
named_buyer_examples: Commonwealth Fusion Systems magnet customers (Realta Fusion, UW WHAM)
DOE milestone-program fusion companies
ASIPP (PF converter control modules / RMP power-supply tenders)
ideas.painful_job: Every merchant HTS magnet sale (L03-035) and every fusion/big-science coil (L01-037/038) needs a bespoke high-stability drive converter, dump circuit, current leads, and busbars engineered per project; there is no standardized, catalog drive-and-dump product line, so magnet buyers re-engineer the electrical island each time.
ideas.product: Modular skids: 1-50 kA precision DC converters (ppm-class stability), integrated energy-extraction/dump units with magnet-aware sequencing, HTS/binary current-lead assemblies, and water-cooled bus systems, with a documented acceptance-test protocol; interfaces cleanly with third-party quench-detection (D-01-class) systems. Entry SKU (absorbed from F-21): catalog binary current leads (0.5-20 kA classes) and vacuum-tight instrumented feedthroughs with published, certified heat-load and quench-tolerance datasheets.
ideas.frontier_vision: The 'power island in a box' for the coming fleet of commercial superconducting magnets - fusion pilot plants, research magnets, industrial field coils - cutting magnet-system integration time from years to months.
ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27 architecture study + IEC/IEEE acceptance-protocol drafting with a university magnet lab; 2027 build 25 kA/10 ppm converter + dump prototype on a lab HTS coil; 2028 LOI from one merchant-magnet buyer (Realta/WHAM-class) and one lab; 2029 pre-qualify supply chain for leads/busbars; export-control and CN-access counsel gate in 2027. Entry-SKU track (from F-21): 5 kA instrumented binary-lead prototype with a university magnet group 2027-28; certified heat-load datasheet methodology and first lab purchase orders 2029.
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: SPARC-era results convert fusion magnet demand from R&D to serial builds after 2030; merchant magnet sales already exist (L03-035) and CN institute tenders recur (L01-037/038); no standardized supplier exists between bespoke lab builds and one-off vendor projects.
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: US: DOE Milestone-Based Fusion Development Program reauthorization decision at FY2027 boundary (L03-032) and post-SPARC pilot-plant magnet procurements; CN: ASIPP/CAS big-science power-supply tender cadence under the 15th Five-Year-Plan large-facility program (ITER PF/RMP pattern, L01-037/038), served via international big-science procurement routes.
ideas.competition_outlook_2030: OCEM/Danfysik-class magnet-PSU specialists - precision magnet converters are catalog items, so the white space is the integrated extraction/dump + leads + acceptance-protocol island, not converter novelty (P4 incumbency map is a pre-promotion requirement); converter groups inside national labs, CN institute in-house builds; CFS vertical integration/bundling remains the named ceiling on the US side (monitor).
ideas.timing_window_risk: Fusion schedule slip pushes serial magnet builds past 2034; hedge is research/industrial magnet and accelerator PSU demand that exists regardless of fusion outcomes.
ideas.commercial_readiness_kill_date: 2034 (kill if by end-2033 there is no paid drive-and-dump order from a merchant magnet buyer or lab; entry-SKU gate: >=$1M cumulative lead/feedthrough orders by end-2031, else the entry line folds to catalog-support only)
ideas.extreme_edge: Magnet-aware dump sequencing co-designed with fiber-based quench detection vendors, sold as a certified pair.
