ideas.concept: High-speed permanent-magnet turbo-generator + power-converter 'electrical cartridge' (0.3-20 MW) for waste-heat sCO2 and geothermal/ORC power blocks
named_buyer_examples: ORC/EGS turbine integrators supplying Fervo-class buyers (US)
sCO2 machine builders in the CNNC 'Chaotan One' retrofit ecosystem (CN, via licensed component supply)
Hanwha-class TEG integrators (KR side market)
ideas.painful_job: Turbine supply is the bottleneck as EGS scales (1.75 GW Fervo-Turboden order, L04-044/115) and China's first commercial sCO2 waste-heat unit opens a steel-sintering retrofit wave (L04-048/051); every machine builder engineers its own high-speed generator + converter + bearing-drive stack, and Sandia names rotor/bearing integration among unresolved sCO2 hardware challenges (L04-113).
ideas.product: A standardized electrical cartridge: high-speed PM generator (10-40 krpm classes), active front-end converter with grid-code compliance package, bearing-drive electronics, and thermal management, sold to turbomachinery integrators as a catalog item with defined shaft interfaces.
ideas.frontier_vision: Commoditize the electrical half of small turbomachinery so any credible mechanical team can field a waste-heat or geothermal power block; the same cartridge family serves sCO2, ORC, air-cycle, and compressed-air-storage machines.
ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27 requirements interviews with 5+ machine builders (US/EU/KR) - the $900k drive-stand experiment is staged behind these findings (no build until interviews confirm the cartridge specification); 2027-28 build 250 kW/30 krpm generator + AFE prototype on a drive rig with representative load transients; 2029 one co-development agreement with an ORC or sCO2 integrator - BINDING GATE; CN plant-integration/licensing chapter (absorbed from F-18) scoped with counsel in 2028: EPC co-bidding memorandum and electrical-island package v1 aligned to Xinjiang first-set completion (~2028, L04-051).
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: Fervo-Turboden deliveries run 2026-2029 and expose ORC electrical-supply constraints; CNNC's Xinjiang first-set project completes ~2028 and 'first-set' designation historically unlocks CN follow-on procurement, putting serial retrofit orders in the 2030-2034 window.
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: US: post-2029 EGS expansion rounds beyond Cape Station (debt-financed procurement precedent L04-115) and NRC Part 53-era advanced-reactor BOP orders (L04-045 pattern); CN: post-Xinjiang 'first (set) major technical equipment' follow-on sintering retrofits across the steel sector (L04-051), served via licensed manufacturing. CN retrofit TAM is single-source (L04-051): triangulation from eligible sources is a pre-scale gate - no scale assumption before it.
ideas.competition_outlook_2030: Calnetix-class merchant high-speed PM+converter specialists sell exactly this pairing for ORC - P4 incumbent map centered on Calnetix-class and turbine-OEM in-house (Turboden/Hanwha, L04-047/055) is a PRE-PROMOTION requirement the white-space claim must survive; CN institute-affiliated suppliers (CAS-IET/SMDERI ecosystem, L04-102/106).
ideas.timing_window_risk: If sCO2 retrofit demand stays CNNC-internal and ORC incumbents keep generators in-house, the merchant socket is small; mitigated by multi-cycle applicability and the documented turbine capacity shortage.
ideas.commercial_readiness_kill_date: 2034 (kill if no machine-builder design win by end-2033; BINDING GATE: signed machine-builder co-development agreement by end-2029)
ideas.extreme_edge: Cartridge-level co-optimization of PM rotor, converter, and magnetic-bearing control as one certified rotordynamic unit.
ideas.first_experiment: 250 kW, 30 krpm PM generator + active-front-end converter on a drive-stand emulating sCO2/ORC load transients, demonstrating grid-code-compliant ride-through and 30-minute hot restart (2028).
ideas.founder_fit_note: Active front-end converter control, bearing-drive electronics, and grid-code compliance testing fit founder circuits and controls skills, while high-speed permanent-magnet machine design and turbomachinery integration are outside the profile.
ideas.merge_import_notes: 2026-07-13 R2: absorbed P3R2-F-18 (MERGED_INTO_P3R2-F-03) as the CN plant-integration/licensing chapter - EPC co-bidding memorandum concept, Xinjiang first-set timeline alignment (~2028 completion), rollout-industrialization framing. No new source IDs required (F-18's L04-048/051 and L04-102/106/111/113 already cited here).
ideas.fix_applied_notes: 2026-07-13 R2 FIX applied: (1) P4 incumbent map (Calnetix-class merchant high-speed PM+converter + turbine-OEM in-house) made a pre-promotion requirement; (2) 2029 machine-builder co-development agreement made a binding gate; (3) single-source CN retrofit TAM (L04-051) given a pre-scale triangulation gate; (4) F-18 absorbed as the CN plant-integration/licensing chapter; (5) $900k drive-stand experiment staged behind the 2026-27 requirements-interview findings.
ideas.adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 R2 adjudication (FIX, N5/E6/C5/V7/T6): fills a real in-pool gap (electrical half of heat-to-power machines) with dual dated anchors (Fervo-Turboden; Chaotan One + first-set); merchant white-space claim must survive Calnetix-class incumbency; CN TAM single-source.

ideas.concept: Merchant active-magnetic-bearing (AMB) drive electronics + rotor-health platform, entering via semiconductor turbomolecular pumps and expanding to sCO2 compressors, blowers, and flywheels
named_buyer_examples: Turbopump and vacuum OEMs (MKS/Ebara-class)
Chinese tool-OEM supply chains (AMEC/NAURA ecosystem)
sCO2/flywheel machine developers
ideas.painful_job: Magnetic-bearing reliability and shock-induced rotor instability remain active engineering problems after 30+ years (L07-010/011/012/013); machine developers either buy from a thin merchant AMB base or build controllers in-house; Sandia lists bearings among unresolved sCO2 challenges (L04-113).
ideas.product: A standardized 5-axis AMB controller family: position sensing, adaptive unbalance/shock control, touchdown-bearing life management, and fleet health telemetry, sold to rotating-machine OEMs with a qualification dossier for semiconductor-grade duty.
ideas.frontier_vision: Bearing electronics as a platform: one certified controller line spanning vacuum pumps, high-speed compressors, expanders, and flywheels, with a cross-fleet rotor-health dataset no single OEM can match.
ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27 retrofit a commercial maglev turbopump rotor with an open controller; publish shock-ride-through and touchdown-life results; 2027 teardown of SKF S2M/Calnetix/Waukesha merchant controllers against the semiconductor-grade duty claim (P4 requirement - the wedge must survive it); 2028 paid evaluation with one pump or compressor OEM - BINDING GATE: a paying OEM is the proof that pump OEMs will externalize AMB electronics (the thesis's stated key uncertainty); 2029 second vertical (sCO2 rig or flywheel) demo.
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: Fab expansion and big-science vacuum demand keep turbopump fleets growing (L07-050/052), CN semiconductor vacuum demand is re-accelerating (L07-053), and new high-speed machine categories (sCO2, flywheels) are reaching hardware in 2030-2034 without an obvious merchant AMB electronics leader for harsh/semiconductor duty.
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: US: STEP-lineage sCO2 hardware scale-up and flywheel power-stabilization deployments (Piller precedent L16-052); CN: continued fab-tool localization pulling domestic pump/subsystem content (AMEC/NAURA growth L06-048) and CAS-IET turbomachinery programs (L04-106/109), served via component design-in. CN leg CONDITIONAL: a named CN pump/tool-OEM design-in channel must be evidenced in P4 or primary_market downgrades to US (INFICON APAC revenue attribution, L07-053, is not buyer evidence).
ideas.competition_outlook_2030: SKF Magnetic Mechatronics (S2M), Calnetix, Waukesha Magnetic Bearings (judgment names; P4 to source); CN: domestic maglev blower/pump makers scaling fast - domestic substitution speed is the main CN risk.
ideas.timing_window_risk: Incumbent merchant AMB vendors are established; the wedge (semiconductor-grade shock recovery + fleet telemetry) must prove decisive by 2030 or the play collapses into a niche retrofit business.
ideas.commercial_readiness_kill_date: 2034 (kill if no OEM design win by end-2032 given incumbent maturity)
ideas.extreme_edge: Self-commissioning AMB control that identifies rotor dynamics in situ, eliminating per-machine tuning.
ideas.first_experiment: Retrofit AMB controller on a commercial turbopump; demonstrate shock-event ride-through (per L07-012 scenario) plus predictive touchdown-bearing life estimation validated against teardown (2027).
ideas.founder_fit_note: Strong transfer: five-axis magnetic-bearing position sensing, adaptive control, drive electronics, and fleet health telemetry form a controls/electromagnetics/embedded product squarely within the founder profile, with rotordynamics modeling supported by numerical-analysis skills.
ideas.fix_applied_notes: 2026-07-13 R2 FIX applied: 2028 paid OEM evaluation made the binding proof that pump OEMs will externalize AMB electronics; 2027 teardown of SKF S2M/Calnetix/Waukesha merchant controllers vs the semiconductor-grade duty claim scoped as a P4 requirement; CN leg made conditional (named CN pump/tool-OEM design-in channel in P4 or primary_market downgrades to US - INFICON APAC revenue attribution is not buyer evidence); 2032 internal kill retained.
ideas.adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 R2 adjudication (FIX, N5/E6/C6/V6/T6): real 30-year documented reliability gap (L07-010..013); thesis lives or dies on OEM externalization; thinnest CN leg among surviving dual seeds - counted dual-conditional in quota.

ideas.concept: Certified battery-electric traction repower kits (pack power conversion + traction inverter + charge interface) for port yard equipment and mining support fleets, including export-certification packages for Chinese OEM platforms
named_buyer_examples: US port operators/terminal fleets funded by EPA Clean Ports grants
repower integrators serving RTG/yard-tractor fleets
Chinese heavy-equipment OEM export programs (XCMG-Fortescue pattern)
ideas.painful_job: US: nearly $3B of EPA Clean Ports awards must convert diesel yard equipment to zero-emission within 3-4-year windows (L10-048), and repowering existing fleets is the cheapest path, but off-highway repower electrics remain per-project engineering (the stronger 'no standardized heavy-duty repower kit exists' claim is downgraded to judgment pending the P4 competitive teardown). CN: Chinese OEMs winning Western fleet orders (XCMG-Fortescue, L10-028) must certify power electronics to IEC/UL/MCS regimes their domestic supply chain was not built for.
ideas.product: A repower kit family: 650-1000 V battery power conversion, harsh-duty traction inverters, vehicle control unit, and dual-personality charge interface (CCS/MCS and GB/T-swap-compatible variants), certified once and reused across equipment classes; sold to repower integrators and OEM export programs, not to end fleets directly. The dual-standard interface personality is bought as a module (B-14-class), not rebuilt - the defensible core is the UL/IEC/GB certification matrix plus the dual-personality interface, not kit existence.
ideas.frontier_vision: The Cummins-of-electrification for off-highway repowers: a certified electrics kit that turns any diesel yard or mine support vehicle into an electric one, with the certification dossier as the moat.
ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27 map EPA-grant fleet composition and pick two beachhead vehicle classes; 2028 one grant-funded pilot repower with a terminal operator plus one CN OEM export-certification engagement LOI (BINDING GATE) and an unsubsidized repower economics model (EPA grants mostly fund new equipment - the repower inference must be evidenced, not assumed); 2029 UL/IEC certification of kit v1; monitor CARB-repeal fallout (L10-044 pattern) as a demand-quality gate.
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: EPA implementation windows run through 2028-29, proving the repower category; the 2030-2034 window is the second, larger wave (non-grant economics plus CN OEM export programs delivering 2028-2030 per L10-028) before vertically integrated OEM electrics close the socket.
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: US: post-grant procurement cycles at ports (equipment mid-life repowers) and MCS-standard chargers arriving at scale (IEC TS 63379, L10-050); CN: XCMG/CATL-ecosystem export deliveries 2028-2030 requiring Western-certified electronics (L10-028), plus GB/T swap-standard equipment lines (L10-046).
ideas.competition_outlook_2030: Dana TM4, BAE Systems, Danfoss Editron, ZF - P4 competitive teardown REQUIRED against these off-highway electrics lines, with the defensible core restated as the certification matrix + dual-personality interface; CN OEM in-house electrics (XCMG, CATL) - domestic substitution is fast, so the CN leg must stay certification-led, not cost-led.
ideas.timing_window_risk: Regulatory reversal (CARB repeal precedent, L10-044) can soften US demand; OEM-integrated electrics can absorb the category by mid-2030s. The kit must win on certification burden and fleet heterogeneity.
ideas.commercial_readiness_kill_date: 2034 (kill if <2 paid repower programs or no CN OEM certification engagement by end-2032)
ideas.extreme_edge: One power stack certified across three regulatory regimes (UL/IEC/GB) - the certification matrix itself becomes the product.
ideas.first_experiment: Repower one yard tractor/RTG-class vehicle with kit v0 under a port pilot; demonstrate duty-cycle equivalence, thermal margins at 45 C, and a certification-gap audit against UL/IEC/GB (2028).
ideas.founder_fit_note: Traction-inverter control, vehicle-control-unit firmware, and charge-interface electronics draw on founder circuits, embedded, and controls skills, but vehicle-level mechanical integration and multi-market certification programs are outside the profile.
ideas.fix_applied_notes: 2026-07-13 R2 FIX applied: defensible core restated as the UL/IEC/GB certification matrix + dual-personality interface (kit-existence claim downgraded to judgment pending P4 teardown vs Danfoss Editron/Dana TM4/BAE); 2028 CN OEM export-certification LOI made binding; unsubsidized repower economics model due 2028 (EPA grants mostly fund new equipment); B-14 interface personality bought as a module, not rebuilt.
ideas.adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 R2 adjudication (FIX, N5/E5/C6/V6/T6): US 'no standardized kit' claim likely overstated; CN certification-demand leg (Western-cert competence sold to XCMG-class exporters) is honest and distinctive.

ideas.concept: Precision wideband DC current/voltage sensing platform (optical and zero-flux hybrid) for HVDC converter stations, electrolyzer plants, and magnet systems
named_buyer_examples: VSC-HVDC integrators on US projects (Southern Spirit class)
electrolyzer/electrowinning plant EPCs
CN secondary equipment makers (license-out route)
ideas.painful_job: Multi-terminal DC protection needs millisecond fault discrimination but the measurement layer (wideband, high-accuracy DC current/voltage sensing) is supplied by a narrow incumbent set; protection algorithms are still re-derived paper-by-paper partly because sensing bandwidth/accuracy varies per project (L08-005/006/007); electrolysis plants lack the instrumentation to even see the ripple that degrades stacks (L11-016..021).
ideas.product: Sensor family: fiber-optic DC CTs and zero-flux hybrid transducers (kA-100 kA, DC-1 MHz), with a published accuracy/bandwidth test protocol; sold to converter and protection OEMs and plant EPCs; CN participation via licensed manufacture only (State Grid procurement is domestic).
ideas.frontier_vision: The measurement backbone for the DC century: every DC asset - HVDC bipole, electrolyzer hall, magnet string - instrumented with interoperable, protocol-published sensing that makes third-party protection and health analytics possible.
ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27 lab prototype of optical DC CT with zero-flux reference; 2028 accuracy round-robin published with a national lab; 2029 one protection-OEM or EPC evaluation agreement - BINDING GATE, go/no-go paired with evidence of E-14-class third-party protection winning sockets (the merchant sensing layer exists only if that layer wins); CN license-out term sheet scoped 2029 (royalty upside only; not a market leg).
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: US HVDC construction (Southern Spirit start 2029, in-service 2032, L08-041) and the PRC-029/IEEE-2800.2 compliance wave (L08-043/047) create measurement demand exactly in the 2030-2034 window; CN UHV tender cadence (L08-034) sustains a license-out channel.
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: US: Southern Spirit and successor interregional VSC-HVDC procurements 2029-2032 (L08-041) plus IBR compliance retrofits; CN (license-royalty upside only, not a counted market leg - State Grid procurement is domestic-only): State Grid annual UHV equipment tender batches (L08-034 pattern) via a licensed CN manufacturer.
ideas.competition_outlook_2030: Hitachi Energy/ABB optical CTs, LEM/Hitec zero-flux, NR/XJ in-house sensing in CN (L08-034). Incumbents are strong; the wedge is DC-wideband performance plus an open qualification protocol for third-party protection (complementary to E-14-class relays). P4 map of Hitachi Energy/ABB optical-CT incumbency is required; the wedge must be the demonstrated combined accuracy+bandwidth+open-protocol result of the published round-robin, not an assertion.
ideas.timing_window_risk: Component-margin business with entrenched incumbents; if HVDC protection stays vertically bundled inside converter OEMs, merchant sensing stays niche.
ideas.commercial_readiness_kill_date: 2034 (kill if no OEM/EPC evaluation converts to a project order by end-2033)
ideas.extreme_edge: Sensing-plus-protocol strategy: publish the DC measurement acceptance standard the industry lacks and own the reference implementations.
ideas.first_experiment: Optical + zero-flux hybrid 10 kA lab transducer; publish accuracy/bandwidth round-robin against commercial references, demonstrating DC-100 kHz fidelity adequate for traveling-wave protection (2027-28).
ideas.founder_fit_note: Strong transfer: fiber-optic current transducers, zero-flux hybrid sensing from DC to 1 MHz, and published accuracy test protocols are precision electromagnetics and instrumentation work at the core of the founder profile.
ideas.fix_applied_notes: 2026-07-13 R2 FIX applied: HONESTY FLAG CHANGE primary_market US+CN -> US, china_beachhead true -> false (seed's own text: State Grid domestic-only, CN revenue 'license royalties at best' - same condition that downgraded C-12/C-21; CN license chapter retained in text, not counted toward CN quota); 2029 OEM/EPC evaluation agreement made binding with go/no-go paired to E-14-class third-party protection traction; P4 map of Hitachi Energy/ABB optical-CT incumbency required - wedge is the demonstrated combined accuracy+bandwidth+open-protocol round-robin result, not an assertion.
ideas.adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 R2 adjudication (FIX with flag change, N5/E7/C7/V6/T6): elegant measurement-layer complement to E-14 with a cheap decisive experiment; structurally dependent on third-party protection winning sockets. Honesty ruling applied: primary_market US, china_beachhead false.

ideas.concept: High-cycle, high-current charging-interface thermal subsystems: liquid-cooled (non-PFAS) megawatt cable/connector assemblies for MCS charging (US) and high-cycle swap-coupler + buffer-battery thermal modules for battery-swap infrastructure (CN)
named_buyer_examples: MCS charger OEMs serving port/mine fleets (US)
SPIC Qiyuan/CATL-ecosystem swap-station integrators (CN, via component design-in)
ideas.painful_job: Megawatt-class connectors/cables are shipping ahead of a mature thermal-engineering base - 2023-25 literature is still resolving cable-core and connector cooling (L14-019..022), and the best-tested coolant family (Novec) is discontinued; CN swap stations cycle 800 kWh packs through sub-7-minute swaps continuously (L10-031), a high-cycle contact + thermal duty with a fresh national standard but no specialist supply base (L10-046).
ideas.product: Thermally engineered interface hardware: water-glycol/low-GWP-cooled MCS cable-connector sets rated for 3,000 A duty with contact-resistance health sensing; swap-coupler thermal/contact modules and buffer-charging cold plates for swap stations; sold to charger and swap-equipment OEMs.
ideas.frontier_vision: Own the amp-meters-per-minute bottleneck of heavy electrification: whatever wins - conductive MCS or swap - the current must cross a connector that this company thermally masters.
ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27 thermal test bench replicating IEC TS 63379 3,000 A duty with non-PFAS coolants; 2028 co-development agreement with one charger OEM (NREL emulator ecosystem, L10-049) - BINDING; CN leg BINDING GATE: P4 confirmation that CN swap-equipment component procurement is open to licensed outside suppliers before any CN investment (no eligible source documents CN conductive MW-charging demand); 2029 1e5-cycle contact endurance dataset; sequencing: port/mining applications (funded, L10-048) before trucking (MCS volume risk).
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: MCS standard published Feb 2026 (L10-050) means volume deployments land 2029-2033; CN swap standardization (L10-045/046) scales the swap architecture in the same window; both need interface thermal hardware that neither EVSE nor connector incumbents have proven at duty.
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: US: MCS charger rollouts for ports/trucking as EPA-funded equipment enters service life (L10-048) and NREL 20 MW emulator qualification work matures (L10-049); CN: swap-network expansion under national swap standards and SOE mining/marine programs (L10-031/040/046).
ideas.competition_outlook_2030: Staubli, Phoenix Contact, Huber+Suhner on cooled cables - P4 incumbent map REQUIRED, with the wedge proven by the 10,000-mate-cycle duty dataset, not asserted; CN connector majors and CATL in-house for swap. Wedge is duty-cycle-proven thermal data plus health sensing, not the connector geometry.
ideas.timing_window_risk: If MCS volumes slip and swap remains vertically integrated inside CATL/SPIC, merchant demand stays thin; B-14-class standards arbitrage may capture the interface layer first (coordinate, not compete).
ideas.commercial_readiness_kill_date: 2034 (kill if no OEM design-in by end-2032)
ideas.extreme_edge: Contact-health prognostics from thermal/electrical micro-signatures across the fleet - the swap network's insurance layer.
ideas.first_experiment: 3,000 A/1,250 V cooled cable-connector prototype on a thermal bench: demonstrate steady-state and 10-minute-cycle duty with water-glycol coolant, contact-resistance drift <10% over 10,000 mate cycles (2027-28).
ideas.founder_fit_note: Low direct fit: liquid-cooled megawatt cable/connector and swap-coupler hardware is thermo-mechanical contact engineering, with founder skills applying mainly to contact-resistance health sensing and telemetry.
ideas.fix_applied_notes: 2026-07-13 R2 FIX applied: CN-leg binding gate added (P4 confirmation that CN swap-equipment component procurement is open to licensed outsiders); 2028 charger-OEM co-development made binding; P4 connector-major incumbent map (Staubli/Phoenix Contact/Huber+Suhner) with the 10,000-mate-cycle duty dataset as wedge proof; sequencing fixed - port/mining (funded) before trucking (MCS volume risk).
ideas.adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 R2 adjudication (FIX, N6/E6/C6/V6/T6): genuine no-man's-land between EVSE OEMs and connector vendors; CN swap evidence country-specific (SPIC, GB/T 45926, CCS); no eligible source documents CN conductive MW-charging demand - CN leg gated.

ideas.concept: Merchant high-power RF vacuum windows and output couplers (brazed-ceramic assemblies with integrated diagnostics) for klystrons, gyrotrons, and solid-state RF chains
named_buyer_examples: US tube rebuilders and DOE/ARDAP-funded accelerator suppliers
gyrotron primes (Thales-class, EU/US programs)
IHEP/CEPC and CGN Dasheng ecosystems (CN, big-science procurement route)
ideas.painful_job: Output-coupler/window failure is named the most common failure mode in klystrons and vacuum electronic devices at high CW power, and fixes are still research-stage (L05-014); every tube maker and lab treats windows/couplers as an internal art, so operators carry unplanned downtime with no merchant qualified-spares supplier.
ideas.product: A qualified family of coaxial/waveguide RF windows and couplers (brazed ceramic, multipactor-suppressed coatings) with integrated arc/temperature diagnostics and a published acceptance protocol per IEC 60060-1:2025-referenced HV practice; sold as OEM components and lifetime-extension spares.
ideas.frontier_vision: The standard RF-vacuum interface company: as solid-state chains and next-generation tubes proliferate (fusion heating, colliders, industrial e-beam), every megawatt crosses a window this company makes or licensed.
ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27 ceramic-metal joining and multipactor test capability at a university/lab partner; 2028 supply first evaluation windows into one NAMED rebuild program (ARDAP channel, L05-031/032) - BINDING GATE; 2029 lifetime dataset (thermal cycling + RF conditioning) published; P4: evidence on whether tube primes buy reliability-critical components merchant - if negative, re-scope to lab spares/aftermarket and re-size honestly; P4 also evaluates one beam-components portfolio company pairing F-09 with C-09/D-07 adjacency; CN big-science channel scoped through international procurement counsel.
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: ITER gyrotron deliveries run to ~2029 (L05-029), fusion-heating and CEPC-class programs decide construction in the early 2030s (L05-012/013), and the EtO-replacement e-beam wave keeps industrial accelerators growing - all consuming windows/couplers from an artisanal supply base.
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: US: DOE ARDAP/stewardship-funded accelerator industrialization and fusion-heating RF procurement post-2030 (L05-031/032); CN: CEPC construction decision and CGN Dasheng accelerator capacity expansion (L05-012/013, L05-035/038) reached via big-science procurement routes.
ideas.competition_outlook_2030: CPI/Thales/Canon in-house (L05-050, L05-029), Kyocera-class ceramics houses (judgment); CN: CAEP/CAS internal supply. Wedge: merchant qualification + diagnostics that in-house shops do not productize.
ideas.timing_window_risk: Small TAM and conservative buyers; if tube demand shrinks faster than SSPA-chain window demand grows, the niche stays sub-scale.
ideas.commercial_readiness_kill_date: 2034 (kill if <2 paying lab/OEM customers by end-2032)
ideas.extreme_edge: Windows with built-in health telemetry - arc precursors and ceramic temperature - turning the most failure-prone RF part into the best-instrumented one.
ideas.first_experiment: Fabricate and RF-condition a 1 MW-class pillbox window prototype; demonstrate thermal-cycling life and multipactor margin versus a reference design, with integrated diagnostics (2028).
ideas.founder_fit_note: Low direct fit: brazed-ceramic vacuum windows and couplers are a materials/joining product, with founder skills applying peripherally to the integrated arc/temperature diagnostics and acceptance-test instrumentation.
ideas.fix_applied_notes: 2026-07-13 R2 FIX applied: 2028 evaluation windows inside one named rebuild program (ARDAP channel) made the binding gate; P4 evidence requirement on prime merchant-buy behavior (if negative, re-scope to lab spares/aftermarket and re-size honestly); two-paying-customers-by-2032 kill retained; P4 evaluation of a beam-components portfolio company with C-09/D-07 adjacency added.
ideas.adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 R2 adjudication (FIX, N5/E6/C6/V5/T6): documented failure mode (L05-014), real qualification wedge, honest small-TAM admission; whether tube primes buy reliability-critical 'internal art' merchant is the killer question.

ideas.concept: Laser-ultrasonic non-contact inline weld-inspection subsystems: production-line laser-generated phased-array ultrasound for battery interconnect welds (CN) and pipeline/pressure-vessel welds (US)
named_buyer_examples: NDT service majors and pipeline operators (US, ISO 13588/ASTM E2700-governed work)
Chinese battery-equipment integrators serving CATL/BYD lines
ideas.painful_job: PAUT dominates pipeline weld inspection under standards on ~7-year revision cycles (L16-026/027) but remains couplant-based and offline for high-rate production; EV battery makers weld millions of Al-Cu joints on equipment bought at documented scale (L16-028) with no non-contact volumetric weld QC at line rate - laser-generated phased-array ultrasound exists only as research (L16-029).
ideas.product: An inline inspection head: fiber-laser ultrasound generation + interferometric/EMAT detection + real-time phased-array reconstruction, packaged as a bolt-on QC subsystem for welding equipment OEMs (battery lines) and crawler/scanner integrators (pipelines), with standards-mapped reporting.
ideas.frontier_vision: Non-contact volumetric weld QC at production rate everywhere ultrasound gel cannot go - batteries, hydrogen pipelines, shipyards - built on one laser-acoustic engine.
ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27 lab demonstration on Al-Cu tab welds with destructive-test correlation; 2028 battery-equipment OEM co-development/design-in LOI - BINDING GATE (the CN demand bridge: inline volumetric QC demand is inferential, only the equipment channel is documented); US pipeline leg DE-WEIGHTED until a named operator/NDT-service evaluation exists by 2028 (standards-cycle timing is not a procurement trigger); 2029 standards engagement (ISO 13588 revision cycle) and speed/POD dataset; refresh L16-028 (2022-vintage buyer concentration) with a current filing before final scoring.
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: Battery line speeds and dissimilar-joint content keep rising while weld QC remains sampling-based; pipeline codes revise on ~7-year cycles putting a new ISO 13588 edition plausibly in 2026-2031 (L16-027) - a 2030 entrant with a proven laser-PAUT head rides both.
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: US: hydrogen/CO2 pipeline construction waves requiring higher-POD girth-weld inspection under updated ISO/ASTM editions (L16-026/027); CN: next battery-capacity buildout cycles and equipment-replacement waves at CATL/BYD-class lines (L16-028), accessed via equipment-OEM design-in.
ideas.competition_outlook_2030: Evident/Olympus, Zetec, Baker Hughes production NDT and Tecnar-class industrial laser-UT - P4 incumbent check REQUIRED; CN: SIUI-class NDT makers; in-line vision/resistance monitoring as the cheap substitute. Wedge: volumetric + non-contact at line rate.
ideas.timing_window_risk: Instrument-shaped wedge; must convert into an OEM-embedded subsystem or it stays a niche scanner business. POD-at-speed is the make-or-break metric.
ideas.commercial_readiness_kill_date: 2034 (BINDING: kill if POD/speed target unmet by end-2029; kill if no OEM design-in by end-2032)
ideas.extreme_edge: Weld-quality data network across fleets of lines - defect signatures pooled into a process-improvement dataset OEMs cannot build alone.
ideas.first_experiment: Laser-UT scan of production Al-Cu tab welds at >=1 weld/s equivalent; correlate with teardown/pull tests to establish POD versus offline PAUT baseline (2027).
ideas.founder_fit_note: Strong transfer: interferometric/EMAT detection, laser-ultrasound signal chains, and real-time phased-array reconstruction combine founder sensors/instrumentation, electromagnetics, signal-processing, and numerical-analysis skills.
ideas.fix_applied_notes: 2026-07-13 R2 FIX applied: CN demand bridge made binding (battery-equipment OEM co-development/design-in LOI by 2028); US pipeline leg de-weighted until a named operator/NDT-service evaluation exists by 2028; POD-at-speed end-2029 kill made binding; L16-028 (2022-vintage) refresh required before final scoring (rule 18); P4 incumbent check extended to Tecnar-class industrial laser-UT and Evident/Baker Hughes production NDT.
ideas.adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 R2 adjudication (FIX, N7/E7/C6/V6/T5): highest-novelty dual seed (first NDT play in the pool); physics risk on thin reflective Al-Cu at line rate honestly named; pipeline standards-cycle timing is too soft to carry demand.

ideas.concept: Magnetic-pulse welding (MPW) production machines for aluminum-copper battery interconnects and busbars - solid-state joining without heat-affected zones, positioned as the successor process to ultrasonic tab welding
named_buyer_examples: Chinese battery-equipment integrators serving CATL/BYD (the documented equipment buyers, L16-028)
pack/busbar line builders
ideas.painful_job: EV battery Al-Cu joining is a documented, buyer-concentrated Chinese equipment category (CATL+BYD >50% of one supplier's comparable purchases, L16-028); ultrasonic welding struggles with thick busbars and particle generation, while MPW research shows sound dissimilar Al-steel/Al-Cu joints inside defined weldability windows (L16-030/031) - but no productized, cycle-rated MPW machine serves battery lines.
ideas.product: A production MPW cell: long-life field-shaper coils (the consumable engineering moat), solid-state pulsed capacitor bank with per-shot energy control and coil-health monitoring, and weld-window process recipes for Al-Cu busbar/tab stacks; sold via co-development with CN battery-equipment integrators (design-in/licensing access path for a foreign founder).
ideas.frontier_vision: Solid-state joining as the default for dissimilar-metal electrification hardware - batteries, busbars, EV structures - with the coil-lifetime and weldability-window database as the durable asset.
ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27 university-lab MPW rig: establish Al-Cu weldability windows and coil-life engineering (the known killer); BINDING coil-life gate: >=10,000 shots at spec by end-2028 - the entire engineering thesis; 2028 co-development agreement with one CN equipment integrator with the IP split CONTRACTUALIZED (founder retains pulsed-power hardware + process recipes; integrator sells the machine); 2028 cost-per-joint and line-rate model versus ultrasonic AND laser welding (laser is the real substitute); 2029 pilot cell on a busbar application; refresh L16-028 (2022) with a current filing before final scoring.
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: EM forming/MPW has had a 15-year 'renaissance' without productization (L16-032) because coil life and process windows were unsolved; battery busbar thickness and throughput demands in the early 2030s create the first application where MPW's advantages are decisive rather than nice-to-have.
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: CN: battery equipment replacement/expansion cycles at CATL/BYD-class plants and next-generation pack architectures (cell-to-pack busbars) procured through the integrator ecosystem documented in L16-028; secondary trigger: 800 V/high-current platforms thickening interconnects beyond comfortable ultrasonic range.
ideas.competition_outlook_2030: Jiaocheng Ultrasonic defending the incumbent process (L16-028), laser-welding equipment lines, EU MPW specialists (Bmax/PSTproducts-class, judgment names); Chinese fast-follow on MPW hardware is certain once windows are published - IP and coil-life know-how must stay ahead.
ideas.timing_window_risk: Process-substitution plays are slow; if ultrasonic + laser cover next-gen interconnects adequately, MPW stays niche. Enthusiasm-zone discipline: demand bridge is the documented equipment-buyer base, not the physics.
ideas.commercial_readiness_kill_date: 2034 (BINDING: kill if coil life <10,000 shots at spec by end-2028; kill if no integrator agreement by end-2030)
ideas.extreme_edge: Per-shot electromagnetic process signature as built-in weld QC - every joint self-certifying.
ideas.first_experiment: Lab MPW rig: demonstrate Al-Cu busbar joints inside a mapped weldability window with >=10,000-shot coil life and pull/conductivity parity versus ultrasonic baseline (2027-28).
ideas.founder_fit_note: Pulsed capacitor-bank electronics, per-shot energy control, and field-shaper coil electromagnetics fit founder circuits and electromagnetics skills, but welding metallurgy, process-window development, and production-line integration are outside the profile.
ideas.fix_applied_notes: 2026-07-13 R2 FIX applied: coil-life gate (>=10,000 shots at spec by end-2028) made binding; 2028 CN integrator co-development agreement now requires a contractualized IP split (founder retains pulsed power + recipes; integrator sells); 2028 cost-per-joint and line-rate model vs ultrasonic AND laser welding added (laser is the real substitute); L16-028 (2022) refresh required before final scoring.
ideas.adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 R2 adjudication (FIX, N7/E7/C6/V6/T5): genuinely non-obvious productization of a 15-year non-productized process; correctly identifies coil economics as blocker-turned-wedge; technology-push honestly gated (no battery maker has asked for MPW).

ideas.concept: Classification-certified DC power and protection stacks for battery-electric and battery-swap inland/coastal vessels (CCS Rules-native string protection, insulation monitoring, containerized-pack power interface)
named_buyer_examples: Inland-vessel shipyards and system integrators building Jining '6006'-class swap ships (L10-040)
non-CATL marine battery integrators needing CCS-rule-compliant electrics
ideas.painful_job: China's CCS Rules for Battery-Powered Ships (2025 edition, effective 2025-12-31, L10-045) impose new requirements - containerized mobile power source inspection, shore-based monitoring, online data exchange - on a fast-growing electric canal/coastal fleet (national-benchmark swap cargo ship, L10-040); shipyards must integrate DC protection, insulation monitoring, and swap-pack interfaces to class rules with no specialist merchant supplier.
ideas.product: A type-approved marine DC electrical stack: string-level protection and isolation, IMD, containerized-pack power/communication interface per CCS rules, and the shore-monitoring data gateway; sold to shipyards/integrators. Foreign-founder access: JV or licensed manufacturing with a CN marine-electric supplier, with class-society type approval (CCS, plus DNV for export variants) as the wedge a startup can own.
ideas.frontier_vision: The class-rule-native electrical backbone for electric shipping: as battery vessels spread beyond China, the same certified stack ports to DNV/ABS markets.
ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27 map CCS 2025-edition electrical requirements into a reference architecture; 2028 partner selection (CN marine-electric JV/license) and begin CCS type-approval of the protection/IMD stack; 2029 first pilot vessel retrofit with an inland operator; DNV variant scoped for export hedge.
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: CCS rules effective end-2025 create a compliance-driven refit and newbuild wave through the early 2030s; swap-ship architecture (15-minute swaps, L10-040) is scaling on the Grand Canal corridor while the specialist supply base is nascent.
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: CN: CCS rule enforcement on newbuilds/refits from 2026 plus the next CCS edition (~2027-29 given the 2023-2025 cadence, L10-045) tightening monitoring requirements; canal/coastal electrification programs recognized as national transport benchmarks (L10-040) driving provincial procurement.
ideas.competition_outlook_2030: CATL-integrated electrics (dominant), CSSC-affiliated suppliers, European marine-electric houses (Danfoss Editron-class, judgment); domestic substitution is fast - the JV/licensing structure must concede manufacturing while retaining protection IP and type-approval ownership.
ideas.timing_window_risk: Access is the honest risk: if CATL bundles the full electrical stack with packs, the merchant socket closes; the wedge is non-CATL integrators and class-rule complexity.
ideas.commercial_readiness_kill_date: 2034 (kill if no CCS type approval + pilot vessel by end-2031)
ideas.extreme_edge: Swap-pack electrical passporting: per-container health/interface certification data that classification societies can audit.
ideas.first_experiment: Build the string-protection + IMD + pack-interface stack against CCS 2025-edition requirements and pass a class-society design appraisal on paper + bench demonstration (2028).
ideas.founder_fit_note: String protection, insulation monitoring, and pack-interface electronics fit founder circuits and embedded skills, but classification-society type approval, shipyard integration, and the required CN joint-venture structure are outside the profile.
ideas.adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 R2 adjudication (PROMOTE, N6/E6/C7/V6/T7): best CN-primary seed of the wave - dated country-specific regulatory trigger (CCS Rules 2025 edition effective 2025-12-31, ~2-year revision cadence), national-benchmark vessel class, type-approval-dossier moat, honest JV structure (concede manufacturing, retain protection IP + type approval), $300k class-design-appraisal experiment, named CATL-bundling kill. P4: confirm non-CATL integrator/shipyard share; map CSSC-affiliated suppliers; scope DNV/ABS export hedge.

ideas.concept: Multi-stack DC distribution and per-stack management skids for 100+ MW alkaline electrolyzer parks (per-stack isolation, current balancing, hot-swap, stray-current control) - the layer between the rectifier and dozens of stacks
named_buyer_examples: EPCs and owners of multi-set parks: Sinopec Kuqa-class projects (L11-049)
China Energy Construction 125-set-tender project sites (L11-051)
LONGi/Peric-supplied park integrators (L11-050)
ideas.painful_job: Chinese hydrogen parks deploy electrolyzers in dozens of sets per site (24-set and 125-set tenders, L11-050/051) but run below nameplate (Kuqa at ~20-51% utilization episodes, L11-049); with renewable-following operation, per-stack current balancing, isolation for maintenance, and stray/shunt-current control (L11-012/013) are plant-level electrical problems no stack OEM owns - the rectifier vendor stops at the DC bus.
ideas.product: DC distribution skids between rectifier and stacks: per-stack disconnection/isolation with hot-swap, active current-share balancing across parallel stacks, stray-current mitigation, and per-stack telemetry; sold to EPCs/owners as balance-of-plant, deliberately complementary to rectifier retrofits (C-07-class) and stack OEMs.
ideas.frontier_vision: The 'string inverter moment' for hydrogen: parks stop being one giant series circuit and become managed arrays, lifting fleet utilization and stack life across the industry.
ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27 model park-level electrical losses/imbalance with published Kuqa-type data; 2028 EPC retrofit-study partnership (access via engineering-services JV) - BINDING DEMAND GATE: measured park-level imbalance/stray-current losses must justify skid cost (>=3% utilization/specific-energy gain target) before any pilot build; 2029 pilot skid on a 4-stack sub-array at a partner site; P4: evidence that a licensed JV can win park-management retrofit scope at one named SOE park (no such tender exists yet - the audit-pressure trigger is inferential); boundary coordination with C-07 (rectifier) and F-23 (stack envelope) codified: one L11 power story, three sockets, at most two funded; explicitly monitor Sungrow/LONGi verticalization as the kill signal.
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: China's park fleet built 2023-2027 hits mid-life underperformance reviews by 2030-2032 exactly as green-hydrogen offtake mandates tighten; retrofit money flows to utilization fixes, not new stacks (respecting the L11 overcapacity negative finding - this sells no stacks).
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: CN: national green-hydrogen consumption mandates under the 15th FYP energy plans and SOE project performance audits forcing utilization retrofits at Kuqa-class parks (L11-049); successive CEC/Sinopec multi-set procurement rounds specifying park-level management (L11-050/051 pattern).
ideas.competition_outlook_2030: Sungrow Hydrogen and LONGi integrating park electrics vertically (the documented threat, L11-050/051); Siemens/ABB BOP packages for export projects. If Sungrow ships per-stack management by 2030, fold or license out.
ideas.timing_window_risk: Foreign access to SOE hydrogen parks is licensing/JV-only; the concept lives in the gap between rectifier vendors and stack OEMs, which either side can close.
ideas.commercial_readiness_kill_date: 2034 (kill if Sungrow/LONGi ship equivalent park-management products before a 2031 pilot, or no EPC partner by end-2030)
ideas.extreme_edge: Park-level 'stack dispatch' - degradation-weighted current allocation across dozens of stacks, an optimization layer only an independent vendor can run across mixed OEM fleets.
ideas.first_experiment: Hardware-in-the-loop park model + 4-stack sub-array pilot demonstrating >=3% utilization/specific-energy improvement from per-stack balancing and isolation-enabled availability (2028-29).
ideas.founder_fit_note: Per-stack isolation control, current-share balancing, stray-current mitigation, and telemetry draw on founder circuits, controls, and electromagnetics skills, with plant-scale DC switchgear hardware and EPC delivery outside the profile.
ideas.fix_applied_notes: 2026-07-13 R2 FIX applied: 2028 EPC retrofit-study partnership made the binding demand gate (measured park-level losses must justify skid cost; >=3% gain target); Sungrow/LONGi verticalization kill retained; P4 requirement added - evidence that a licensed JV can win park-management retrofit scope at one named SOE park; C-07/F-23 boundary coordination codified (one L11 power story, at most two funded).
ideas.adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 R2 adjudication (FIX, N6/E7/C6/V6/T5): most elegant CN-primary framing ('string-inverter moment for hydrogen'); respects the L11 stack-overcapacity negative finding; demand mechanism (SOE audits forcing utilization retrofits) is inferential - gated.

ideas.concept: Inline-metrology plasma surface-treatment machines for PCB and advanced-packaging substrates: atmospheric/vacuum plasma cleaning with closed-loop surface-energy and residue metrology
named_buyer_examples: Shennan Circuits-class PCB manufacturers (documented plasma-cleaner tender, L01-114)
CN OSAT/panel-level-packaging lines
ideas.painful_job: Chinese PCB/packaging manufacturers already procure plasma cleaning machines by tender (L01-114) and the category is a durable product line globally (Nordson MARCH, L01-115), but treatment is run open-loop: no inline verification of surface energy/residue, so yield excursions surface downstream at bond/underfill - an acute problem as CN advanced packaging densifies.
ideas.product: A plasma treatment cell with built-in metrology: plasma dose monitoring, inline surface-energy/contamination sensing, and closed-loop recipe control with lot-level traceability; positioned above commodity cleaners on qualified-process value, sold direct in CN with local service (wholly-foreign-owned equipment sales are routine in this category).
ideas.frontier_vision: Surface state as a controlled, logged process variable across electronics manufacturing - the treatment tool that certifies its own result.
ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27 integrate candidate inline sensors (contact-angle proxies, OES dose models) on a lab plasma cell - the 2027 experiment must validate inline surface-energy proxy robustness AT LINE RATE (the stated key uncertainty); 2028 beta at one CN PCB maker via tender channel (L01-114 shows the procurement route is open) - the beta must carry a PAID premium commitment: the >=30% premium thesis is tested before the 2030 gate, not at it (documented evidence is category procurement, not metrology-premium demand); 2029 yield-correlation dataset that justifies premium pricing.
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: CN advanced packaging and high-layer-count PCB investment keeps rising with the domestic-substitution cycle (equipment vendors growing 26-57%, L01-044); commodity cleaner price war (B-15 finding) makes differentiation-by-metrology the only defensible premium position, which incumbents have not productized.
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: CN: packaging-substrate capacity build under 15th FYP electronics localization and the AI-hardware substrate boom; recurring PCB-maker tenders (L01-114 pattern) with yield-traceability requirements tightening as lines serve automotive/high-reliability customers.
ideas.competition_outlook_2030: Nordson MARCH (L01-115), Panasonic/Samco (JP), and low-cost CN cleaner makers; the commodity floor is the main threat - if metrology does not command a premium by 2031, exit. P4 roadmap check REQUIRED (Nordson MARCH/Panasonic/Samco): closed-loop treat-to-spec is absorbable by the incumbent premium tier.
ideas.timing_window_risk: Commodity knife-fight risk is real (B-15 lesson); the seed survives only as a metrology-differentiated premium tool with consumable/service pull-through.
ideas.commercial_readiness_kill_date: 2034 (kill if beta yield-correlation fails to justify >=30% price premium by end-2030)
ideas.extreme_edge: Closed-loop 'treat-to-spec' operation: the tool guarantees surface state, not plasma minutes.
ideas.first_experiment: Lab plasma cell with inline surface-energy proxy sensing; demonstrate closed-loop treat-to-spec across contaminated PCB coupons with downstream bond-strength correlation (2027). Must validate proxy robustness at production line rate.
ideas.founder_fit_note: Inline surface-energy/residue metrology and closed-loop recipe control fit founder instrumentation and controls skills, while plasma-source hardware, process chemistry, and CN direct-sales operations are outside the profile.
ideas.fix_applied_notes: 2026-07-13 R2 FIX applied: 2028 beta now requires a paid premium commitment (>=30% premium thesis tested before the 2030 gate, not at it); 2027 experiment scoped to validate inline surface-energy proxy robustness at line rate; P4 Nordson MARCH/Panasonic/Samco roadmap check added (closed-loop treat-to-spec is absorbable by the incumbent premium tier).
ideas.adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 R2 adjudication (FIX, N5/E6/C7/V6/T6): converts the rejected B-15 knife-fight into a treat-to-spec premium position with a documented open procurement channel (L01-114) and durable category (L01-115); documented evidence is category demand, not metrology-premium demand - gated.

ideas.concept: Active safety-interlock modules for handheld laser welding/cleaning tools (sub-millisecond beam shutoff on loss of workpiece return-path/body-proximity detection), design-in for Chinese OEM export compliance
named_buyer_examples: Raycus-class handheld-welder OEMs (handheld exports +66.22% in 2025, L12-038)
Han's-class systems makers seeking differentiation amid the price war (L12-037)
ideas.painful_job: Handheld laser welding exports are exploding (+66% unit growth, L12-038) while the governing safety standard (ISO 11553-2) still carries a 2007 date (L12-050) - Class-4 kW-class beams in untrained hands with interlocks designed for a different product era; OEMs compete on price (Han's high-power revenue fell 6.6% on +30% units, L12-037) and need a certifiable safety differentiator to defend export access as US/EU scrutiny tightens.
ideas.product: A drop-in safety module: workpiece return-path sensing, capacitive body-proximity detection, and sub-millisecond beam-disable chain with certified diagnostics, licensed/design-in to CN OEMs; the compliance dossier (mapped to ISO 11553/IEC 60825 and anticipated revisions) is the product as much as the hardware.
ideas.frontier_vision: The safety-silicon of industrial lasers: every open-beam tool ships with an interlock chain this company defined, worldwide.
ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: HELD AS A PRICED OPTION: pre-company spend capped at the $150k prototype until a dated regulatory signal exists. 2026-27 prototype sensing + shutoff chain and human-factors testing; 2028 GATE (one of three, else kill at the existing end-2030 trigger): a documented ISO/IEC revision work-item, an EU/US market-surveillance action on handheld Class-4 welders, or an insurer/OEM requirement; one CN OEM design-in LOI remains the second gate; P4 discovery lead: European welding-association handheld-laser safety materials (judge's external check found safety materials but no dated enforcement action) - chase for an eligible dated anchor; 2029 certification body (TUV-class) assessment of module architecture.
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: Standards revision pressure (aging 2007 edition against an explosively growing product class, L12-050/038) plausibly lands new requirements in 2028-2032; OEMs will buy compliance rather than build it, exactly when a certified module exists.
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: CN: export-market access defense - anticipated EU/US enforcement or revised ISO/IEC requirements on handheld laser tools forcing OEM redesigns; the price war makes in-house safety R&D unattractive (L12-037/038).
ideas.competition_outlook_2030: OEM in-house safety circuits, generic safety-PLC vendors (Pilz/Sick-class, judgment); if standards never tighten, demand stays voluntary and thin - that is the honest bet being made.
ideas.timing_window_risk: Demand is contingent on regulatory tightening that is plausible but not yet dated; the pre-company phase costs little, making this an option purchase.
ideas.commercial_readiness_kill_date: 2034 (kill if no standards-revision signal or OEM design-in by end-2030)
ideas.extreme_edge: Interlock telemetry as fleet safety data - insurers and regulators as second customers.
ideas.first_experiment: Prototype return-path + proximity sensing with <1 ms beam disable on a 1.5 kW handheld welder; third-party-witnessed hazard-scenario test matrix (2027).
ideas.founder_fit_note: Strong transfer: return-path and capacitive body-proximity sensing with a sub-millisecond certified beam-disable chain is a compact sensing and embedded-safety-electronics product within founder instrumentation and circuits skills.
ideas.fix_applied_notes: 2026-07-13 R2 FIX applied: held as a priced option - pre-company spend capped at the $150k prototype until a dated signal exists; 2028 gate added (documented ISO/IEC revision work-item, EU/US market-surveillance action on handheld Class-4 welders, or insurer/OEM requirement - one of three, else kill at the existing end-2030 trigger); P4 discovery lead added (European welding-association handheld-laser safety materials - chase for an eligible dated anchor); OEM design-in LOI remains the second gate.
ideas.adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 R2 adjudication (FIX - option, N6/E6/C7/V5/T4): real gap (ISO 11553-2 dated 2007 vs documented export explosion) but the entire demand thesis is an undated regulatory contingency (judge's external check found association safety materials, no dated enforcement); adjudicated as an honest option purchase, D-16-style.

ideas.concept: In-service coolant-health monitoring and side-stream conditioning skids for liquid-cooled AI-datacenter fleets (chemistry, particulates, bio-fouling, wear-metal telemetry with automated correction)
named_buyer_examples: Hyperscaler/colocation operators running Deschutes-class CDU fleets (L14-043)
CN operators under GB 40879 PUE acceptance and T/CIEP liquid-cooling spec regimes (L14-035/039)
ideas.painful_job: Fleets of liquid-cooled racks are entering service (Vertiv liquid-cooling orders +50%, $15B backlog, L14-048) governed by specs that define installation but not multi-year coolant-health operations; fluid chemistry drift, particulates, and bio-growth are the failure modes air-cooled operators never managed, and OCP maintains fluid-property specs (L14-041) without an operations-layer product.
ideas.product: A side-stream skid per cooling loop: continuous sensing (conductivity, pH, inhibitor level, particle counts, wear metals, bio-activity) plus automated dosing/filtration, with fleet analytics and spec-compliance reporting mapped to OCP and T/CIEP requirements; consumables + telemetry business model.
ideas.frontier_vision: The water-treatment industry of the AI era, built fleet-native: every liquid-cooled campus runs on certified coolant health, with the cross-fleet corrosion/fouling dataset as the moat.
ideas.precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27 accelerated-aging loop studies establishing failure signatures for PG25-class fluids (GS Caltex/OCP context, L14-049/041) - the 12-month aging study must produce the failure-signature dataset that IS the moat (else the concept is a CDU-vendor feature); 2028 pilot skid in one colocation hall plus one operator LOI - BINDING; P4 REQUIREMENT: source operator incident/warranty data (direct pain evidence is currently inference from spec structure + fleet scale); 2029 fleet analytics MVP; C-05 boundary confirmed (lab qualification vs in-service operations + consumables): pair commercially, do not merge prematurely; CN route via licensed manufacture with a domestic thermal vendor (Envicool-class, L14-043).
ideas.launch_2030_timing_thesis: The 2024-2028 liquid-cooling installations reach the fluid mid-life problem years exactly at 2030+; operators will pay for uptime insurance once the first coolant-driven outages surface, and no survivor seed owns the in-service chemistry layer (C-05 owns lab/qualification benches).
ideas.demand_trigger_2030_2034: US: warranty/insurance requirements emerging from the first large coolant-failure incidents in 2028-2031 fleets (Vertiv-scale installed base, L14-048); CN: GB 40879 PUE-acceptance renewals and T/CIEP-spec operational audits (L14-035/039).
ideas.competition_outlook_2030: CDU vendors adding sensors (Vertiv/Motivair-class), fluid suppliers (GS Caltex, Chemours) bundling services, water-treatment majors (Nalco/Veolia) entering sideways - crowded by 2032; speed and dataset are the only defenses. Datacenter-capex-dependent (counts against the 4-seed cap).
ideas.timing_window_risk: Explicitly datacenter-correlated; a capex downturn delays fleet growth but the installed base still ages - demand is partly downturn-resistant (maintenance).
ideas.commercial_readiness_kill_date: 2034 (kill if CDU vendors bundle equivalent monitoring as standard before a 2030 design win)
ideas.extreme_edge: Coolant 'blood test' standard: publish the fluid-health acceptance methodology OCP/T-CIEP currently lack and own its instrument.
ideas.first_experiment: 12-month accelerated-aging loop with instrumented coupons across three commercial coolants; publish failure-signature library and side-stream correction efficacy (2027-28).
ideas.founder_fit_note: The multi-parameter sensing skid, telemetry, and automated dosing control fit founder instrumentation, embedded, and controls skills, but coolant chemistry, bio-fouling science, and consumables formulation are outside the profile.
